Overview\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md)\n- [LICENSE](LICENSE)\n- [README.md](README.md)\n- [docs/home.mdx](docs/home.mdx)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document provides a high-level introduction to the Agent Skills repository located at https://github.com/agentskills/agentskills. It covers the repository's purpose, major components, and how they relate to each other as an open standard for extending AI agent capabilities.\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThe Agent Skills repository serves as the central hub for a simple, open format designed to give AI agents new capabilities and expertise [README.md:1-5](). Skills are packaged as folders containing instructions, scripts, and resources that agents can discover and use to perform tasks more accurately and efficiently [docs/home.mdx:10-15]().\n\nThe project addresses the \"context gap\" where agents lack the specific procedural knowledge or organizational context needed for reliable work [docs/home.mdx:25-27](). By providing a standardized format, it enables:\n*   **Skill Authors**: To build capabilities once and deploy them across multiple agent products [docs/home.mdx:31]().\n*   **Agent Developers**: To allow end users to provide agents with new capabilities out of the box [docs/home.mdx:27]().\n*   **Teams and Enterprises**: To capture organizational knowledge in portable, version-controlled packages [docs/home.mdx:27-29]().\n\n**Sources**: [README.md:1-5](), [docs/home.mdx:10-31]()\n\n## Repository Architecture\n\nThe repository is organized into three primary functional areas: the specification/documentation, the reference implementation, and the ecosystem configuration.\n\n### Repository Structure Overview\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Root[\"Repository Root\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Format \u0026 Documentation\"\n        Docs[\"docs/\"]\n        Home[\"docs/home.mdx\"]\n        Clients[\"docs/snippets/clients.jsx\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Reference Implementation\"\n        SkillsRef[\"skills-ref/\"]\n        License[\"LICENSE\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Ecosystem \u0026 Assets\"\n        Logos[\"docs/images/logos/\"]\n        Carousel[\"docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx\"]\n    end\n    \n    Root --\u003e Docs\n    Root --\u003e SkillsRef\n    Root --\u003e License\n    Docs --\u003e Home\n    Docs --\u003e Clients\n    Docs --\u003e Logos\n    Docs --\u003e Carousel\n```\n\n**Sources**: [README.md:14](), [docs/home.mdx:1-23](), [CONTRIBUTING.md:32-40]()\n\n### Component Relationships\n\n| Component | Location | Primary Purpose | Key Outputs |\n|-----------|----------|-----------------|-------------|\n| **Format Specification** | `docs/` | Define the `SKILL.md` structure and metadata requirements | Standardized agent instructions [docs/home.mdx:68-73]() |\n| **Reference Library** | `skills-ref/` | Provide a Python SDK for validating skills and generating prompts | `skills-ref` package, CLI tools [README.md:14]() |\n| **Documentation Site** | `docs/` | Host the Mintlify-based portal at agentskills.io | Searchable web documentation [CONTRIBUTING.md:53-56]() |\n| **Ecosystem Assets** | `docs/snippets/` | Manage partner logos and reusable UI components | Logo carousels, client showcases [CONTRIBUTING.md:26-36]() |\n\n**Sources**: [README.md:14-22](), [docs/home.mdx:45-74](), [CONTRIBUTING.md:53-66]()\n\n## The Agent Skills Format\n\nAgent Skills are defined by a specific directory structure centered around a `SKILL.md` file. This file uses YAML frontmatter for metadata (at minimum `name` and `description`) and Markdown for the body of instructions [docs/home.mdx:10-15]().\n\n### Progressive Disclosure Model\n\nA core technical principle of the Agent Skills standard is **progressive disclosure**. This mechanism optimizes the agent's context window by loading information in three phases:\n\n1.  **Discovery**: At startup, agents load only the name and description of each available skill, just enough to know when it might be relevant [docs/home.mdx:37]().\n2.  **Activation**: When a task matches a skill's description, the agent reads the full `SKILL.md` instructions into context [docs/home.mdx:39]().\n3.  **Execution**: The agent follows the instructions, optionally executing bundled code or loading referenced files as needed [docs/home.mdx:41]().\n\n**Sources**: [docs/home.mdx:33-43]()\n\n## Reference Implementation: skills-ref\n\nThe `skills-ref` directory contains the reference implementation of the standard. It is a Python-based library designed to help developers validate their skills and integrate support into their own agents [README.md:14]().\n\n### Implementation Mapping\n\nThe following diagram bridges the conceptual \"Skill\" space to the actual code entities in the reference library.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Natural Language Space\"\n        UserIntent[\"User wants to 'Validate a Skill'\"]\n        AgentNeed[\"Agent needs 'System Prompt XML'\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space (skills-ref)\"\n        CLI[\"skills-ref CLI\"]\n        Validator[\"validator.py\"]\n        Parser[\"parser.py\"]\n        PromptGen[\"prompt.py\"]\n        Props[\"SkillProperties (models.py)\"]\n    end\n\n    UserIntent --\u003e CLI\n    CLI --\u003e Validator\n    CLI --\u003e Parser\n    Parser --\u003e Props\n    AgentNeed --\u003e PromptGen\n    PromptGen --\u003e Props\n```\n\n**Sources**: [README.md:14](), [docs/home.mdx:68-73]()\n\n## Documentation and Ecosystem\n\nThe project maintains a high-quality documentation site using Mintlify [CONTRIBUTING.md:53-56](). It includes custom React components like the `LogoCarousel` to showcase the growing ecosystem of compatible tools [docs/home.mdx:7-8, 49]().\n\n### Adopting Platforms\nAgent Skills are supported by a variety of AI development tools and platforms. Organizations can request to be listed by providing logo files (SVG/PNG) and adding a client entry to the documentation snippets [CONTRIBUTING.md:26-36]().\n\n### Licensing\n*   **Code**: The reference implementation and repository code are licensed under the **Apache License 2.0** [README.md:21-22](), [LICENSE:1-200]().\n*   **Documentation**: The documentation content is licensed under **CC-BY-4.0** [README.md:22]().\n\n**Sources**: [README.md:21-22](), [CONTRIBUTING.md:26-36](), [LICENSE:1-200]()\n\n## Use Cases\n\nAgent Skills enable several categories of capabilities:\n*   **Domain expertise**: Specialized knowledge like legal reviews or data pipelines [docs/home.mdx:29]().\n*   **Repeatable workflows**: Consistent, auditable multi-step tasks [docs/home.mdx:30]().\n*   **Cross-product reuse**: Building a skill once and using it across any compatible agent [docs/home.mdx:31]().\n\n**Sources**: [docs/home.mdx:25-32]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"18:T17db,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Agent Skills Format\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md)\n- [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx](docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx)\n- [docs/home.mdx](docs/home.mdx)\n- [docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx](docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx)\n- [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx](docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx)\n- [docs/specification.mdx](docs/specification.mdx)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document provides a high-level overview of the Agent Skills format specification. It covers the structure of a skill directory, the `SKILL.md` file format, and the progressive disclosure model that enables efficient context management.\n\nFor detailed information about specific aspects of the format:\n- For an introduction to the core value proposition, see [What Are Skills](#2.1)\n- For the complete technical specification of the `SKILL.md` file, see [SKILL.md Specification](#2.2)\n- For details on the progressive disclosure mechanism, see [Progressive Disclosure Model](#2.3)\n- For directory structure requirements and subdirectories, see [Skill Directory Structure](#2.4)\n\n## Core Format Structure\n\nAt its most basic level, a skill is a directory containing a `SKILL.md` file. This file acts as the entry point, containing both machine-readable metadata and human-readable instructions for the agent.\n\n```\nskill-name/\n├── SKILL.md          # Required: metadata + instructions\n├── scripts/          # Optional: executable code\n├── references/       # Optional: additional documentation\n└── assets/           # Optional: templates, resources\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:6-17](), [docs/home.mdx:10-23]()\n\n### Skill Entity Mapping\n\nThe following diagram maps the logical components of a skill to the specific file entities defined in the specification, bridging the gap between natural language requirements and the filesystem.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space (Filesystem)\"\n        DIR[\"[skill-name]/\"]\n        S_MD[\"SKILL.md\"]\n        S_SCR[\"scripts/\"]\n        S_REF[\"references/\"]\n        S_AST[\"assets/\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Natural Language Space (Agent Context)\"\n        DISC[\"Discovery Metadata (name, description)\"]\n        INST[\"Task Instructions (Markdown body)\"]\n        TOOL[\"Executable Logic (Python/Bash/JS)\"]\n        KNOW[\"Domain Knowledge (References/Assets)\"]\n    end\n\n    DIR --\u003e S_MD\n    S_MD -- \"Frontmatter\" --\u003e DISC\n    S_MD -- \"Markdown Body\" --\u003e INST\n    S_SCR -- \"scripts/*.py\" --\u003e TOOL\n    S_REF -- \"REFERENCE.md\" --\u003e KNOW\n    S_AST -- \"templates/\" --\u003e KNOW\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:8-16](), [docs/specification.mdx:187-213](), [docs/home.mdx:10-23]()\n\n## The SKILL.md File\n\nThe `SKILL.md` file is the heart of every skill. It uses YAML frontmatter for metadata and standard Markdown for the instruction body.\n\n### Metadata (Frontmatter)\nThe frontmatter must contain at least a `name` and a `description`. The `name` must match the parent directory name and follow strict alphanumeric constraints [docs/specification.mdx:58-66](). The `description` is critical as it is the primary mechanism agents use to decide whether to \"activate\" a skill for a given task [docs/specification.mdx:91-97](), [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx:7-15]().\n\nOptional fields include `license`, `compatibility` (for environment requirements like `Python 3.14+`), `metadata` (arbitrary KV pairs), and `allowed-tools` [docs/specification.mdx:110-174]().\n\n### Instructions (Body)\nThe Markdown body follows the frontmatter. It contains the procedural knowledge the agent needs. The specification recommends including step-by-step instructions, examples, and common edge cases [docs/specification.mdx:176-186]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:21-56](), [docs/specification.mdx:176-186](), [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx:7-15]()\n\n## Progressive Disclosure Model\n\nAgent Skills use a three-phase \"Progressive Disclosure\" model to manage the agent's context window efficiently. This prevents the agent from being overwhelmed by too many instructions at once [docs/home.mdx:33-43]().\n\n| Phase | What is Loaded | When | Token Cost |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| **1. Discovery** | `name` and `description` | Session startup | ~50-100 tokens per skill |\n| **2. Activation** | Full `SKILL.md` body | Task matches description | \u003c 5000 tokens (recommended) |\n| **3. Execution** | Files in `scripts/`, `references/`, etc. | Referenced in instructions | As needed |\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:214-223](), [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:18-31](), [docs/home.mdx:33-43]()\n\n### Disclosure Flow Logic\n\nThis diagram illustrates how an agent moves from \"knowing about\" a skill to \"executing\" it using the progressive disclosure phases.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant A as AI Agent\n    participant FS as Filesystem (SKILL.md)\n    participant R as Resources (scripts/assets)\n\n    Note over A, FS: Phase 1: Discovery (Startup)\n    A-\u003e\u003eFS: Read YAML Frontmatter (name, description)\n    A-\u003e\u003eA: Register skill in Catalog\n\n    Note over A, FS: Phase 2: Activation (Task Match)\n    A-\u003e\u003eA: Task matches description in Catalog\n    A-\u003e\u003eFS: Read full SKILL.md Markdown Body\n    A-\u003e\u003eA: Load instructions into context\n\n    Note over A, R: Phase 3: Execution (On-demand)\n    A-\u003e\u003eA: Follow instructions\n    opt If resource needed\n        A-\u003e\u003eR: Read scripts/extract.py or references/REFERENCE.md\n    end\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docs/home.mdx:33-43](), [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:18-31](), [docs/specification.mdx:214-235]()\n\n## Validation\n\nSkills can be validated using the `skills-ref` reference library. The `validate` command ensures that the `SKILL.md` file follows the schema and that directory names match the metadata.\n\n```bash\n# Example validation using the reference library\nskills-ref validate ./my-skill\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:237-244]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"19:T1887,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# What Are Skills\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md)\n- [LICENSE](LICENSE)\n- [README.md](README.md)\n- [docs/home.mdx](docs/home.mdx)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document explains what Agent Skills are, their purpose, and the core value propositions for different stakeholders. For the detailed technical specification of the `SKILL.md` format, see **2.2 SKILL.md Specification**.\n\n## Definition\n\nAgent Skills are a lightweight, open format for extending AI agent capabilities with specialized knowledge and workflows [docs/home.mdx:12-12](). At its core, a skill is a portable, version-controlled folder containing a `SKILL.md` file, which includes metadata and instructions that tell an agent how to perform a specific task [docs/home.mdx:14-14](), [docs/home.mdx:27-27]().\n\n```text\nmy-skill/\n├── SKILL.md          # Required: Metadata + Instructions\n├── scripts/          # Optional: Executable code (Python, JS, Bash)\n├── references/       # Optional: Documentation/Context\n├── assets/           # Optional: Templates, resources\n└── ...               # Any additional files or directories\n```\n[docs/home.mdx:16-23]()\n\nThe `SKILL.md` file serves as the primary entry point, containing:\n- **YAML frontmatter**: Minimum required fields include `name` and `description` [docs/home.mdx:14-14]().\n- **Markdown body**: Procedural instructions that guide the agent's behavior during execution [docs/home.mdx:14-14]().\n\nSources: [docs/home.mdx:12-23](), [README.md:3-5]()\n\n## Why Agent Skills?\n\n### The Context Problem\nAgents are increasingly capable but often lack the specific context needed to perform real-world work reliably [docs/home.mdx:27-27](). Without standardized skills, agents struggle with:\n- **Domain expertise**: Capturing specialized knowledge like legal review processes or data analysis pipelines [docs/home.mdx:29-29]().\n- **Repeatable workflows**: Turning multi-step tasks into consistent, auditable procedures [docs/home.mdx:30-30]().\n- **Portability**: Reusing the same capability across different AI products [docs/home.mdx:31-31]().\n\n### Core Value Propositions\n| Stakeholder | Value Proposition |\n| :--- | :--- |\n| **Skill Authors** | **Build once, deploy everywhere.** Create a skill once and use it across any skills-compatible agent [docs/home.mdx:31-31](). |\n| **Agent Developers** | **User-provided capabilities.** Allow agents to load context on demand, keeping the core system prompt small while expanding utility [docs/home.mdx:43-43](). |\n| **Enterprises** | **Knowledge Packaging.** Capture team-specific context and procedural knowledge into version-controlled folders [docs/home.mdx:27-27](). |\n\nSources: [docs/home.mdx:25-31](), [docs/home.mdx:43-43]()\n\n## Technical Implementation \u0026 Data Flow\n\nAgent Skills bridge the gap between \"Natural Language Space\" (user intent) and \"Code Entity Space\" (executable resources and file structures).\n\n### Discovery to Execution Flow\nThe following diagram illustrates how an agent system interacts with the skill directory structure to move from a user request to an executed action.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Natural Language Space\"\n        UserTask[\"User: 'Generate a legal summary'\"]\n        SystemPrompt[\"System Prompt: \u003cavailable_skills\u003e\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space\"\n        Scanner[\"Directory Scanner\"]\n        Parser[\"SKILL.md Parser\"]\n        Loader[\"Skill Activation (Full Markdown)\"]\n        FS[\"Filesystem: ./skills/legal-skill/\"]\n    end\n\n    UserTask --\u003e Scanner\n    FS --\u003e Scanner\n    Scanner --\u003e Parser\n    Parser --\u003e|\"Extract name/description\"| SystemPrompt\n    SystemPrompt --\u003e Match{Intent Match?}\n    Match -- \"Yes\" --\u003e Loader\n    Loader --\u003e|\"Read instructions\"| AgentAction[\"Agent runs scripts/ or reads assets/\"]\n```\n\n### Progressive Disclosure Model\nTo manage finite context windows, skills are processed in three distinct stages:\n1.  **Discovery**: At startup, agents load only the `name` and `description` of each available skill. This is just enough information to determine relevance without exhausting context [docs/home.mdx:37-37]().\n2.  **Activation**: When a task matches a skill's description, the agent reads the full `SKILL.md` instructions into the active context [docs/home.mdx:39-39]().\n3.  **Execution**: The agent follows the instructions, optionally executing bundled code in `scripts/` or loading referenced files in `assets/` as needed [docs/home.mdx:41-41]().\n\nSources: [docs/home.mdx:35-43]()\n\n## Reference Implementation: `skills-ref`\n\nThe repository includes a reference implementation (located in `skills-ref/`) to assist developers in adopting the standard [README.md:14-14](). This library provides the core logic for bridging the filesystem and the agent's prompt.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"skills-ref Code Entities\"\n        V[\"validate()\"]\n        RP[\"read_properties()\"]\n        TP[\"to_prompt()\"]\n    end\n\n    CLI[\"CLI: skills-ref validate\"] --\u003e V\n    Integration[\"Agent Integration\"] --\u003e RP\n    Integration --\u003e TP\n    RP --\u003e|\"Returns Metadata\"| Integration\n    TP --\u003e|\"Returns XML Block\"| Integration\n```\n\n### Key Functions\n- **`read_properties`**: Parses the YAML frontmatter of a `SKILL.md` file to extract metadata for the Discovery phase.\n- **`to_prompt`**: Formats the skill (metadata + instructions) into a structured format (often XML) suitable for injection into an agent's system prompt.\n- **`validate`**: Ensures a skill directory adheres to the specification requirements.\n\nSources: [README.md:14-14](), [CONTRIBUTING.md:38-40]()\n\n## Ecosystem and Governance\n\nThe Agent Skills format was originally developed by **Anthropic** and is now maintained as an open standard [docs/home.mdx:53-53](). \n\n- **Open Development**: The standard is open to contributions from the source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx](docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx)\n- [docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx](docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx)\n- [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx](docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx)\n- [docs/specification.mdx](docs/specification.mdx)\n- [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/models.py](skills-ref/src/skills_ref/models.py)\n- [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py](skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document provides the detailed technical specification for the `SKILL.md` file format, which is the core component of every Agent Skill. It defines the structure, required and optional fields, validation constraints, and content conventions.\n\nFor an overview of what skills are and why they exist, see [What Are Skills](). For information about the three-phase loading mechanism (metadata, instructions, resources), see [Progressive Disclosure Model](). For the complete skill directory structure including optional subdirectories, see [Skill Directory Structure]().\n\n## File Format Overview\n\nEvery skill must contain a `SKILL.md` file at its root [docs/specification.mdx:8-12](). This file consists of two parts: YAML frontmatter and Markdown body content [docs/specification.mdx:21-21]().\n\nTitle: SKILL.md Structural Components\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    SKILL_MD[\"SKILL.md File\"]\n    FRONTMATTER[\"YAML Frontmatter\u003cbr/\u003e(bounded by ---)\"]\n    BODY[\"Markdown Body\u003cbr/\u003e(Instructions)\"]\n    \n    SKILL_MD --\u003e FRONTMATTER\n    SKILL_MD --\u003e BODY\n    \n    FRONTMATTER --\u003e REQUIRED[\"Required Fields\"]\n    FRONTMATTER --\u003e OPTIONAL[\"Optional Fields\"]\n    \n    REQUIRED --\u003e NAME[\"name\u003cbr/\u003e(1-64 chars)\"]\n    REQUIRED --\u003e DESC[\"description\u003cbr/\u003e(1-1024 chars)\"]\n    \n    OPTIONAL --\u003e LICENSE[\"license\"]\n    OPTIONAL --\u003e COMPAT[\"compatibility\u003cbr/\u003e(max 500 chars)\"]\n    OPTIONAL --\u003e METADATA[\"metadata\u003cbr/\u003e(key-value map)\"]\n    OPTIONAL --\u003e TOOLS[\"allowed-tools\u003cbr/\u003e(space-delimited)\"]\n    \n    BODY --\u003e INSTRUCTIONS[\"Step-by-step instructions\"]\n    BODY --\u003e EXAMPLES[\"Examples and edge cases\"]\n    BODY --\u003e REFS[\"File references\u003cbr/\u003e(relative paths)\"]\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:8-32](), [docs/specification.mdx:176-186]()\n\n## YAML Frontmatter Structure\n\nThe frontmatter is delimited by triple dashes (`---`) at the beginning and end. It must appear at the start of the `SKILL.md` file [docs/specification.mdx:21-23]().\n\n**Basic structure:** [docs/specification.mdx:37-42]()\n\n```yaml\n---\nname: skill-name\ndescription: A description of what this skill does and when to use it.\n---\n```\n\n**Structure with all optional fields:** [docs/specification.mdx:46-55]()\n\n```yaml\n---\nname: pdf-processing\ndescription: Extract text and tables from PDF files, fill forms, merge documents.\nlicense: Apache-2.0\ncompatibility: Requires git, docker, jq, and access to the internet\nmetadata:\n  author: example-org\n  version: \"1.0\"\nallowed-tools: Bash(git:*) Bash(jq:*) Read\n---\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:21-55]()\n\n## Field Specifications\n\n### Required Fields\n\n| Field | Type | Constraints | Purpose |\n|-------|------|-------------|---------|\n| `name` | string | 1-64 characters, lowercase alphanumeric and hyphens only, must not start/end with hyphen, no consecutive hyphens | Short identifier for the skill |\n| `description` | string | 1-1024 characters, non-empty | Describes what the skill does and when to use it |\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:25-28](), [docs/specification.mdx:60-66](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:10-11]()\n\n### Optional Fields\n\n| Field | Type | Constraints | Purpose |\n|-------|------|-------------|---------|\n| `license` | string | None | License name or reference to bundled license file |\n| `compatibility` | string | 1-500 characters if provided | Environment requirements (product, packages, network) |\n| `metadata` | object | String keys to string values | Additional custom metadata |\n| `allowed-tools` | string | Space-delimited list | Pre-approved tools (experimental) |\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:29-32](), [docs/specification.mdx:112-174](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:12-12]()\n\n### `name` Field Constraints\n\nThe `name` field has strict validation rules enforced by the specification [docs/specification.mdx:60-66]().\n\n**Requirements:**\n- Must be 1-64 characters [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:39-43]()\n- May only contain unicode lowercase alphanumeric characters (`a-z`) and hyphens (`-`) [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:54-58]()\n- Must not start or end with a hyphen (`-`) [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:48-49]()\n- Must not contain consecutive hyphens (`--`) [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:51-52]()\n- Must match the parent directory name [docs/specification.mdx:65-65](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:60-65]()\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:58-89](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:25-67]()\n\n### `description` Field Guidelines\n\nThe `description` field serves as the primary mechanism for skill discovery. It is loaded at startup to help agents identify relevant tasks [docs/specification.mdx:93-97](), [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx:11-15]().\n\n**Requirements:**\n- Must be 1-1024 characters [docs/specification.mdx:94-94](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:78-82]()\n- Should describe both **what** the skill does and **when** to use it [docs/specification.mdx:95-95]()\n- Should include specific keywords that help agents identify relevant tasks [docs/specification.mdx:96-96]()\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:91-108](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:70-84]()\n\n### `license` Field\n\nThe optional `license` field specifies the license applied to the skill. The specification recommends keeping it short [docs/specification.mdx:112-114]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:110-121]()\n\n### `compatibility` Field\n\nThe optional `compatibility` field indicates specific environment requirements for the skill [docs/specification.mdx:125-129]().\n\n**Requirements:**\n- Must be 1-500 characters if provided [docs/specification.mdx:126-126](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:95-99]()\n- Can indicate intended product, required system packages, or network access needs [docs/specification.mdx:128-128]()\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:123-145](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:87-101]()\n\n### `metadata` Field\n\nThe optional `metadata` field is a map from string keys to string values for additional properties [docs/specification.mdx:149-152]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:147-161](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/models.py:26-26]()\n\n### `allowed-tools` Field\n\nThe optional `allowed-tools` field contains a space-delimited list of tools that are pre-approved to run. This is an experimental feature [docs/specification.mdx:165-168]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:163-174]()\n\n## Markdown Body Content\n\nThe Markdown body follows the YAML frontmatter and contains the actual skill instructions. There are no format restrictions [docs/specification.mdx:176-178]().\n\n**Recommended sections:** [docs/specification.mdx:180-183]()\n- Step-by-step instructions\n- Examples of inputs and outputs\n- Common edge cases\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:176-186]()\n\n## File Reference Convention\n\nWhen referencing other files within your skill, use relative paths from the skill root directory [docs/specification.mdx:224-226]().\n\n**Example:** [docs/specification.mdx:228-233]()\n```markdown\nSee [the reference guide](references/REFERENCE.md) for details.\n\nRun the extraction script:\nscripts/extract.py\n```\n\n**Best practices:** [docs/specification.mdx:234-235]()\n- Keep file references one level deep from `SKILL.md`\n- Avoid deeply nested reference chains\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:224-235]()\n\n## SKILL.md Processing Flow\n\nTitle: Data Flow from File to Agent Context\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"File System\"\n        SKILL_FILE[\"skill-name/SKILL.md\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Parsing (skills-ref)\"\n        FIND[\"find_skill_md()\"]\n        PARSE[\"parse_frontmatter()\"]\n        VALIDATE[\"validate_metadata()\"]\n        MODEL[\"SkillProperties class\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Output Formats\"\n        DICT[\"to_dict()\"]\n        PROMPT[\"to_prompt()\"]\n        ERRORS[\"list[str] errors\"]\n    end\n    \n    SKILL_FILE --\u003e FIND\n    FIND --\u003e PARSE\n    PARSE --\u003e VALIDATE\n    VALIDATE --\u003e MODEL\n    \n    MODEL --\u003e DICT\n    MODEL --\u003e PROMPT\n    VALIDATE --\u003e ERRORS\n```\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/parser.py:8-8](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:150-177](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/models.py:8-39]()\n\n## Frontmatter Validation Rules\n\nTitle: Frontmatter Validation Logic\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    START[\"SKILL.md frontmatter\"]\n    \n    CHECK_NAME{\"_validate_name()\"}\n    CHECK_DESC{\"_validate_description()\"}\n    CHECK_COMPAT{\"_validate_compatibility()\"}\n    CHECK_FIELDS{\"_validate_metadata_fields()\"}\n    \n    VALID[\"Valid Metadata\"]\n    INVALID[\"Validation errors list\"]\n    \n    START --\u003e CHECK_FIELDS\n    CHECK_FIELDS --\u003e CHECK_NAME\n    CHECK_NAME --\u003e CHECK_DESC\n    CHECK_DESC --\u003e CHECK_COMPAT\n    \n    CHECK_COMPAT --\u003e|Errors Empty| VALID\n    CHECK_COMPAT --\u003e|Errors Present| INVALID\n```\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:25-147]()\n\n## Validation with skills-ref\n\nThe `skills-ref` reference library provides validation functionality to check that a `SKILL.md` file follows the specification [docs/specification.mdx:237-239]().\n\n**CLI validation:** [docs/specification.mdx:241-243]()\n```bash\nskills-ref validate ./my-skill\n```\n\nThe `validate()` function in `validator.py` performs the following:\n1. Locates `SKILL.md` using `find_skill_md()` [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:167-167]()\n2. Parses the frontmatter using `parse_frontmatter()` [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:173-173]()\n3. Validates field presence and constraints via `validate_metadata()` [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:177-177]()\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:150-177](), [docs/specification.mdx:237-243]()\n\n## Content Size Recommendations\n\nThe specification follows a progressive disclosure model with recommended size limits for efficient context management [docs/specification.mdx:214-222]().\n\n| Component | Recommended Size | When Loaded |\n|-----------|------------------|-------------|\n| Metadata (`name` + `description`) | ~100 tokens | At agent startup (Discovery) [docs/specification.mdx:218-218]() |\n| Instructions (full `SKILL.md` body) | \u003c 5000 tokens | When skill is activated (Activation) [docs/specification.mdx:219-219]() |\n| Resources (`scripts/`, `references/`, `assets/`) | As needed | On-demand during execution [docs/specification.mdx:220-220]() |\n\n**Best practice:** Keep the main `SKILL.md` under 500 lines [docs/specification.mdx:222-222]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:214-222](), [docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx:86-91]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"1b:T2558,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Progressive Disclosure Model\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx](docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx)\n- [docs/clients.mdx](docs/clients.mdx)\n- [docs/docs.json](docs/docs.json)\n- [docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx](docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx)\n- [docs/skill-creation/evaluating-skills.mdx](docs/skill-creation/evaluating-skills.mdx)\n- [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx](docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx)\n- [docs/skill-creation/quickstart.mdx](docs/skill-creation/quickstart.mdx)\n- [docs/skill-creation/using-scripts.mdx](docs/skill-creation/using-scripts.mdx)\n- [docs/snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx](docs/snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx)\n- [docs/specification.mdx](docs/specification.mdx)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document explains the progressive disclosure mechanism used by Agent Skills to manage context efficiently. Progressive disclosure is a three-phase loading strategy that minimizes the amount of information in an agent's context window at any given time, loading skill details only when they become relevant.\n\n## Overview\n\nProgressive disclosure is the core architectural pattern that makes Agent Skills practical for large-scale agent deployments. Rather than loading all skill instructions at startup, the system reveals skill information in three distinct phases, each providing increasingly detailed information as needed.\n\nThe model addresses a fundamental constraint of AI agents: limited context window size. By loading only metadata initially and deferring detailed instructions and resources until activation, agents can be aware of hundreds of skills while maintaining efficient context usage [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:18-31]().\n\nSources: [docs/specification.mdx:214-222](), [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:18-31]()\n\n## The Three Phases\n\n### Phase 1: Discovery\n\nDuring the discovery phase, agents load only the minimal metadata required to identify when a skill might be relevant. This occurs at agent startup or when skills directories are rescanned [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:32-34]().\n\n**Discovery Phase Workflow**\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    STARTUP[\"Agent Startup\"]\n    SCAN[\"Scan Skills Directories\"]\n    PARSE[\"Parse SKILL.md Frontmatter\"]\n    EXTRACT[\"Extract name + description\"]\n    INJECT[\"Inject into System Prompt\"]\n    \n    STARTUP --\u003e SCAN\n    SCAN --\u003e PARSE\n    PARSE --\u003e EXTRACT\n    EXTRACT --\u003e INJECT\n```\n\nThe agent reads only the YAML frontmatter from each `SKILL.md` file, extracting:\n- `name`: The skill identifier [docs/specification.mdx:60-65]()\n- `description`: When and why to use the skill [docs/specification.mdx:93-97]()\n\n**Context usage during discovery:** ~100 tokens per skill [docs/specification.mdx:218]().\n\nThis allows an agent to maintain awareness of many skills while consuming minimal context. The `description` field should be written specifically to help agents determine relevance without reading the full instructions [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx:7-17]().\n\nSources: [docs/specification.mdx:218](), [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:32-34](), [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx:11-18]()\n\n### Phase 2: Activation\n\nWhen a user task matches a skill's description, the agent transitions to the activation phase. At this point, the agent loads the complete `SKILL.md` file, including the full Markdown body containing detailed instructions [docs/skill-creation/quickstart.mdx:67-68]().\n\n**Activation Phase Sequence**\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant Agent\n    participant SkillMetadata as \"Skill Metadata\u003cbr/\u003e(in context)\"\n    participant SKILL_MD as \"SKILL.md File\"\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eAgent: \"Roll a d20\"\n    Agent-\u003e\u003eSkillMetadata: Match description\n    SkillMetadata--\u003e\u003eAgent: \"roll-dice matches\"\n    Agent-\u003e\u003eSKILL_MD: Read full instructions\n    SKILL_MD--\u003e\u003eAgent: Markdown Body content\n    Agent-\u003e\u003eAgent: Follow instructions\n```\n\nDuring activation, the agent:\n1. Identifies which skill(s) match the current task based on metadata [docs/skill-creation/quickstart.mdx:67-68]().\n2. Reads the entire `SKILL.md` file into context [docs/specification.mdx:185-186]().\n3. Receives detailed step-by-step instructions [docs/specification.mdx:180-184]().\n\n**Context usage during activation:** \u003c 5000 tokens recommended [docs/specification.mdx:219]().\n\nThe specification recommends keeping `SKILL.md` files under 500 lines to ensure efficient activation [docs/specification.mdx:222]().\n\nSources: [docs/specification.mdx:185-186](), [docs/specification.mdx:219-222](), [docs/skill-creation/quickstart.mdx:67-68]()\n\n### Phase 3: Execution\n\nThe execution phase involves accessing optional resources bundled with the skill. These resources are loaded on-demand, only when the skill instructions explicitly reference them [docs/specification.mdx:216-220]().\n\n**Execution Phase Resource Loading**\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    ACTIVATE[\"Skill Activated\u003cbr/\u003eSKILL.md loaded\"]\n    EXECUTE[\"Follow Instructions\"]\n    NEED_SCRIPT{\"Instructions\u003cbr/\u003ereference script?\"}\n    NEED_REF{\"Instructions\u003cbr/\u003ereference docs?\"}\n    NEED_ASSET{\"Instructions\u003cbr/\u003ereference assets?\"}\n    LOAD_SCRIPT[\"Load scripts/*\"]\n    LOAD_REF[\"Load references/*\"]\n    LOAD_ASSET[\"Load assets/*\"]\n    COMPLETE[\"Task Complete\"]\n    \n    ACTIVATE --\u003e EXECUTE\n    EXECUTE --\u003e NEED_SCRIPT\n    NEED_SCRIPT --\u003e|Yes| LOAD_SCRIPT\n    NEED_SCRIPT --\u003e|No| NEED_REF\n    LOAD_SCRIPT --\u003e NEED_REF\n    NEED_REF --\u003e|Yes| LOAD_REF\n    NEED_REF --\u003e|No| NEED_ASSET\n    LOAD_REF --\u003e NEED_ASSET\n    NEED_ASSET --\u003e|Yes| LOAD_ASSET\n    NEED_ASSET --\u003e|No| COMPLETE\n    LOAD_ASSET --\u003e COMPLETE\n```\n\nDuring execution, the agent may access:\n- **`scripts/`**: Executable code (Python, Bash, JS) to run specific operations [docs/specification.mdx:189-196]().\n- **`references/`**: Additional documentation like `REFERENCE.md` or `FORMS.md` [docs/specification.mdx:198-206]().\n- **`assets/`**: Templates, images, or data files [docs/specification.mdx:207-213]().\n\n**Context usage during execution:** Variable, loaded as needed [docs/specification.mdx:220]().\n\nSources: [docs/specification.mdx:187-213](), [docs/specification.mdx:220]()\n\n## Directory Structure and Loading Map\n\nThe progressive disclosure model maps directly to the skill directory structure [docs/specification.mdx:6-17]().\n\n| Directory/File | Phase | Loaded | Context Impact |\n|----------------|-------|--------|----------------|\n| `SKILL.md` frontmatter | Discovery | Always (startup) | ~100 tokens |\n| `SKILL.md` body | Activation | On skill match | \u003c 5000 tokens |\n| `scripts/*` | Execution | On demand | Variable |\n| `references/*` | Execution | On demand | Variable |\n| `assets/*` | Execution | On demand | Variable |\n\n**Directory Structure to Loading Phase Mapping**\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    ROOT[\"skill-name/\"]\n    SKILL_MD[\"SKILL.md\"]\n    FRONTMATTER[\"--- YAML Frontmatter ---\u003cbr/\u003ename, description\"]\n    BODY[\"# Markdown Body\u003cbr/\u003eDetailed instructions\"]\n    SCRIPTS[\"scripts/\u003cbr/\u003eExecutable code\"]\n    REFS[\"references/\u003cbr/\u003eAdditional docs\"]\n    ASSETS[\"assets/\u003cbr/\u003eTemplates, data\"]\n    \n    PHASE1[\"Phase 1: Discovery\u003cbr/\u003e~100 tokens\"]\n    PHASE2[\"Phase 2: Activation\u003cbr/\u003e\u003c5000 tokens\"]\n    PHASE3[\"Phase 3: Execution\u003cbr/\u003eVariable\"]\n    \n    ROOT --\u003e SKILL_MD\n    ROOT --\u003e SCRIPTS\n    ROOT --\u003e REFS\n    ROOT --\u003e ASSETS\n    \n    SKILL_MD --\u003e FRONTMATTER\n    SKILL_MD --\u003e BODY\n    \n    FRONTMATTER -.-\u003e|Loaded| PHASE1\n    BODY -.-\u003e|Loaded| PHASE2\n    SCRIPTS -.-\u003e|Loaded| PHASE3\n    REFS -.-\u003e|Loaded| PHASE3\n    ASSETS -.-\u003e|Loaded| PHASE3\n```\n\nSources: [docs/specification.mdx:6-17](), [docs/specification.mdx:214-222]()\n\n## Context Optimization Best Practices\n\n### Metadata Optimization\nThe `description` field carries the entire burden of triggering [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx:15](). It should focus on user intent and use imperative phrasing [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx:21-26]().\n\n### Instruction Size Management\nKeep the main `SKILL.md` body under 500 lines [docs/specification.mdx:222](). When instructions grow larger, move detailed reference material to separate files in `references/` [docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx:88-91]().\n\n### Reference File Granularity\nKeep individual reference files focused. Since agents load these on-demand, smaller files mean less use of context [docs/specification.mdx:205]().\n\nSources: [docs/specification.mdx:205-222](), [docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx:86-91](), [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx:11-26]()\n\n## Progressive Disclosure State Diagram\n\n```mermaid\nstateDiagram-v2\n    [*] --\u003e Discovery\n    Discovery --\u003e Discovery: Scan additional skills\n    Discovery --\u003e Activation: Task matches description\n    Activation --\u003e Execution: Load resources\n    Activation --\u003e Discovery: Task complete\n    Execution --\u003e Execution: Load additional resources\n    Execution --\u003e Discovery: Task complete\n    \n    note right of Discovery\n        All skills: name + description\n        ~100 tokens per skill\n    end note\n    \n    note right of Activation\n        Matched skill: full SKILL.md\n        \u003c5000 tokens per skill\n    end note\n    \n    note right of Execution\n        Referenced files only\n        Variable tokens per file\n    end note\n```\n\nSources: [docs/specification.mdx:214-222](), [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:20-31]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"1c:T21dd,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Skill Directory
