[docs/specification.mdx](docs/specification.mdx)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document describes the expected directory structure for an Agent Skill, including the required `SKILL.md` file and the optional `scripts/`, `references/`, and `assets/` subdirectories. It explains how these directories facilitate the progressive disclosure of information to an AI agent.\n\n## Directory Structure Overview\n\nAn Agent Skill is defined as a directory. At its most basic level, it must contain a `SKILL.md` file [docs/specification.mdx:8-9](). To support more complex workflows, it can include optional subdirectories for executable code, extended documentation, and static resources.\n\n### Standard Layout\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Skill Directory Space\"\n    Root[\"skill-name/ (Directory)\"]\n    SkillMD[\"SKILL.md (File)\"]\n    Scripts[\"scripts/ (Directory)\"]\n    Refs[\"references/ (Directory)\"]\n    Assets[\"assets/ (Directory)\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space\"\n    Parser[\"Parser (skills-ref)\"]\n    Discovery[\"Discovery Mechanism\"]\n    Activation[\"Activation Logic\"]\n    end\n\n    Root --\u003e SkillMD\n    Root -.-\u003e Scripts\n    Root -.-\u003e Refs\n    Root -.-\u003e Assets\n\n    Discovery --\u003e|\"Scans for SKILL.md\"| Root\n    Parser --\u003e|\"Reads Frontmatter\"| SkillMD\n    Activation --\u003e|\"Loads Body\"| SkillMD\n```\n\n**Directory Component Summary**\n\n| Component | Requirement | Purpose |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `SKILL.md` | **Required** | Contains YAML metadata and core Markdown instructions. [docs/specification.mdx:12-12]() |\n| `scripts/` | Optional | Contains executable code (Python, Bash, JS) that the agent can run. [docs/specification.mdx:13-13]() |\n| `references/` | Optional | Contains supplemental documentation to keep the main context small. [docs/specification.mdx:14-14]() |\n| `assets/` | Optional | Stores static resources like templates, images, or data schemas. [docs/specification.mdx:15-15]() |\n\nSources: [docs/specification.mdx:6-17](), [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:62-73]()\n\n## The Required SKILL.md File\n\nThe `SKILL.md` file is the entry point for every skill. It must contain YAML frontmatter followed by Markdown content [docs/specification.mdx:21-21]().\n\n### Validation Rules\n- **Naming**: The `name` field in the YAML frontmatter must match the parent directory name exactly [docs/specification.mdx:65-65]().\n- **Activation**: The file is used by the agent to decide whether to \"activate\" the skill based on the `description` field [docs/skill-creation/quickstart.mdx:43-45]().\n- **Format**: The frontmatter must be enclosed between `---` delimiters [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:109-109]().\n\nSources: [docs/specification.mdx:19-21](), [docs/skill-creation/quickstart.mdx:41-46](), [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:107-113]()\n\n## Optional Subdirectories\n\n### 1. scripts/\nThe `scripts/` directory houses executable logic. This is used when a task is too complex for the agent to generate code for on-the-fly, or when specific environment/dependency management is required [docs/skill-creation/using-scripts.mdx:87-88]().\n\n- **Self-Containment**: Scripts should be self-contained or clearly document dependencies [docs/specification.mdx:191-192](). Python scripts often use [PEP 723](https://peps.python.org/pep-0723/) for inline dependency declarations [docs/skill-creation/using-scripts.mdx:129-137]().\n- **Invocation**: Agents are instructed via `SKILL.md` to run these scripts using relative paths (e.g., `bash scripts/validate.sh`) [docs/skill-creation/using-scripts.mdx:108-110]().\n\n### 2. references/\nThis directory is for \"Context Parsimony.\" Instead of bloating the `SKILL.md` with thousands of lines of documentation, authors move detailed technical specs here [docs/specification.mdx:205-206]().\n\n- **On-Demand Loading**: The agent only reads these files if the instructions in `SKILL.md` explicitly tell it to [docs/specification.mdx:205-205](). For example: \"Read `references/api-errors.md` if the API returns a non-200 status code\" [docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx:90-91]().\n- **Recommended Files**: `REFERENCE.md` for technical details and `FORMS.md` for structured templates or data formats [docs/specification.mdx:201-202]().\n\n### 3. assets/\nThe `assets/` directory contains static resources that do not fall into the category of executable scripts or documentation [docs/specification.mdx:207-208]().\n- **Templates**: Document or configuration templates [docs/specification.mdx:210-210]().\n- **Images**: Diagrams or examples [docs/specification.mdx:211-211]().\n- **Data Files**: Lookup tables, schemas, or data files [docs/specification.mdx:212-212]().\n\nSources: [docs/specification.mdx:189-213](), [docs/skill-creation/using-scripts.mdx:7-8](), [docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx:86-91]()\n\n## Data Flow and Progressive Disclosure\n\nThe directory structure is designed to support the **Progressive Disclosure Model**, ensuring the agent's context window is used efficiently by loading details only as needed [docs/specification.mdx:214-216]().\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Agent as AI Agent\n    participant Disk as Filesystem\n    participant Context as Context Window\n\n    Note over Agent, Context: Phase 1: Discovery (Catalog)\n    Agent-\u003e\u003eDisk: Scan directories for SKILL.md\n    Disk--\u003e\u003eAgent: Metadata (name, description)\n    Agent-\u003e\u003eContext: Load Metadata (~50-100 tokens/skill)\n\n    Note over Agent, Context: Phase 2: Activation (Instructions)\n    Agent-\u003e\u003eAgent: Match User Prompt to Description\n    Agent-\u003e\u003eDisk: Read SKILL.md Body\n    Agent-\u003e\u003eContext: Load Instructions (\u003c5000 tokens)\n\n    Note over Agent, Context: Phase 3: Execution (Resources)\n    Agent-\u003e\u003eDisk: Read scripts/ or references/\n    Disk--\u003e\u003eAgent: File Content\n    Agent-\u003e\u003eContext: Load Resource (On-demand)\n```\n\n| Tier | Content | Location | Trigger |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| **1. Catalog** | `name`, `description` | `SKILL.md` Frontmatter | Session Start [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:24-24]() |\n| **2. Instructions** | Markdown Body | `SKILL.md` Body | Skill Activation [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:25-25]() |\n| **3. Resources** | Scripts, Assets, Docs | `scripts/`, `references/`, `assets/` | Explicit Reference [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:26-26]() |\n\nSources: [docs/specification.mdx:214-222](), [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:18-31]()\n\n## File Referencing Conventions\n\nWhen referencing other files in your skill, use **relative paths** from the skill root [docs/specification.mdx:226-227]().\n\n- **Depth Limit**: It is recommended to keep file references one level deep from `SKILL.md` [docs/specification.mdx:235-235]().\n- **Avoid Nesting**: Authors should avoid deeply nested reference chains to minimize complexity for the agent [docs/specification.mdx:235-236]().\n\nSources: [docs/specification.mdx:224-236](), [docs/skill-creation/using-scripts.mdx:91-92]()\n\n## Validation of Structure\n\nThe `skills-ref` reference library provides a validation utility to ensure a directory adheres to the Agent Skills specification [docs/specification.mdx:239-240]().\n\n```bash\n# Example validation command\nskills-ref validate ./my-skill\n```\n\nThe validation process (typically performed by a `validate()` function in the reference implementation) ensures:\n1. The directory contains a `SKILL.md` file [docs/specification.mdx:8-9]().\n2. The `SKILL.md` file contains valid YAML frontmatter [docs/specification.mdx:21-21]().\n3. The `name` and `description` fields are present and meet length/character constraints [docs/specification.mdx:27-28]().\n4. The `name` field matches the parent directory name [docs/specification.mdx:65-65]().\n\nSources: [docs/specification.mdx:237-245](), [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:107-115]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"1d:T415d,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Integration Guide\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx](docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx)\n- [docs/images/logos/fast-agent/fast-agent-dark.svg](docs/images/logos/fast-agent/fast-agent-dark.svg)\n- [docs/images/logos/fast-agent/fast-agent-light.svg](docs/images/logos/fast-agent/fast-agent-light.svg)\n- [docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx](docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx)\n- [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx](docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx)\n- [docs/snippets/clients.jsx](docs/snippets/clients.jsx)\n- [docs/specification.mdx](docs/specification.mdx)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document provides an overview of integrating Agent Skills support into AI agent systems. It explains the core concepts, integration approaches, and lifecycle phases required to make an agent compatible with the Agent Skills format.\n\nFor detailed specifications of the `SKILL.md` format itself, see [SKILL.md Specification](#2.2). For information about the progressive disclosure mechanism, see [Progressive Disclosure Model](#2.3). For practical validation and tooling, see [skills-ref Reference Library](#5).\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nIntegration enables AI agents to discover, load, and execute skills from the Agent Skills format. This document covers:\n\n- The two primary integration approaches (filesystem-based and tool-based) [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:9-14]()\n- The three-phase integration lifecycle (discovery, activation, execution) [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:20-31]()\n- How skill metadata flows into agent system prompts [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:116-120]()\n- Reference implementation utilities available in `skills-ref` [docs/specification.mdx:239-245]()\n\nDetailed implementation requirements for each approach are covered in child pages: [Integration Requirements](#3.1), [Filesystem-Based Integration](#3.2), [Tool-Based Integration](#3.3), and [System Prompt Injection](#3.4).\n\n---\n\n## Integration Architecture Overview\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Agent_System\"\n        AGENT[\"Agent_Runtime\"]\n        SYS_PROMPT[\"System_Prompt\u003cbr/\u003ewith_available_skills\"]\n        CONTEXT[\"Context_Window\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Skill_Discovery_Phase\"\n        SCAN[\"Directory_Scanner\"]\n        YAML_PARSE[\"YAML_Frontmatter_Parser\"]\n        META_CACHE[\"Metadata_Cache\u003cbr/\u003e{name,_description,_path}\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Skill_Files_on_Disk\"\n        SKILL_DIR[\"skill-name/\"]\n        SKILL_MD[\"SKILL.md\u003cbr/\u003efrontmatter_+_body\"]\n        SCRIPTS[\"scripts/\"]\n        REFS[\"references/\"]\n        ASSETS[\"assets/\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Integration_Approach\"\n        FS_ACCESS[\"Filesystem_Access\u003cbr/\u003e(cat,_ls_commands)\"]\n        TOOL_IMPL[\"Tool_Implementation\u003cbr/\u003e(custom_tools)\"]\n    end\n    \n    SKILL_DIR --\u003e SKILL_MD\n    SKILL_DIR --\u003e SCRIPTS\n    SKILL_DIR --\u003e REFS\n    SKILL_DIR --\u003e ASSETS\n    \n    SCAN --\u003e SKILL_MD\n    YAML_PARSE --\u003e META_CACHE\n    SKILL_MD --\u003e YAML_PARSE\n    \n    META_CACHE --\u003e SYS_PROMPT\n    SYS_PROMPT --\u003e AGENT\n    AGENT --\u003e CONTEXT\n    \n    AGENT --\u003e FS_ACCESS\n    AGENT --\u003e TOOL_IMPL\n    \n    FS_ACCESS --\u003e SKILL_MD\n    TOOL_IMPL --\u003e SKILL_MD\n    \n    FS_ACCESS --\u003e SCRIPTS\n    FS_ACCESS --\u003e REFS\n    FS_ACCESS --\u003e ASSETS\n    \n    TOOL_IMPL --\u003e SCRIPTS\n    TOOL_IMPL --\u003e REFS\n    TOOL_IMPL --\u003e ASSETS\n```\n\n**Integration Architecture: Skills-Compatible Agent System**\n\nThe architecture separates skill storage (filesystem), metadata extraction (discovery phase), and skill access (integration approach). The agent system maintains a lightweight metadata cache and injects it into the system prompt, enabling the model to identify relevant skills without loading full instructions.\n\nSources: [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:7-31](), [docs/specification.mdx:6-17]()\n\n---\n\n## Integration Approaches\n\nAgent Skills supports two primary integration approaches, determined by the agent's execution environment and capabilities.\n\n| Approach | Environment | Access Method | Complexity | Capabilities |\n|----------|-------------|---------------|------------|--------------|\n| **Filesystem-based** | Computer environment with bash/unix | Shell commands (`cat`, `ls`, etc.) | Low | Full skill capabilities including script execution |\n| **Tool-based** | No dedicated computer environment | Custom tools implemented by agent | Medium | Limited by tool implementation |\n\n### Filesystem-Based Integration\n\nFilesystem-based agents operate within a computer environment where the model can issue shell commands directly. Skills are accessed through standard filesystem operations.\n\n**Characteristics:**\n- Skills activated via `cat /path/to/skill/SKILL.md` [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:12]()\n- Scripts executed via direct shell invocation (e.g., `python scripts/extract.py`) [docs/specification.mdx:231-233]()\n- Resources accessed through relative paths from skill root [docs/specification.mdx:226-235]()\n- Requires agent to have shell command execution capability [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:127]()\n\nDetailed implementation guidance: [Filesystem-Based Integration](#3.2)\n\n### Tool-Based Integration\n\nTool-based agents lack direct filesystem access and must implement custom tools that abstract skill operations.\n\n**Characteristics:**\n- Agent implements tools like `read_skill`, `execute_skill_script`, `get_skill_resource` [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:12]()\n- Tool signatures and behavior defined by implementer [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:12]()\n- Works in sandboxed or cloud-hosted environments [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:93-102]()\n- Requires explicit tool design and implementation [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:12]()\n\nDetailed implementation guidance: [Tool-Based Integration](#3.3)\n\nSources: [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:9-16](), [docs/specification.mdx:226-235]()\n\n---\n\n## Integration Lifecycle\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Agent as \"Agent_Runtime\"\n    participant Scanner as \"Skill_Scanner\"\n    participant FS as \"Filesystem\u003cbr/\u003e(skill_directories)\"\n    participant Parser as \"YAML_Parser\"\n    participant Prompt as \"System_Prompt\"\n    participant Model as \"LLM\"\n    \n    Note over Agent,Model: Phase 1: Discovery (Startup)\n    Agent-\u003e\u003eScanner: scan_skill_directories([\"/path/to/skills\"])\n    Scanner-\u003e\u003eFS: list directories\n    FS--\u003e\u003eScanner: [\"pdf-processing/\", \"data-analysis/\", ...]\n    \n    loop For each skill directory\n        Scanner-\u003e\u003eFS: read \"SKILL.md\"\n        FS--\u003e\u003eScanner: file contents\n        Scanner-\u003e\u003eParser: extract frontmatter\n        Parser--\u003e\u003eScanner: {name, description}\n    end\n    \n    Scanner--\u003e\u003eAgent: skill_metadata[]\n    \n    Note over Agent,Model: Phase 2: Load Metadata\n    Agent-\u003e\u003ePrompt: inject available_skills XML\n    Prompt--\u003e\u003eAgent: updated system prompt\n    \n    Note over Agent,Model: Phase 3: Match Task to Skill\n    Model-\u003e\u003eAgent: \"I need to extract data from a PDF\"\n    Agent-\u003e\u003eModel: context includes skill descriptions\n    Model-\u003e\u003eModel: identify \"pdf-processing\" skill\n    \n    Note over Agent,Model: Phase 4: Activate Skill\n    Model-\u003e\u003eAgent: cat /path/to/pdf-processing/SKILL.md\n    Agent-\u003e\u003eFS: read full SKILL.md\n    FS--\u003e\u003eAgent: complete file (frontmatter + body)\n    Agent--\u003e\u003eModel: skill instructions in context\n    \n    Note over Agent,Model: Phase 5: Execute Resources\n    Model-\u003e\u003eAgent: python scripts/extract_pdf.py input.pdf\n    Agent-\u003e\u003eFS: execute script\n    FS--\u003e\u003eAgent: script output\n    Agent--\u003e\u003eModel: execution results\n```\n\n**Integration Lifecycle: Phases from Discovery to Execution**\n\nThe lifecycle follows the progressive disclosure pattern: minimal metadata at startup, full instructions on activation, and resources on demand. Each phase minimizes context usage while maximizing capability.\n\nSources: [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:20-31](), [docs/specification.mdx:214-223]()\n\n---\n\n## Core Integration Requirements\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Required_Capabilities\"\n        DIR_SCAN[\"Directory_Scanning\u003cbr/\u003eFind_SKILL.md_files\"]\n        YAML_PARSE[\"YAML_Parsing\u003cbr/\u003eExtract_name_+_description\"]\n        PROMPT_INJ[\"Prompt_Injection\u003cbr/\u003eavailable_skills_block\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Optional_Capabilities\"\n        VALIDATE[\"Validation\u003cbr/\u003eskills-ref_validate\"]\n        PROPS_READ[\"Property_Reading\u003cbr/\u003eskills-ref_read-properties\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Skill_Access_Layer\"\n        FS_OPS[\"Filesystem_Operations\"]\n        TOOL_IMPL[\"Tool_Implementation\"]\n    end\n    \n    DIR_SCAN --\u003e YAML_PARSE\n    YAML_PARSE --\u003e PROMPT_INJ\n    \n    VALIDATE -.-\u003e|\"optional_helper\"| DIR_SCAN\n    PROPS_READ -.-\u003e|\"optional_helper\"| YAML_PARSE\n    \n    PROMPT_INJ --\u003e FS_OPS\n    PROMPT_INJ --\u003e TOOL_IMPL\n```\n\n**Core vs Optional Integration Capabilities**\n\nA minimally compliant agent must implement directory scanning, YAML parsing, and prompt injection. The `skills-ref` library provides optional utilities but is not required for integration.\n\n### Minimum Required Implementation\n\n1. **Skill Discovery**: Scan configured directories (e.g., `.agents/skills/`) for folders containing `SKILL.md` files [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:45-54, 62-73]().\n2. **Metadata Extraction**: Parse YAML frontmatter from each `SKILL.md` to extract `name` and `description` fields [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:103-114]().\n3. **System Prompt Injection**: Format skill metadata into system prompt (typically as XML `\u003cavailable_skills\u003e` blocks) [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:116-120]().\n4. **Skill Activation**: Provide mechanism for model to read full `SKILL.md` contents [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:25]().\n5. **Resource Access**: Enable model to access `scripts/`, `references/`, and `assets/` directories [docs/specification.mdx:187-213]().\n\nDetailed requirements specification: [Integration Requirements](#3.1)\n\n### Optional Reference Utilities\n\nThe `skills-ref` library provides optional utilities:\n\n| Utility | Purpose | Usage |\n|---------|---------|-------|\n| `skills-ref validate` | Validate `SKILL.md` format | Development-time checking [docs/specification.mdx:242]() |\n| `skills-ref read-properties` | Extract metadata as JSON | Programmatic metadata access [docs/specification.mdx:242]() |\n\nSee [skills-ref Reference Library](#5) for complete documentation of these utilities.\n\nSources: [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:20-31](), [docs/specification.mdx:239-245]()\n\n---\n\n## SKILL.md File Structure Integration\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"SKILL.md_File\"\n        FILE[\"skill-name/SKILL.md\"]\n        \n        subgraph \"YAML_Frontmatter\"\n            NAME[\"name:_skill-name\"]\n            DESC[\"description:_When_to_use...\"]\n            LICENSE[\"license:_Apache-2.0\"]\n            COMPAT[\"compatibility:_Requirements\"]\n            META[\"metadata:_{author,_version}\"]\n            TOOLS[\"allowed-tools:_Bash_Read\"]\n        end\n        \n        subgraph \"Markdown_Body\"\n            INSTRUCTIONS[\"#_Instructions\u003cbr/\u003e##_When_to_use\u003cbr/\u003e##_How_to...\"]\n        end\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Discovery_Phase_Extraction\"\n        PARSE_NAME[\"Extract:_name\"]\n        PARSE_DESC[\"Extract:_description\"]\n        IGNORE_REST[\"Ignore:_other_frontmatter\"]\n        SKIP_BODY[\"Skip:_markdown_body\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"System_Prompt_Format\"\n        XML_SKILL[\"skill\u003cbr/\u003ename.../name\u003cbr/\u003edescription.../description\u003cbr/\u003elocation.../location\u003cbr/\u003e/skill\"]\n    end\n    \n    FILE --\u003e NAME\n    FILE --\u003e DESC\n    FILE --\u003e LICENSE\n    FILE --\u003e COMPAT\n    FILE --\u003e META\n    FILE --\u003e TOOLS\n    FILE --\u003e INSTRUCTIONS\n    \n    NAME --\u003e PARSE_NAME\n    DESC --\u003e PARSE_DESC\n    LICENSE --\u003e IGNORE_REST\n    COMPAT --\u003e IGNORE_REST\n    META --\u003e IGNORE_REST\n    TOOLS --\u003e IGNORE_REST\n    INSTRUCTIONS --\u003e SKIP_BODY\n    \n    PARSE_NAME --\u003e XML_SKILL\n    PARSE_DESC --\u003e XML_SKILL\n```\n\n**SKILL.md Parsing for Discovery Phase**\n\nDuring discovery, only `name` and `description` fields are extracted from YAML frontmatter. All other frontmatter fields and the entire markdown body are ignored to minimize initial context usage. The extracted metadata is formatted into the system prompt.\n\n### Frontmatter Field Constraints\n\n| Field | Required | Discovery Phase | Constraints |\n|-------|----------|----------------|-------------|\n| `name` | Yes | **Extracted** | 1-64 chars, lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens [docs/specification.mdx:27, 58-65]() |\n| `description` | Yes | **Extracted** | 1-1024 chars, explains when to use skill [docs/specification.mdx:28, 91-97]() |\n| `license` | No | Ignored | License identifier or file reference [docs/specification.mdx:29, 110-115]() |\n| `compatibility` | No | Ignored | Max 500 chars, environment requirements [docs/specification.mdx:30, 123-129]() |\n| `metadata` | No | Ignored | Arbitrary key-value mapping [docs/specification.mdx:31, 147-152]() |\n| `allowed-tools` | No | Ignored | Space-delimited tool list (experimental) [docs/specification.mdx:32, 163-168]() |\n\nThe markdown body containing skill instructions is loaded only during the activation phase, not during discovery [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:25]().\n\nSources: [docs/specification.mdx:21-32](), [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:103-114]()\n\n---\n\n## Progressive Disclosure Integration Pattern\n\nThe integration follows a state machine where each phase loads progressively more content. Discovery loads minimal metadata (~100 tokens per skill), activation loads full instructions (\u003c5000 tokens), and execution accesses resources on demand.\n\n### Context Usage by Phase\n\n| Tier | What's loaded | When | Token cost |\n|------|--------------|------|------------|\n| 1. Catalog | Name + description | Session start | ~50-100 tokens per skill [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:24]() |\n| 2. Instructions | Full `SKILL.md` body | When skill is activated | \u003c5000 tokens (recommended) [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:25]() |\n| 3. Resources | Scripts, references, assets | When instructions reference them | Varies [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:26]() |\n\nThis phased approach allows agents to maintain awareness of dozens or hundreds of skills without overwhelming the context window, loading full details only when relevant.\n\nSources: [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:20-31](), [docs/specification.mdx:214-223]()\n\n---\n\n## Security Considerations\n\nScript execution and resource access introduce security risks that integrations must address.\n\n**Trust Considerations**: Project-level skills come from the repository being worked on, which may be untrusted. Consider gating project-level skill loading on a trust check to prevent untrusted repositories from silently injecting instructions [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:89-92]().\n\n**Scanning Bounds**: Set reasonable bounds (e.g., max depth of 4-6 levels, max 2000 directories) to prevent runaway scanning in large directory trees [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:79]().\n\nSources: [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:75-92]()\n\n---\n\n## Integration Checklist\n\nUse this checklist to verify integration completeness:\n\n**Discovery Phase:**\n- [ ] Scan configured directories (Project, User, Global) for `SKILL.md` files [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:38-54]()\n- [ ] Parse YAML frontmatter from each file [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:103-114]()\n- [ ] Handle name collisions (Project-level overrides User-level) [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:81-88]()\n\n**System Prompt Phase:**\n- [ ] Format metadata into `\u003cavailable_skills\u003e` XML [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:116-120]()\n- [ ] Inject XML block into system prompt [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:12]()\n\n**Activation Phase:**\n- [ ] Provide mechanism to read full `SKILL.md` contents [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:25]()\n- [ ] Load complete markdown body into context [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:25]()\n\n**Execution Phase:**\n- [ ] Enable access to `scripts/`, `references/`, and `assets/` directories [docs/specification.mdx:187-213]()\n- [ ] Support relative path references from skill root [docs/specification.mdx:226-235]()\n\nSources: [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:1-114](), [docs/specification.mdx:187-237]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"1e:T220f,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Integration Requirements\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx](docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx)\n- [docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx](docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx)\n- [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx](docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx)\n- [docs/specification.mdx](docs/specification.mdx)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThis document defines the technical requirements for AI agent systems to support the Agent Skills standard. To be compatible, an agent must implement a lifecycle that handles skill discovery, metadata parsing, and multi-stage activation. These requirements ensure that agents can scale to hundreds of specialized capabilities without overwhelming their context window by following the principle of progressive disclosure.\n\nFor implementation-specific guides, see [Filesystem-Based Integration](3.2) or [Tool-Based Integration](3.3).\n\n## The Core Principle: Progressive Disclosure\n\nEvery skills-compatible agent follows a three-tier loading strategy to manage context tokens efficiently. This flow moves from high-level discovery to deep execution.\n\n| Tier | What's Loaded | When | Token Cost |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| **1. Catalog** | `name` + `description` | Session start | ~50-100 tokens per skill |\n| **2. Instructions** | Full `SKILL.md` body | On activation | \u003c 5000 tokens (recommended) |\n| **3. Resources** | Scripts, references, assets | On demand | Variable |\n\n**Sources:** [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:18-30](), [docs/specification.mdx:214-223]()\n\n## Step 1: Skill Discovery\n\nAgents must scan specific environment locations to find valid skill directories. A directory is recognized as a skill if it contains a file named exactly `SKILL.md` [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:62-63]().\n\n### Directory Scanning Logic\nAgents should scan multiple scopes to support both project-specific and global skills. While the specification doesn't mandate paths, the following have emerged as industry conventions:\n\n| Scope | Recommended Path | Purpose |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| **Project** | `\u003cproject\u003e/.agents/skills/` | Cross-client interoperability [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:50-50]() |\n| **User** | `~/.agents/skills/` | Shared skills across all projects [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:52-52]() |\n\n### Discovery Workflow\n1. **Identify Roots**: Locate project and user home directories. Some implementations also scan ancestor directories up to the git root or XDG config directories [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:56-58]().\n2. **Scan**: Look for subdirectories containing `SKILL.md`. Agents should skip `node_modules` and `.git` and optionally respect `.gitignore` [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:75-79]().\n3. **Handle Collisions**: If two skills share a name, **project-level skills override user-level skills** [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:83-85]().\n4. **Trust Check**: For project-level skills (which may come from untrusted repos), agents should gate loading behind a user trust confirmation [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:89-92]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:32-55](), [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:75-92]()\n\n## Step 2: Metadata Parsing\n\nOnce discovered, the agent must parse the `SKILL.md` frontmatter. This is a YAML block delimited by `---` [docs/specification.mdx:21-23]().\n\n### Required Fields\nThe agent must extract these fields for the Tier 1 Catalog:\n*   **`name`**: 1-64 chars, lowercase alphanumeric and hyphens. It must match the parent directory name [docs/specification.mdx:58-65]().\n*   **`description`**: 1-1024 chars, used by the LLM to decide when to trigger the skill. It should use imperative phrasing (e.g., \"Use this skill when...\") [docs/specification.mdx:91-97](), [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx:21-26]().\n\n### Data Flow: Discovery to Metadata\nThe following diagram maps the transition from the filesystem (Natural Language Space) to the internal agent data structures (Code Entity Space).\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Filesystem (NL Space)\"\n        FS_DIR[\"/path/to/my-skill/\"]\n        FS_MD[\"SKILL.md\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Agent Logic (Code Entity Space)\"\n        SCAN[\"scanForSkills()\"]\n        PARSE[\"parseMetadata()\"]\n        YAML[\"YAML.parse(frontmatter)\"]\n        CACHE[\"skillsCache: Map\u003cstring, SkillProperties\u003e\"]\n    end\n\n    FS_DIR --\u003e SCAN\n    SCAN --\u003e FS_MD\n    FS_MD --\u003e PARSE\n    PARSE --\u003e YAML\n    YAML --\u003e|\"name \u0026 description\"| CACHE\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:21-33](), [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:103-113](), [docs/specification.mdx:58-65]()\n\n## Step 3: Task Matching \u0026 Prompt Injection\n\nThe agent makes the LLM aware of available skills by injecting their metadata into the system prompt. This allows the model to \"see\" the capabilities without loading full instructions.\n\n### System Prompt Injection\nThe standard format for injection uses an `\u003cavailable_skills\u003e` XML block. This block should contain the name and description of every discovered skill [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:131-143]().\n\n```xml\n\u003cavailable_skills\u003e\n  \u003cskill\u003e\n    \u003cname\u003epdf-processing\u003c/name\u003e\n    \u003cdescription\u003eExtract PDF text, fill forms, merge files. Use when handling PDFs.\u003c/description\u003e\n  \u003c/skill\u003e\n\u003c/available_skills\u003e\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:131-143]()\n\n## Step 4: Skill Activation\n\nWhen the LLM identifies a task matching a skill description, the agent moves to Tier 2: **Activation**.\n\n1. **Trigger**: The model requests the full content of the skill. This can be done via a file-reading tool or a dedicated skill-loading tool [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:145-155]().\n2. **Content Loading**: The agent reads the entire `SKILL.md` file, including the Markdown body content [docs/specification.mdx:185-185]().\n3. **Context Injection**: The full instructions are added to the conversation history or system prompt to guide the agent's next steps [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:145-155]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:145-155](), [docs/specification.mdx:214-223]()\n\n## Step 5: Resource Execution\n\nTier 3 involves the agent accessing auxiliary files within the skill directory on an as-needed basis.\n\n### Directory Resolution\nAgents must resolve relative paths from the skill root to support the following optional directories [docs/specification.mdx:226-235]():\n*   **`scripts/`**: Executable code (Python, Bash, JS). Scripts should be self-contained or clearly document dependencies [docs/specification.mdx:189-196]().\n*   **`references/`**: Documentation like `REFERENCE.md` or `FORMS.md`. These should be loaded on demand to save context [docs/specification.mdx:198-206]().\n*   **`assets/`**: Static resources like templates or data files [docs/specification.mdx:207-213]().\n\n### Resource Execution Flow\nThis diagram illustrates how an agent resolves a request for a script file within a skill.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"LLM Context\"\n        INSTR[\"SKILL.md instructions:\u003cbr/\u003e'Run scripts/extract.py'\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Agent Implementation (Code Entity Space)\"\n        REQ[\"handleResourceRequest()\"]\n        RESOLVE[\"resolveRelativePath(skillRoot, 'scripts/extract.py')\"]\n        EXEC[\"executeScript()\"]\n        SHELL[\"subprocess.run(['python3', ...])\"]\n    end\n\n    INSTR --\u003e REQ\n    REQ --\u003e RESOLVE\n    RESOLVE --\u003e EXEC\n    EXEC --\u003e SHELL\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:187-213](), [docs/specification.mdx:224-235]()\n\n## Technical Implementation Checklist\n\n| Requirement | Description | Reference |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| **YAML Support** | Must parse frontmatter between `---` delimiters. | [docs/specification.mdx:21-23]() |\n| **Relative Pathing** | Must resolve files relative to the `SKILL.md` location. | [docs/specification.mdx:224-228]() |\n| **Interpreter Support** | Should support common runtimes (Python, Node, Bash). | [docs/specification.mdx:196-196]() |\n| **Token Management** | Must avoid loading Tier 2/3 content until requested. | [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:18-30]() |\n| **Validation** | Use `skills-ref validate` to ensure skill compatibility. | [docs/specification.mdx:237-242]() |\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:21-242](), [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:18-30]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"1f:T2162,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Filesystem-Based Integration\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx](docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx)\n- [docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx](docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx)\n- [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx](docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx)\n- [docs/specification.mdx](docs/specification.mdx)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document describes the integration approach for AI agents that operate within a computer environment with direct filesystem access (bash/unix). This method allows agents to discover, activate, and execute skills through standard shell operations and file manipulation.\n\nFor agents without direct filesystem access, see **3.3 Tool-Based Integration**. For the underlying format details, see **2.2 SKILL.md Specification**.\n\n## Overview\n\nIn a filesystem-based integration, skills are represented as directories on the local or remote storage. The agent interacts with these skills using standard system commands like `ls`, `cat`, and script interpreters (e.g., `python`, `node`). This approach leverages the host's existing environment for execution and dependency management.\n\n**Sources:** [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:11-12](), [docs/specification.mdx:6-17]()\n\n## Skill Discovery and Scanning\n\nAt session startup, the agent identifies available skills by scanning specific directory scopes. A directory is recognized as a skill if it contains a file named exactly `SKILL.md` [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:62-67]().\n\n### Recommended Scan Scopes\n\nTo ensure interoperability and user convenience, agents should scan the following locations:\n\n| Scope | Path Convention | Purpose |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| **Project** | `\u003cproject\u003e/.agents/skills/` | Cross-client interoperability within a repo [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:50-50]() |\n| **Project** | `\u003cproject\u003e/.\u003cyour-client\u003e/skills/` | Client-specific project skills [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:49-49]() |\n| **User** | `~/.agents/skills/` | Global skills shared across all projects [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:52-52]() |\n| **User** | `~/.\u003cyour-client\u003e/skills/` | Client-specific global skills [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:51-51]() |\n\n### Scanning Constraints\nTo prevent performance degradation in large filesystems (e.g., monorepos), the scanner should:\n- Skip directories that won't contain skills, such as `node_modules/` and `.git/` [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:77-77]().\n- Optionally respect `.gitignore` rules to avoid scanning build artifacts [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:78-78]().\n- Set reasonable bounds, such as a max depth of 4-6 levels and a max of 2000 directories [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:79-79]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:38-80]()\n\n## Data Flow: Discovery to Prompt\n\nThe following diagram illustrates how the system transitions from the physical filesystem into the agent's logical \"Code Entity Space\" by parsing metadata into a system prompt.\n\n### Discovery and Metadata Extraction\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    subgraph \"Filesystem Space\"\n        DIR[\"Skill Directory: .agents/skills/pdf-tool/\"]\n        FILE[\"SKILL.md\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space (Parser)\"\n        PARSE[\"Parser Logic\"]\n        FRONT[\"YAML Frontmatter\"]\n        BODY[\"Markdown Body\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Natural Language Space (Agent Context)\"\n        PROMPT[\"System Prompt XML\"]\n        DESC[\"Skill Description\"]\n        NAME[\"Skill Name\"]\n    end\n\n    DIR --\u003e FILE\n    FILE --\u003e PARSE\n    PARSE --\u003e FRONT\n    PARSE --\u003e BODY\n    \n    FRONT --\u003e|extract name| NAME\n    FRONT --\u003e|extract description| DESC\n    NAME --\u003e|inject into available_skills| PROMPT\n    DESC --\u003e|inject into available_skills| PROMPT\n```\n**Sources:** [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:103-115](), [docs/specification.mdx:214-223]()\n\n## Progressive Disclosure Implementation\n\nFilesystem agents implement progressive disclosure by strictly controlling when file content is read into the LLM context. This manages the agent's context window efficiently.\n\n| Tier | Content | Filesystem Operation | Context Impact |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| **1. Catalog** | `name`, `description` | `cat SKILL.md` (Partial/Head) | ~100 tokens [docs/specification.mdx:218-218]() |\n| **2. Instructions** | Full `SKILL.md` body | `cat SKILL.md` (Full) | \u003c 5000 tokens (recommended) [docs/specification.mdx:219-219]() |\n| **3. Resources** | Scripts, References, Assets | `cat`, `python`, `ls`, `npx` | Variable [docs/specification.mdx:220-220]() |\n\n### Activation Mechanism\nWhen an agent determines a skill is relevant based on its `description` [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx:7-15](), it must explicitly read the file. In a filesystem-based integration, the agent typically uses a file-reading tool to load the full `SKILL.md` content [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:12-13]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:18-31](), [docs/specification.mdx:214-223](), [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx:11-18]()\n\n## Execution and Resource Access\n\nOnce a skill is activated, the agent follows the instructions in the `SKILL.md` body. These instructions often reference files in subdirectories.\n\n### Relative Path Resolution\nAll references within `SKILL.md` are relative to the skill's root directory [docs/specification.mdx:226-227](). Agents are encouraged to keep file references one level deep (e.g., `scripts/` or `references/`) [docs/specification.mdx:234-235]().\n\n```markdown\n# SKILL.md Example\nRun the script:\nscripts/process_data.py\n```\n\nThe agent resolves this by prepending the skill's absolute path before executing a shell command or reading a reference file [docs/specification.mdx:231-235]().\n\n### Resource Interaction Diagram\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant LLM as \"Agent (LLM)\"\n    participant Shell as \"Bash/Shell\"\n    participant FS as \"Filesystem\"\n\n    Note over LLM: Matches task to 'pdf-processing'\n    LLM-\u003e\u003eShell: cat /path/to/pdf-processing/SKILL.md\n    Shell-\u003e\u003eFS: Read SKILL.md\n    FS--\u003e\u003eLLM: Return full instructions\n    \n    Note over LLM: Instruction: \"Use scripts/extract.py\"\n    LLM-\u003e\u003eShell: python3 /path/to/pdf-processing/scripts/extract.py input.pdf\n    Shell-\u003e\u003eFS: Execute Script\n    FS--\u003e\u003eLLM: Return script output (JSON/Text)\n    \n    Note over LLM: Instruction: \"Read references/REFERENCE.md\"\n    LLM-\u003e\u003eShell: cat /path/to/pdf-processing/references/REFERENCE.md\n    Shell-\u003e\u003eFS: Read Documentation\n    FS--\u003e\u003eLLM: Return technical details\n```\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:189-205](), [docs/specification.mdx:224-235](), [docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx:86-91]()\n\n## Key Implementation Considerations\n\n### Handling Collisions\nIf two skills share the same `name`, agents apply a deterministic precedence rule. The universal convention is that **project-level skills override user-level skills** [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:83-88]().\n\n### Trust Boundaries\nBecause project-level skills can be part of an untrusted repository, agents should implement a **trust check** before loading or executing skills found in a project directory. This prevents untrusted code from silently injecting instructions or running malicious scripts [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:89-92]().\n\n### Directory Structure Requirements\nA valid skill directory must follow the structure defined in the specification:\n- `SKILL.md`: Required root file containing metadata and instructions [docs/specification.mdx:12-12]().\n- `scripts/`: Optional directory for executable code (Python, Bash, JS) [docs/specification.mdx:13-13]().\n- `references/`: Optional directory for additional documentation [docs/specification.mdx:14-14]().\n- `assets/`: Optional directory for static resources like templates or data files [docs/specification.mdx:15-15]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:83-92](), [docs/specification.mdx:6-17]()\n\n---\n**Sources:**\n- [docs/specification.mdx:1-245]()\n- [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:1-115]()\n- [docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx:1-109]()\n- [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx:1-18]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"20:T1e7d,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Tool-Based Integration\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx](docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx)\n- [docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx](docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx)\n- [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx](docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx)\n- [docs/specification.mdx](docs/specification.mdx)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document describes how agents without direct filesystem access integrate Agent Skills support through custom tool implementations. Tool-based agents cannot execute shell commands like `ls` or `cat` directly on the host, so they must provide alternative mechanisms for skill activation and resource access. For agents with bash/unix environment access, see [Filesystem-Based Integration](). For the general integration requirements all agents must implement, see [Integration Requirements]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:9-14]()\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nTool-based integration is designed for agents operating in constrained or managed environments:\n- Web-based AI assistants without a terminal interface.\n- Sandboxed environments where filesystem operations are restricted to specific APIs.\n- Platforms where custom tool definition is the primary extensibility mechanism.\n- Agents that prefer explicit tool invocation over raw shell commands for better observability.\n\nThe agent developer must implement custom tools that allow the model to trigger skill activation and access bundled resources. The specific tool names, parameters, and behavior are implementation-defined but should follow the progressive disclosure pattern.\n\n**Sources:** [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:11-14](), [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:18-31]()\n\n## Architecture Overview\n\nTool-based agents replace direct file reading with structured tool calls. The following diagram shows how a tool-based agent bridges the gap between the Natural Language Space (User Intent) and the Code Entity Space (Skill Assets).\n\n### From Intent to Skill Execution\nTitle: \"Tool-Based Skill Interaction Flow\"\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Natural Language Space\"\n        USER_REQ[\"User Request:\u003cbr/\u003e'Process this PDF'\"]\n        MODEL_DECISION[\"Model identifies\u003cbr/\u003e'pdf-processing' skill\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Agent Integration Layer\"\n        TOOL_REGISTRY[\"Tool Registry\"]\n        ACTIVATE[\"activate_skill(name)\"]\n        READ_REF[\"read_reference(skill, file)\"]\n        EXEC_SCRIPT[\"execute_script(skill, script)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space (Filesystem/DB)\"\n        SKILL_DIR[\"pdf-processing/\"]\n        SKILL_MD[\"SKILL.md\"]\n        REF_DIR[\"references/\"]\n        SCRIPT_DIR[\"scripts/\"]\n    end\n    \n    USER_REQ --\u003e MODEL_DECISION\n    MODEL_DECISION --\u003e|\"Call Tool\"| ACTIVATE\n    MODEL_DECISION --\u003e|\"Call Tool\"| READ_REF\n    \n    ACTIVATE --\u003e|\"Read Body\"| SKILL_MD\n    READ_REF --\u003e|\"Read File\"| REF_DIR\n    EXEC_SCRIPT --\u003e|\"Run Code\"| SCRIPT_DIR\n    \n    TOOL_REGISTRY -.-\u003e ACTIVATE\n    TOOL_REGISTRY -.-\u003e READ_REF\n    TOOL_REGISTRY -.-\u003e EXEC_SCRIPT\n```\n**Sources:** [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:18-31](), [docs/specification.mdx:6-17]()\n\n## Required Tool Implementations\n\nTo support the full lifecycle of a skill, an agent should implement tools corresponding to the tiers of progressive disclosure.\n\n| Tier | Tool Purpose | Parameters | Returns |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| **Activation** | Load full instructions | `skill_name: str` | `SKILL.md` body content (Markdown) |\n| **Discovery** | Read reference file | `skill_name: str`, `file: str` | Content of file in `references/` |\n| **Resource** | Read asset file | `skill_name: str`, `file: str` | Content of file in `assets/` |\n| **Execution** | Execute script | `skill_name: str`, `script: str` | Stdout/Stderr from `scripts/` |\n\n**Sources:** [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:22-26](), [docs/specification.mdx:187-213]()\n\n### Tool Implementation Details\n\n#### The `activate_skill` Tool\nThis tool transitions the agent from Tier 1 (Metadata) to Tier 2 (Instructions). When the model's router identifies a relevant skill from the system prompt, it invokes this tool to ingest the specific \"how-to\" guide.\n\n- **Input**: The `name` field from the skill's YAML frontmatter [docs/specification.mdx:27-27]().\n- **Logic**: The agent locates the `SKILL.md` file, strips the YAML frontmatter, and returns only the Markdown body [docs/specification.mdx:176-178]().\n\n#### The `read_reference` and `read_asset` Tools\nThese tools support Tier 3 (Resources). They prevent context bloat by allowing the model to pull in supplementary data (like `REFERENCE.md` or `FORMS.md`) only when the primary instructions require it [docs/specification.mdx:198-205]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:214-223]()\n\n## Skill Activation Workflow\n\nThe following sequence diagram maps the internal logic of a tool-based agent during a multi-step task.\n\n### Sequence of Progressive Disclosure\nTitle: \"Tool-Based Activation Sequence\"\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant U as User\n    participant M as Model\n    participant T as Tools (activate_skill)\n    participant S as Storage (SKILL.md)\n\n    U-\u003e\u003eM: \"I need to fill out a PDF form\"\n    Note over M: Scans \u003cavailable_skills\u003e in prompt\n    M-\u003e\u003eT: activate_skill(\"pdf-processing\")\n    T-\u003e\u003eS: Read SKILL.md\n    S--\u003e\u003eT: YAML + Markdown content\n    T--\u003e\u003eM: Markdown instructions only\n    Note over M: Instructions say: \"Read references/FORMS.md\"\n    M-\u003e\u003eT: read_reference(\"pdf-processing\", \"FORMS.md\")\n    T--\u003e\u003eM: Form field schema\n    M--\u003e\u003eU: \"I'm ready to fill the form...\"\n```\n**Sources:** [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:18-31](), [docs/specification.mdx:214-223]()\n\n## System Prompt Metadata\n\nFor tool-based agents, the system prompt must include the skill catalog (Tier 1). Unlike filesystem-based agents, tool-based agents do not necessarily need to provide the absolute path to the model, as the `skill_name` acts as the unique identifier for the custom tools.\n\n```xml\n\u003cavailable_skills\u003e\n  \u003cskill\u003e\n    \u003cname\u003epdf-processing\u003c/name\u003e\n    \u003cdescription\u003eExtract PDF text, fill forms, merge files. Use when handling PDFs.\u003c/description\u003e\n  \u003c/skill\u003e\n\u003c/available_skills\u003e\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:22-26](), [docs/specification.mdx:38-41]()\n\n## Handling Optional Directories\n\nThe agent's tool implementations must resolve relative paths defined in the `SKILL.md` body to the actual storage location.\n\n1. **Scripts**: Files in `scripts/` should be executed using the appropriate runtime (Python, Node, or Bash) as supported by the agent [docs/specification.mdx:189-196]().\n2. **References**: Files in `references/` are intended for reading and context injection [docs/specification.mdx:198-206]().\n3. **Assets**: Static files in `assets/` (templates, images, data) [docs/specification.mdx:208-213]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:224-235]()\n\n## Security and Trust\n\nIn tool-based integrations, the agent developer acts as a gatekeeper. \n\n- **Project-level skills**: If the agent is operating on a remote repository, it should verify if the user trusts the repository before allowing `activate_skill` to load instructions that might contain prompt injections [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:89-92]().\n- **Allowed Tools**: Agents should respect the `allowed-tools` field in the frontmatter to restrict which host-level tools a skill is permitted to invoke [docs/specification.mdx:163-174]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:89-92](), [docs/specification.mdx:32-32]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"21:T2272,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# System Prompt Injection\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx](docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx)\n- [docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx](docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx)\n- [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx](docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx)\n- [docs/specification.mdx](docs/specification.mdx)\n- [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/prompt.py](skills-ref/src/skills_ref/prompt.py)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThis page documents the technical implementation of injecting Agent Skills metadata into an AI agent's system prompt. This mechanism is the primary driver for the **Discovery** phase of the progressive disclosure model, allowing the model to identify relevant skills from a large catalog without the overhead of loading full instructions for every available skill. [docs/specification.mdx:214-221]().\n\nFor the high-level theory behind this approach, see **2.3 Progressive Disclosure Model**. For implementation details on scanning and parsing, see **3.1 Integration Requirements**.\n\n---\n\n## Overview\n\nSystem prompt injection occurs during agent initialization. Instead of providing the full contents of every `SKILL.md` file, the agent client extracts only the `name` and `description` from the YAML frontmatter of discovered skills [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:20-31](). These snippets are then formatted into a structured block (typically XML) and appended to the system prompt.\n\nThis approach ensures that:\n1. The model is aware of all available capabilities [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:28-30]().\n2. Initial context usage remains low (approx. 100 tokens per skill) [docs/specification.mdx:218]().\n3. The model has a \"hook\" (the file path or a tool call) to request the full instructions when needed [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:12-13]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:214-221](), [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:18-31]()\n\n---\n\n## XML Format Specification\n\nWhile the Agent Skills standard is model-agnostic, the reference implementation and best practices are optimized for XML-tag-aware models like Anthropic's Claude.\n\n### The `\u003cavailable_skills\u003e` Block\n\nThe recommended structure for injecting skills into a system prompt uses the following hierarchy, as implemented in the `skills-ref` library [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/prompt.py:24-30]():\n\n```xml\n\u003cavailable_skills\u003e\n  \u003cskill\u003e\n    \u003cname\u003eskill-name\u003c/name\u003e\n    \u003cdescription\u003eWhat this skill does and when to use it\u003c/description\u003e\n    \u003clocation\u003e/absolute/path/to/skill-directory/SKILL.md\u003c/location\u003e\n  \u003c/skill\u003e\n\u003c/available_skills\u003e\n```\n\n### Schema Elements\n\n| Element | Source | Requirement | Role |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `\u003cname\u003e` | `SKILL.md` frontmatter | Required | Unique identifier (1-64 chars, lowercase/hyphens) [docs/specification.mdx:27](). |\n| `\u003cdescription\u003e` | `SKILL.md` frontmatter | Required | Trigger text (1-1024 chars) used by the model for task matching [docs/specification.mdx:28](). |\n| `\u003clocation\u003e` | Filesystem path | Conditional | The absolute path used by the agent to `cat` or `read` the full instructions [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/prompt.py:49-52](). |\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:25-32](), [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:103-115](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/prompt.py:35-58]()\n\n---\n\n## Data Flow: From Frontmatter to Prompt\n\nThe following diagram bridges the **Natural Language Space** (the skill author's Markdown) to the **Code Entity Space** (the parsed metadata and final prompt string).\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Skill Directory (Filesystem)\"\n        SKILL_FILE[\"SKILL.md\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space (Parser/Logic)\"\n        PARSER[\"read_properties()\"]\n        PROPS[\"SkillProperties Object\u003cbr/\u003e(name, description)\"]\n        FIND_MD[\"find_skill_md()\"]\n        TO_PROMPT[\"to_prompt() Function\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"System Prompt (String Buffer)\"\n        XML_BLOCK[\"\u0026lt;available_skills\u0026gt;\u003cbr/\u003e  \u0026lt;skill\u0026gt;...\u0026lt;/skill\u0026gt;\u003cbr/\u003e\u0026lt;/available_skills\u0026gt;\"]\n    end\n\n    SKILL_FILE -- \"Read File\" --\u003e PARSER\n    PARSER -- \"Extract YAML\" --\u003e PROPS\n    SKILL_FILE -- \"Resolve Path\" --\u003e FIND_MD\n    PROPS -- \"List of SkillProperties\" --\u003e TO_PROMPT\n    FIND_MD -- \"Skill MD Path\" --\u003e TO_PROMPT\n    TO_PROMPT -- \"XML Serialization\" --\u003e XML_BLOCK\n```\n\n**Diagram: Metadata Extraction and Prompt Serialization Flow**\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/prompt.py:9-58](), [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:103-115]()\n\n---\n\n## Implementation via `skills-ref`\n\nThe `skills-ref` reference library provides the standard implementation for this injection logic via the `prompt` module [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/prompt.py:1-58]().\n\n### CLI Generation\nThe `to-prompt` command automates the generation of the XML block for one or more skill directories. This is useful for testing how skills will appear to the model.\n\n### Python API Integration\nIn a Python-based agent client, the `to_prompt` function is used to build the system message dynamically:\n\n```python\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom skills_ref.prompt import to_prompt\n\n# 1. Discover skill paths (e.g., from .agents/skills/)\nskill_paths = [Path(\"./skills/pdf-processing\"), Path(\"./skills/data-analysis\")]\n\n# 2. Generate the XML block\n# This function calls read_properties() and find_skill_md() internally\navailable_skills_xml = to_prompt(skill_paths)\n\n# 3. Inject into system prompt\nsystem_prompt = f\"You are a helpful assistant.\\n\\n{available_skills_xml}\"\n```\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/prompt.py:9-58]()\n\n---\n\n## Integration Patterns\n\nThe content of the `\u003clocation\u003e` element and the model's behavior change based on the agent's architecture.\n\n### 1. Filesystem-Based (Bash/Unix)\nFor agents with direct shell access, the `\u003clocation\u003e` provides the absolute path. The model is instructed to use a tool like `cat` or `read_file` on that path when it needs the full instructions [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:11-12]().\n\n### 2. Tool-Based (Sandboxed)\nFor agents in restricted environments (cloud-hosted or sandboxed), discovery might happen via a remote registry [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:95-99](). The agent might provide a dedicated tool to load skill content programmatically instead of relying on direct file reads [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:12-13]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:9-15](), [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:93-101]()\n\n---\n\n## Lifecycle: Discovery to Activation\n\nThe diagram below illustrates how the injected metadata in the system prompt enables the transition from the Discovery phase to the Activation phase.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant Agent_Client\n    participant LLM\n    participant Filesystem\n\n    Note over Agent_Client, Filesystem: Phase 1: Discovery (Startup)\n    Agent_Client-\u003e\u003eFilesystem: Scan .agents/skills/\n    Filesystem--\u003e\u003eAgent_Client: List of skill directories\n    Agent_Client-\u003e\u003eAgent_Client: to_prompt(skill_dirs)\n    Agent_Client-\u003e\u003eLLM: System Prompt with \u003cavailable_skills\u003e\n\n    Note over User, LLM: Phase 2: Activation (Runtime)\n    User-\u003e\u003eLLM: \"Merge these two PDF files.\"\n    LLM-\u003e\u003eLLM: Match \"PDF\" to description in \u003cavailable_skills\u003e\n    LLM-\u003e\u003eAgent_Client: Call read_file(\u003clocation\u003e)\n    Agent_Client-\u003e\u003eFilesystem: Read full SKILL.md body\n    Filesystem--\u003e\u003eLLM: Full Instructions \u0026 Examples\n    LLM-\u003e\u003eUser: Follows instructions to perform task\n```\n\n**Diagram: The Role of System Prompt Injection in Skill Activation**\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:214-221](), [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:18-31](), [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx:11-18]()\n\n---\n\n## Token and Performance Optimization\n\nTo maintain agent performance as the number of skills grows, clients and authors should adhere to these constraints:\n\n*   **Description Calibration:** Use imperative phrasing and focus on user intent [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx:21-26]().\n*   **Context Parsimony:** Add what the agent lacks, omit what it knows [docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx:50-54]().\n*   **Metadata Overhead:** The `name` and `description` fields are loaded at startup for all skills, targeting ~100 tokens per skill [docs/specification.mdx:218]().\n*   **Size Limits:** The `description` field is limited to 1024 characters [docs/specification.mdx:28]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/specification.mdx:91-108](), [docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx:48-54](),
