[skills-ref/README.md:61-66](), [skills-ref/README.md:93-107]()\n\n## Module Architecture\n\n### Entry Points and Interfaces\n\nThe library is structured to provide a clean separation between the command-line interface and the core logic modules, all exposed via the top-level package.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    User[\"User\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Entry Points\"\n        CLIEntry[\"CLI Executable\"]\n        PythonImport[\"from skills_ref import\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Public API [src/skills_ref/__init__.py]\"\n        ValidateAPI[\"validate()\"]\n        ReadPropsAPI[\"read_properties()\"]\n        ToPromptAPI[\"to_prompt()\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Data Models \u0026 Errors\"\n        SkillProps[\"SkillProperties\"]\n        SkillErr[\"SkillError\"]\n    end\n    \n    User --\u003e CLIEntry\n    User --\u003e PythonImport\n    \n    CLIEntry --\u003e ValidateAPI\n    CLIEntry --\u003e ReadPropsAPI\n    CLIEntry --\u003e ToPromptAPI\n    \n    ValidateAPI --\u003e SkillErr\n    ReadPropsAPI --\u003e SkillProps\n    ToPromptAPI --\u003e ReadPropsAPI\n```\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/__init__.py:1-21](), [skills-ref/README.md:71-73]()\n\n## Usage Patterns\n\n### CLI Usage Pattern\n\nThe CLI provides direct command-line access to all three capabilities, outputting results to `stdout`:\n\n```bash\n# Validation: exits with 0 on success, or prints errors on failure\nskills-ref validate path/to/skill\n\n# Property extraction: outputs JSON to stdout\nskills-ref read-properties path/to/skill\n\n# Prompt generation: outputs XML to stdout\nskills-ref to-prompt path/to/skill-a path/to/skill-b\n```\n\nThe CLI is activated after installing the package and sourcing the virtual environment [skills-ref/README.md:51-52]().\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/README.md:58-67]()\n\n### Python API Usage Pattern\n\nThe Python API exposes the same functionality through importable functions:\n\n```python\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom skills_ref import validate, read_properties, to_prompt\n\n# Validation returns list of problem strings\nproblems = validate(Path(\"my-skill\"))\n\n# Property extraction returns a SkillProperties object\nprops = read_properties(Path(\"my-skill\"))\n\n# Prompt generation returns XML string\nprompt = to_prompt([Path(\"skill-a\"), Path(\"skill-b\")])\n```\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/README.md:71-87](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/__init__.py:9-18]()\n\n## Integration with Agent Systems\n\nThe `to_prompt()` function generates XML structured for agent system prompts. The recommended format follows this structure:\n\n```xml\n\u003cavailable_skills\u003e\n\u003cskill\u003e\n\u003cname\u003e\nmy-skill\n\u003c/name\u003e\n\u003cdescription\u003e\nWhat this skill does and when to use it\n\u003c/description\u003e\n\u003clocation\u003e\n/path/to/my-skill/SKILL.md\n\u003c/location\u003e\n\u003c/skill\u003e\n\u003c/available_skills\u003e\n```\n\nThe `\u003clocation\u003e` element provides the filesystem path where agents can access full skill instructions during the activation phase of the progressive disclosure model [skills-ref/README.md:93-109]().\n\nWhile this XML format is optimized for Anthropic's models, agent developers may adapt the format for other model providers [skills-ref/README.md:90-91]().\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/README.md:89-107]()\n\n## Design Philosophy\n\nThe `skills-ref` library embodies several key design principles:\n\n1. **Demonstration Focus**: Explicitly not production-ready; serves as an educational reference for implementers [skills-ref/README.md:5-6]().\n2. **Dual Interface**: Provides both a CLI for manual testing and a Python API for programmatic integration [skills-ref/README.md:54-87]().\n3. **Path-Based**: All operations work with filesystem paths to skill directories [skills-ref/README.md:58-67]().\n4. **Progressive Disclosure Support**: The `to_prompt()` function generates metadata-only XML, supporting the discovery phase where full skill content is not yet loaded [skills-ref/README.md:109]().\n\n## Installation Overview\n\nThe library supports multiple installation methods across platforms:\n- `pip` with manual virtual environment creation.\n- `uv` for streamlined dependency management.\n- Platform-specific activation scripts for Windows, macOS, and Linux.\n\nAfter installation, the `skills-ref` CLI executable becomes available within the activated virtual environment [skills-ref/README.md:51-52]().\n\nFor complete installation procedures, see [Installation and Setup](#5.1).\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/README.md:8-52]()\n\n## License\n\nThe `skills-ref` library is released under the Apache License 2.0 [skills-ref/README.md:111-113]().\n\nFor detailed licensing information, see [Licensing](#5.6).\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/README.md:111-113]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"29:T1afb,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Installation and Setup\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [skills-ref/README.md](skills-ref/README.md)\n- [skills-ref/pyproject.toml](skills-ref/pyproject.toml)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThis page describes how to install the `skills-ref` reference library and configure a development environment. It covers system requirements, installation methods, dependency management, and verification procedures. The `skills-ref` library serves as a demonstration tool for validating and processing Agent Skills, rather than a production-ready framework [skills-ref/README.md:5-6]().\n\n---\n\n## Prerequisites\n\nThe `skills-ref` library requires Python 3.11 or higher, as specified in the project configuration [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:10]().\n\n**System Requirements:**\n\n| Requirement | Version |\n|-------------|---------|\n| Python | ≥ 3.11 |\n| Package Manager | `pip` (standard) or `uv` (recommended) |\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:10](), [skills-ref/README.md:10-25]()\n\n---\n\n## Installation Methods\n\nThe library supports two primary installation approaches: traditional `pip` installation and modern `uv` synchronization. Both methods are designed to set up an editable installation suitable for development.\n\n### Installation Flow Decision Tree\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Start[\"Install skills-ref\"]\n    CheckUV{\"Have uv installed?\"}\n    UseUV[\"Use uv sync method\"]\n    UsePip[\"Use pip method\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Pip Workflow\"\n        CreateVenv[\"python -m venv .venv\"]\n        ActivateVenv[\"source .venv/bin/activate\"]\n        PipInstall[\"pip install -e .\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"UV Workflow\"\n        UvSync[\"uv sync\"]\n        UvActivate[\"source .venv/bin/activate\"]\n    end\n    \n    Verify[\"Verify: skills-ref --help\"]\n    \n    Start --\u003e CheckUV\n    CheckUV --\u003e|Yes| UseUV\n    CheckUV --\u003e|No| UsePip\n    UsePip --\u003e CreateVenv\n    CreateVenv --\u003e ActivateVenv\n    ActivateVenv --\u003e PipInstall\n    UseUV --\u003e UvSync\n    UvSync --\u003e UvActivate\n    PipInstall --\u003e Verify\n    UvActivate --\u003e Verify\n```\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/README.md:10-50]()\n\n### Using pip\n\nFor macOS/Linux and Windows (PowerShell/CMD), the standard installation uses Python's built-in virtual environment module:\n\n**macOS / Linux:**\n```bash\npython -m venv .venv\nsource .venv/bin/activate\npip install -e .\n```\n\n**Windows (PowerShell):**\n```powershell\npython -m venv .venv\n.venv\\Scripts\\Activate.ps1\npip install -e .\n```\n\n**Windows (Command Prompt):**\n```cmd\npython -m venv .venv\n.venv\\Scripts\\activate.bat\npip install -e .\n```\n\nThe `-e` flag performs an \"editable\" install, meaning changes to the source code in `src/skills_ref` are immediately available without re-running the installation [skills-ref/README.md:17]().\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/README.md:10-18](), [skills-ref/README.md:29-43]()\n\n### Using uv\n\n[uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) provides a faster, more reliable installation experience by managing the virtual environment and dependencies in a single step:\n\n**macOS / Linux:**\n```bash\nuv sync\nsource .venv/bin/activate\n```\n\n**Windows (PowerShell):**\n```powershell\nuv sync\n.venv\\Scripts\\Activate.ps1\n```\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/README.md:22-25](), [skills-ref/README.md:45-50]()\n\n---\n\n## Dependency Architecture\n\nThe library's dependencies are defined in `pyproject.toml` and managed via two distinct groups: core runtime and development.\n\n### Dependency Graph (Natural Language to Code Entity)\n\nThis diagram bridges the conceptual requirements (e.g., \"YAML Parsing\") to the specific libraries and entry points defined in the codebase.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Natural Language Space\"\n        CLI[\"Command Line Interface\"]\n        YAML[\"YAML Validation\"]\n        Test[\"Unit Testing\"]\n        Lint[\"Code Quality\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space\"\n        Click[\"click\u003e=8.0\"]\n        StrictYAML[\"strictyaml\u003e=1.7.3\"]\n        Pytest[\"pytest\u003e=7.0\"]\n        Ruff[\"ruff\u003e=0.8.0\"]\n        Entry[\"skills-ref (CLI Entrypoint)\"]\n    end\n\n    CLI --\u003e Entry\n    Entry --\u003e Click\n    YAML --\u003e StrictYAML\n    Test --\u003e Pytest\n    Lint --\u003e Ruff\n\n    style Entry stroke-dasharray: 5 5\n```\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:11-30](), [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:16-17]()\n\n### Runtime Dependencies\n\n| Package | Version | Purpose |\n|---------|---------|---------|\n| `click` | ≥ 8.0 | Powers the CLI commands in `skills_ref.cli:main` [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:17]() |\n| `strictyaml` | ≥ 1.7.3 | Used for schema-based parsing of `SKILL.md` frontmatter [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:13]() |\n\n### Development Dependencies\n\nDefined under the `[dependency-groups]` dev section [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:26-30]():\n- **pytest**: For running the test suite.\n- **ruff**: For linting and formatting the codebase.\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:11-30]()\n\n---\n\n## Project Structure and Build Configuration\n\nThe project follows a `src/` layout, which is configured in the build system settings to ensure proper packaging.\n\n### Code Entity Mapping\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Project Root\"\n        PP[\"pyproject.toml\"]\n        RM[\"README.md\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Source Code (src/skills_ref/)\"\n        CLI_MOD[\"cli.py\"]\n        INIT[\"__init__.py\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Build Artifacts\"\n        WHEEL[\"Wheel Target\"]\n    end\n\n    PP -- \"Defines Package\" --\u003e INIT\n    PP -- \"Maps Entrypoint\" --\u003e CLI_MOD\n    PP -- \"Hatchling Config\" --\u003e WHEEL\n```\n\nThe `pyproject.toml` file uses **Hatchling** as the build backend [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:19-21](). It explicitly defines the package location to include only the `src/skills_ref` directory in the final distribution [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:23-24]().\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:19-24](), [skills-ref/README.md:71-73]()\n\n---\n\n## Verifying Installation\n\nOnce installed, the `skills-ref` executable is added to the `PATH` within the activated virtual environment [skills-ref/README.md:52]().\n\n### Verification Commands\n\n1. **Check CLI Accessibility:**\n   ```bash\n   skills-ref --help\n   ```\n   This confirms that the `project.scripts` mapping `skills-ref = \"skills_ref.cli:main\"` is working correctly [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:16-17]().\n\n2. **Verify Python API:**\n   Run a short script to ensure the package is importable:\n   ```python\n   from skills_ref import validate\n   print(\"Installation successful\")\n   ```\n   The `validate` function is exported via the top-level `skills_ref` package [skills-ref/README.md:73-76]().\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/README.md:52-67](), [skills-ref/README.md:71-87](), [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:16-17]()\n\n---\n\n## License and Metadata\n\n- **License**: Apache-2.0 [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:5]().\n- **Author**: Keith Lazuka (Anthropic) [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:7-9]().\n- **Version**: 0.1.0 [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:3]().\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:1-9](), [skills-ref/README.md:111-113]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"2a:T1806,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Command-Line Interface\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [skills-ref/README.md](skills-ref/README.md)\n- [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/cli.py](skills-ref/src/skills_ref/cli.py)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThis document describes the command-line interface (CLI) provided by the `skills-ref` reference library. The CLI exposes three primary commands for working with Agent Skills: `validate`, `read-properties`, and `to-prompt`. These commands enable validation of skill directories, extraction of skill metadata, and generation of XML blocks for agent system prompts.\n\nThe CLI is implemented using the `click` library and serves as a thin wrapper around the core logic in `parser.py`, `validator.py`, and `prompt.py`.\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/cli.py:1-13](), [skills-ref/README.md:1-13]()\n\n---\n\n## Overview\n\nThe `skills-ref` CLI is installed as a command-line executable when the library is installed via `pip install -e .` or `uv sync`. All commands operate on skill directories containing a `SKILL.md` file. A helper function `_is_skill_md_file` allows the CLI to accept either a directory path or a direct path to a `SKILL.md` file by resolving to the parent directory.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Natural Language Space\"\n        USER_INPUT[\"User Command \u0026 Paths\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space\"\n        CLI_MAIN[\"cli.py: main()\"]\n        CMD_VAL[\"cli.py: validate_cmd()\"]\n        CMD_READ[\"cli.py: read_properties_cmd()\"]\n        CMD_PROMPT[\"cli.py: to_prompt_cmd()\"]\n        \n        VAL_FUNC[\"validator.py: validate()\"]\n        READ_FUNC[\"parser.py: read_properties()\"]\n        PROMPT_FUNC[\"prompt.py: to_prompt()\"]\n    end\n\n    USER_INPUT --\u003e CLI_MAIN\n    CLI_MAIN --\u003e CMD_VAL\n    CLI_MAIN --\u003e CMD_READ\n    CLI_MAIN --\u003e CMD_PROMPT\n\n    CMD_VAL --\u003e VAL_FUNC\n    CMD_READ --\u003e READ_FUNC\n    CMD_PROMPT --\u003e PROMPT_FUNC\n```\n\n**Diagram: Mapping CLI Commands to Implementation Functions**\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/cli.py:15-24](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/cli.py:27-101]()\n\n---\n\n## Command Reference\n\n### validate\n\nThe `validate` command checks a skill directory for compliance with the specification. It calls the `validate()` function from the `validator` module.\n\n#### Syntax\n```bash\nskills-ref validate path/to/skill\n```\n\n#### Implementation Detail\nThe command checks if the provided path is a file named `skill.md` (case-insensitive) via `_is_skill_md_file`; if so, it uses the parent directory. It then executes `validate(skill_path)` and iterates through any returned errors.\n\n- **Success (Exit 0):** Prints \"Valid skill: [path]\" to stdout.\n- **Failure (Exit 1):** Prints \"Validation failed for [path]:\" followed by a list of errors to stderr.\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/cli.py:15-17](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/cli.py:27-51]()\n\n---\n\n### read-properties\n\nThe `read-properties` command extracts metadata from a skill's YAML frontmatter and outputs it as a JSON object.\n\n#### Syntax\n```bash\nskills-ref read-properties path/to/skill\n```\n\n#### Implementation Detail\nThis command invokes `read_properties(skill_path)` from the `parser` module. The resulting `SkillProperties` object is converted to a dictionary via `props.to_dict()` and printed as indented JSON.\n\n- **Error Handling:** If a `SkillError` occurs during parsing, the error message is printed to stderr and the process exits with code 1.\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/cli.py:53-73]()\n\n---\n\n### to-prompt\n\nThe `to-prompt` command generates XML-formatted `\u003cavailable_skills\u003e` blocks suitable for agent system prompts. It supports multiple skill paths.\n\n#### Syntax\n```bash\nskills-ref to-prompt path/to/skill-a path/to/skill-b [...]\n```\n\n#### Implementation Detail\nThe command accepts a variable number of arguments (`nargs=-1`). It resolves each path (handling direct `SKILL.md` references) and passes the list of resolved paths to `to_prompt(resolved_paths)` from the `prompt` module.\n\n#### Output Format\nThe generated XML follows this structure, which is recommended for Anthropic's models to facilitate progressive disclosure:\n```xml\n\u003cavailable_skills\u003e\n\u003cskill\u003e\n\u003cname\u003e\nmy-skill\n\u003c/name\u003e\n\u003cdescription\u003e\nWhat this skill does and when to use it\n\u003c/description\u003e\n\u003clocation\u003e\n/path/to/my-skill/SKILL.md\n\u003c/location\u003e\n\u003c/skill\u003e\n\u003c/available_skills\u003e\n```\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/cli.py:76-101](), [skills-ref/README.md:90-107]()\n\n---\n\n## Data Flow and Error Handling\n\nThe CLI acts as a coordinator between user input and the library's internal logic.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    subgraph \"CLI Entry (cli.py)\"\n        START[\"main()\"]\n        PATH_RES[\"_is_skill_md_file()\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Core Logic\"\n        P_READ[\"parser.py: read_properties()\"]\n        P_VAL[\"validator.py: validate()\"]\n        P_PROMPT[\"prompt.py: to_prompt()\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Output Processing\"\n        JSON_OUT[\"json.dumps(props.to_dict())\"]\n        XML_OUT[\"click.echo(output)\"]\n        ERR_OUT[\"click.echo(err=True)\"]\n    end\n\n    START --\u003e PATH_RES\n    PATH_RES --\u003e P_READ \u0026 P_VAL \u0026 P_PROMPT\n    \n    P_READ -- \"SkillProperties\" --\u003e JSON_OUT\n    P_VAL -- \"list[str]\" --\u003e ERR_OUT\n    P_PROMPT -- \"str (XML)\" --\u003e XML_OUT\n    \n    P_READ -. \"SkillError\" .-\u003e ERR_OUT\n    P_PROMPT -. \"SkillError\" .-\u003e ERR_OUT\n```\n\n**Diagram: CLI Data Flow and Error Handling**\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/cli.py:9-13](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/cli.py:39-48](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/cli.py:65-73](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/cli.py:89-101]()\n\n---\n\n## Exit Codes\n\nThe CLI uses standard exit codes to signal the result of operations:\n\n| Exit Code | Command | Meaning |\n|-----------|---------|---------|\n| 0 | All | Operation completed successfully. |\n| 1 | `validate` | Validation errors were found in the skill. |\n| 1 | `read-properties` | A `SkillError` occurred (e.g., missing file, invalid YAML). |\n| 1 | `to-prompt` | A `SkillError` occurred during prompt generation. |\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/cli.py:48](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/cli.py:73](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/cli.py:101]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"2b:T22d4,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Python API\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/errors.py](skills-ref/src/skills_ref/errors.py)\n- [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/models.py](skills-ref/src/skills_ref/models.py)\n- [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/parser.py](skills-ref/src/skills_ref/parser.py)\n- [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/prompt.py](skills-ref/src/skills_ref/prompt.py)\n- [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py](skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document covers the Python API provided by the `skills-ref` reference library. The API exposes functions for programmatically validating skills, reading skill metadata, and generating agent prompt XML.\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThe Python API enables programmatic integration of Agent Skills validation and processing into custom tooling, build pipelines, and agent systems. The API mirrors the functionality of the CLI but provides structured return types suitable for programmatic consumption.\n\n\u003e [!IMPORTANT]\n\u003e This library is intended for demonstration purposes only. It is not meant to be used in production [skills-ref/README.md:5-6]().\n\n## Module Structure\n\nThe `skills_ref` package exports its primary functions and classes from the top-level `__init__.py` file.\n\n```python\nfrom skills_ref import (\n    validate,          # Skill validation logic\n    read_properties,   # Metadata parsing\n    to_prompt,         # XML generation\n    SkillProperties,   # Data model\n    SkillError,        # Base exception\n    ParseError,        # Parsing exception\n    ValidationError    # Validation exception\n)\n```\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/errors.py:4-21](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/models.py:8-26](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/parser.py:67-112](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:150-177](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/prompt.py:9-58]()\n\n## API Architecture\n\nThe following diagram bridges the Natural Language concepts of \"Skills\" to the specific Code Entities in the `skills-ref` library.\n\n### Code Entity Space Mapping\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Natural Language Space\"\n        SKILL[\"Agent Skill\"]\n        FRONT[\"Frontmatter\"]\n        PROMPT[\"System Prompt\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space\"\n        DIR[\"Path (skill_dir)\"]\n        MODEL[\"SkillProperties class\"]\n        VALIDATE_FN[\"validate() function\"]\n        READ_FN[\"read_properties() function\"]\n        PROMPT_FN[\"to_prompt() function\"]\n        PARSER[\"parse_frontmatter() function\"]\n    end\n\n    SKILL -- \"represented by\" --\u003e DIR\n    FRONT -- \"parsed into\" --\u003e MODEL\n    PROMPT -- \"generated by\" --\u003e PROMPT_FN\n    \n    DIR -- \"input to\" --\u003e VALIDATE_FN\n    DIR -- \"input to\" --\u003e READ_FN\n    READ_FN -- \"calls\" --\u003e PARSER\n    PARSER -- \"returns\" --\u003e MODEL\n```\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/parser.py:67-112](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:150-177](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/prompt.py:9-58]()\n\n## Data Models\n\n### SkillProperties\n\nThe `SkillProperties` dataclass represents the structured metadata extracted from a skill's YAML frontmatter [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/models.py:8-19]().\n\n| Attribute | Type | Description |\n|-----------|------|-------------|\n| `name` | `str` | Skill name in kebab-case (required) |\n| `description` | `str` | Purpose and usage instructions (required) |\n| `license` | `Optional[str]` | License information |\n| `compatibility` | `Optional[str]` | Compatibility notes |\n| `allowed_tools` | `Optional[str]` | Tool patterns required by the skill |\n| `metadata` | `dict[str, str]` | Client-specific key-value pairs (defaults to empty dict) |\n\n**Methods:**\n- `to_dict()`: Converts the instance to a dictionary, excluding `None` values and empty `metadata` [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/models.py:28-39]().\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/models.py:7-40]()\n\n## Core Functions\n\n### validate()\nValidates a skill directory against the Agent Skills specification [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:150-177]().\n\n- **Input:** `skill_dir: Path`\n- **Returns:** `list[str]` - A list of error messages. An empty list indicates a valid skill.\n- **Implementation:**\n  1. Checks if the directory and `SKILL.md` exist [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:161-169]().\n  2. Parses YAML frontmatter using `parse_frontmatter` [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:173]().\n  3. Validates field constraints via `validate_metadata`:\n     - `name`: Lowercase, kebab-case, max 64 chars, matches directory name [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:25-67]().\n     - `description`: Non-empty, max 1024 chars [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:70-84]().\n     - `compatibility`: Max 500 chars [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:87-101]().\n     - Schema: No unexpected fields [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:104-115]().\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:10-177]()\n\n### read_properties()\nParses the `SKILL.md` frontmatter into a structured object [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/parser.py:67-112]().\n\n- **Input:** `skill_dir: Path`\n- **Returns:** `SkillProperties`\n- **Raises:** `ParseError` if YAML is invalid; `ValidationError` if required fields (`name`, `description`) are missing or empty [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/parser.py:80-103]().\n- **Implementation:** Locates `SKILL.md` (case-insensitive), extracts the content between `---` delimiters, and loads it using `strictyaml` [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/parser.py:12-64]().\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/parser.py:1-112]()\n\n### to_prompt()\nGenerates the recommended `\u003cavailable_skills\u003e` XML block for agent system prompts [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/prompt.py:9-58]().\n\n- **Input:** `skill_dirs: list[Path]`\n- **Returns:** `str` (XML formatted string)\n- **Implementation:** Iterates through provided directories, calls `read_properties()` to get metadata, and wraps the HTML-escaped name, description, and absolute file path in XML tags [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/prompt.py:37-56]().\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/prompt.py:1-58]()\n\n## Data Flow Diagram\n\nThe following diagram illustrates how data moves from the filesystem through the library's internal modules.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    subgraph \"Filesystem\"\n        MD[\"SKILL.md\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"parser.py\"\n        FIND[\"find_skill_md()\"]\n        PARSE_FM[\"parse_frontmatter()\"]\n        READ_PROP[\"read_properties()\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"validator.py\"\n        VAL[\"validate()\"]\n        VAL_META[\"validate_metadata()\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"prompt.py\"\n        TOPROMPT[\"to_prompt()\"]\n    end\n\n    MD --\u003e FIND\n    FIND --\u003e PARSE_FM\n    PARSE_FM --\u003e READ_PROP\n    \n    READ_PROP -- \"Metadata Dict\" --\u003e VAL_META\n    VAL -- \"Calls\" --\u003e PARSER_CALL[\"parse_frontmatter()\"]\n    VAL -- \"Calls\" --\u003e VAL_META\n    \n    TOPROMPT -- \"Calls\" --\u003e READ_PROP\n    READ_PROP -- \"Returns\" --\u003e SP[\"SkillProperties\"]\n    TOPROMPT -- \"Formats\" --\u003e XML[\"XML String\"]\n```\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/parser.py:1-112](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:150-177](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/prompt.py:9-58]()\n\n## Usage Examples\n\n### Validating and Reading a Skill\n```python\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom skills_ref import validate, read_properties\n\nskill_path = Path(\"skills/my-skill\")\n\n# 1. Validate structure\nerrors = validate(skill_path)\nif errors:\n    print(f\"Invalid skill: {errors}\")\nelse:\n    # 2. Access metadata\n    props = read_properties(skill_path)\n    print(f\"Skill Name: {props.name}\")\n    print(f\"Description: {props.description}\")\n```\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:150-158](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/parser.py:67-82]()\n\n### Generating System Prompts\n```python\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom skills_ref import to_prompt\n\nskills = [Path(\"skills/search\"), Path(\"skills/calculator\")]\nxml_block = to_prompt(skills)\n\n# This block can be injected into a Claude system prompt\nprint(xml_block)\n```\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/prompt.py:9-31]()\n\n## Error Handling\n\nThe API uses custom exception classes for different failure modes defined in `errors.py` [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/errors.py:1-26]():\n\n- `SkillError`: Base class for all library errors [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/errors.py:4-7]().\n- `ParseError`: Raised when `SKILL.md` is missing, the YAML frontmatter is not properly delimited, or the YAML is syntactically incorrect [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/parser.py:39-59]().\n- `ValidationError`: Raised by `read_properties` when the frontmatter is valid YAML but fails basic constraints like missing required fields [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/parser.py:81-103]().\n\nNote that `validate()` does **not** raise `ValidationError`; it returns a list of strings to allow for reporting multiple issues at once [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:150-177]().\n\n**Sources:** [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/errors.py:1-26](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/parser.py:67-112](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:150-177]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"2c:T220f,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Development Guide\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [skills-ref/CLAUDE.md](skills-ref/CLAUDE.md)\n- [skills-ref/tests/test_parser.py](skills-ref/tests/test_parser.py)\n- [skills-ref/tests/test_prompt.py](skills-ref/tests/test_prompt.py)\n- [skills-ref/tests/test_validator.py](skills-ref/tests/test_validator.py)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThis document provides guidance for developers contributing to the `skills-ref` reference library. It covers the development environment setup, code quality tools, testing procedures, and the development workflow. This guide is intended for contributors working on the `skills-ref` Python package itself, not for end users of the library. For information about installing and using `skills-ref`, see [Installation and Setup](#5.1). For details on the Python API, see [Python API](#5.3).\n\n## Development Environment\n\nThe `skills-ref` library requires Python 3.11 or higher as specified in [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:10](). The project uses `hatchling` as its build backend [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:20-21]().\n\n### Development Dependencies\n\nDevelopment dependencies are managed through a dependency group in `pyproject.toml`:\n\n| Dependency | Version | Purpose |\n|------------|---------|---------|\n| `pytest` | \u003e=7.0 | Test framework |\n| `ruff` | \u003e=0.8.0 | Code formatting and linting |\n\nThese are specified in [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:26-30]() under the `[dependency-groups]` section.\n\n### Ignored Artifacts\n\nThe project's `.gitignore` is configured to exclude common Python development artifacts:\n\n- Build artifacts: `dist/`, `build/`, `.eggs/`, `*.egg-info/` [skills-ref/.gitignore:1-4]()\n- Bytecode: `__pycache__/`, `*.py[cod]` [skills-ref/.gitignore:6-8]()\n- Virtual environments: `.venv/`, `venv/` [skills-ref/.gitignore:10-11]()\n- Test artifacts: `.pytest_cache/`, `.coverage`, `htmlcov/` [skills-ref/.gitignore:14-16]()\n- Tool caches: `.ruff_cache/`, `.mypy_cache/` [skills-ref/.gitignore:22-23]()\n\nSources: [skills-ref/.gitignore:1-23](), [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:26-30]()\n\n## Code Quality Tools\n\n### Ruff\n\nThe project uses `ruff` for both code formatting and linting. Ruff is a fast Python linter and formatter written in Rust that combines the functionality of multiple tools (flake8, isort, black, etc.).\n\n#### Formatting\n\nFormat code with:\n\n```bash\nuv run ruff format .\n```\n\nThis command formats all Python files in the current directory and subdirectories according to ruff's default style rules [skills-ref/CLAUDE.md:8]().\n\n#### Linting\n\nRun linting checks and auto-fix issues with:\n\n```bash\nuv run ruff check --fix .\n```\n\nThe `--fix` flag automatically corrects issues that can be safely fixed [skills-ref/CLAUDE.md:9](). Ruff caches results in `.ruff_cache/` to speed up subsequent runs [skills-ref/.gitignore:22]().\n\nSources: [skills-ref/CLAUDE.md:3-10](), [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:29]()\n\n## Testing Framework\n\n### Pytest\n\nThe project uses `pytest` (version 7.0 or higher) as its testing framework [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:28]().\n\nRun the test suite with:\n\n```bash\nuv run pytest\n```\n\nPytest automatically discovers test files (typically named `test_*.py` or `*_test.py`) and executes them [skills-ref/CLAUDE.md:17](). Test results are cached in `.pytest_cache/` [skills-ref/.gitignore:14]() to enable features like `--lf` (last-failed) and `--ff` (failed-first) test selection.\n\n### Test Coverage and Implementation\n\nThe test suite covers the core logic of the reference implementation, focusing on parsing, validation, and prompt generation:\n\n- **Parser Tests**: Validate frontmatter extraction, YAML handling, and `SKILL.md` file discovery. Tests include handling of missing or unclosed frontmatter [skills-ref/tests/test_parser.py:29-42](), invalid YAML syntax [skills-ref/tests/test_parser.py:44-53](), and preference for uppercase `SKILL.md` over lowercase `skill.md` [skills-ref/tests/test_parser.py:128-137]().\n- **Validator Tests**: Ensure skill names, descriptions, and directory structures meet the specification. It validates name length [skills-ref/tests/test_validator.py:54-66](), hyphen usage [skills-ref/tests/test_validator.py:68-92](), and character sets including i18n support for Chinese and Russian [skills-ref/tests/test_validator.py:165-220](). It also enforces the 1024 character limit for descriptions [skills-ref/tests/test_validator.py:221-234]().\n- **Prompt Tests**: Verify that skill metadata is correctly transformed into the XML format used for agent system prompts [skills-ref/tests/test_prompt.py:1-71](). This includes testing for XML special character escaping in descriptions [skills-ref/tests/test_prompt.py:55-71]().\n\n### Test Entity Mapping\n\nThe following diagram maps test functions to the core code entities they exercise.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space\"\n        PARSER[\"skills_ref.parser.read_properties\"]\n        VALIDATOR[\"skills_ref.validator.validate\"]\n T_FAIL[\"test_missing_name\u003cbr/\u003etest_parser.py\"]\n        T_I18N[\"test_i18n_chinese_name\u003cbr/\u003etest_validator.py\"]\n    end\n\n    T_PARSER --\u003e PARSER\n    T_FAIL --\u003e PARSER\n    T_VALID --\u003e VALIDATOR\n    T_I18N --\u003e VALIDATOR\n    T_PROMPT --\u003e PROMPT\n```\n\nSources: [skills-ref/tests/test_parser.py:67-81](), [skills-ref/tests/test_validator.py:6-17](), [skills-ref/tests/test_prompt.py:11-27](), [skills-ref/tests/test_parser.py:104-114](), [skills-ref/tests/test_validator.py:165-177]()\n\n## Development Workflow\n\n### Recommended Development Cycle\n\n1. **Make code changes** to the `src/skills_ref` package.\n2. **Format code** using `uv run ruff format .` [skills-ref/CLAUDE.md:8]().\n3. **Run linting** using `uv run ruff check --fix .` [skills-ref/CLAUDE.md:9]().\n4. **Execute tests** using `uv run pytest` [skills-ref/CLAUDE.md:17]().\n5. **Commit changes** once all checks pass.\n\n### Quality Checks Pipeline\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    CODE[\"Source Code\u003cbr/\u003esrc/skills_ref/\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Quality Checks\"\n        RUFF_FMT[\"ruff format .\u003cbr/\u003eCode RUFF_CACHE[\".ruff_cache/\"]\n        PYTEST_CACHE[\".pytest_cache/\"]\n    end\n    \n    CODE --\u003e RUFF_FMT\n    RUFF_FMT --\u003e RUFF_CHK\n    RUFF_CHK --\u003e PYTEST\n    \n    RUFF_FMT -.-\u003e|\"Uses\"| RUFF_CACHE\n    RUFF_CHK -.-\u003e|\"Uses\"| RUFF_CACHE\n    PYTEST -.-\u003e|\"Uses\"| PYTEST_CACHE\n    \n    PYTEST --\u003e|\"Pass\"| READY[\"Ready to Commit\"]\n    PYTEST --\u003e|\"Fail\"| CODE\n    RUFF_CHK --\u003e|\"Errors\"| CODE\n```\n\nSources: [skills-ref/CLAUDE.md:3-18](), [skills-ref/.gitignore:14-22]()\n\n## Build Configuration\n\n### Package [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:2]() |\n| `version` | `\"0.1.0\"` | Current version [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:3]() |\n| `requires-python` | `\"\u003e=3.11\"` | Minimum Python version This ensures that development dependencies (`pytest`, `ruff`) are available and isolated from other Python environments.\n\nThe pattern `uv run \u003ccommand\u003e` is used throughout the development workflow:\n- `uv run ruff format .` [skills-ref/CLAUDE.md:8]()\n- `uv run ruff check --fix .` [skills-ref/CLAUDE.md:9]()\n- `uv run pytest` [skills-ref/CLAUDE.md:17]()\n\nThis approach eliminates the need to manually activate virtual environments before running development tools.\n\nSources: [skills-ref/CLAUDE.md:6-17]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"2d:T17e2,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Dependencies and Configuration\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [skills-ref/.gitignore](skills-ref/.gitignore)\n- [skills-ref/pyproject.toml](skills-ref/pyproject.toml)\n- [skills-ref/uv.lock](skills-ref/uv.lock)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis page documents the dependencies, build artifacts, virtual environment setup, and development tool configurations for the `skills-ref` reference library. framework for `skills_ref.cli:main` [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:12]() |\n| `strictyaml` | `\u003e=1.7.3` | Schema-based YAML parsing for `SKILL.md` frontmatter [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:13]() |\n\n### Test runner and suite execution [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:28]() |\n| `ruff` | `\u003e=0.8.0` | Unified linter and code formatter [skills-ref/pyproject.toml:29]() |\n\n**Sources:** [Mintlify](https://mintlify.com) platform. The site serves as the primary technical interface for the Agent Skills specification, offering guides for skill authors, integration instructions for agent developers, and an ecosystem showcase.\n\nFor detailed technical documentation of specific site components and styling, see the following child pages:\n- [Site Configuration](#6.1) — Overview of `docs.json` settings, navigation, and theme.\n- [LogoCarousel Component](#6.2) — Architecture of the animated logo slider on the homepage.\n- [ClientShowcase Component](#6.3) — Logic for the searchable client directory.\n- [Partner Logo Assets](#6.4) — Organization and naming of the 27+ partner brand assets.\n- [Documentation Styling](#6.5) — Custom CSS for section numbering and animations.\n\n## System Architecture\n\nThe documentation site is organized into a `docs/` directory containing MDX content, React snippets, and configuration files. It bridges the \"Natural Language Space\" of user guides with the \"Code Entity Space\" of the specification and reference implementation.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Platform Layer (Mintlify)\"\n        MINT[\"mint dev/build\"]\n        CONFIG[\"docs.json\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Content Layer (MDX)\"\n        HOME[\"home.mdx\"]\n        SPEC[\"specification.mdx\"]\n        CLIENTS[\"clients.mdx\"]\n        QUICK[\"quickstart.mdx\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Component Layer (React Snippets)\"\n        CAROUSEL[\"LogoCarousel.jsx\"]\n        SHOWCASE[\"ClientShowcase.jsx\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Asset Layer\"\n        SNIPPETS[\"/snippets/clients.jsx\"]\n    end\n\n    CONFIG --\u003e MINT\n    HOME --\u003e CAROUSEL\n    CLIENTS --\u003e SHOWCASE\n    SHOWCASE --\u003e SNIPPETS\n    MINT --\u003e HOME\n    MINT --\u003e SPEC\n    MINT --\u003e CLIENTS\n    MINT --\u003e QUICK\n```\n**Documentation Site Component Hierarchy**\n\nSources: [docs/CLAUDE.md:7-8](), [docs/clients.mdx:6-9](), [package.json:5]()\n\n## Core Components and Content\n\nThe site is defined by its navigation structure and custom interactive elements that demonstrate ecosystem adoption.\n\n### Navigation and Routing\nThe site structure is defined in `docs.json` under the `navigation` key [docs/docs.json:21-43](). It organizes content into groups for \"For skill creators\" (Quickstart, Best Practices, etc.) and \"For client implementors\" [docs/docs.json:26-41]().\n\n### Interactive Snippets\nThe documentation utilizes React components (stored as `.jsx` snippets) to provide a dynamic experience:\n*   **`LogoCarousel`**: An infinitely scrolling horizontal track of partner logos used on the homepage.\n*   **`ClientShowcase`**: A filterable grid of compatible platforms and tools defined in `ClientShowcase.jsx` [docs/snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx:6-82](). It uses `React.useReducer` to manage \"shuffle\" and \"alpha\" sorting modes [docs/snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx:18-24]().\n*   **`clients` data**: A centralized registry of ecosystem partners imported by the showcase component [docs/clients.mdx:6]().\n\nSources: [docs/snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx:6-24](), [docs/clients.mdx:6-9](), [docs/docs.json:21-43]()\n\n## Information Architecture\n\nThe site maps conceptual documentation to specific MDX files and navigation structures.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Natural Language Space\"\n        TUTORIAL[\"Step-by-step Tutorial\"]\n        EVALS[\"Evaluation Framework\"]\n        ECOSYSTEM[\"Compatible Platforms\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space\"\n        QUICK_MDX[\"docs/skill-creation/quickstart.mdx\"]\n        EVAL_MDX[\"docs/skill-creation/evaluating-skills.mdx\"]\n        CLIENTS_MDX[\"docs/clients.mdx\"]\n        SHOWCASE_JS[\"docs/snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx\"]\n    end\n\n    QUICK_MDX -- files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [docs/clients.mdx](docs/clients.mdx)\n- [docs/docs.json](docs/docs.json)\n- describes the configuration system for the Agent Skills documentation site, specifically the structure and options available in the `docs.json` file. The documentation site is built with Mintlify and deployed automatically when changes are pushed to the main branch.\n\n## Configuration File Location and Structure\n\nThe documentation site is configured through a single JSON file located at [docs/docs.json:1-66](). This file defines all aspects of the site's appearance, navigation, and interactive features. The configuration follows the Mintlify schema, referenced at the top of the file [docs/docs.json:2-2]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/docs.json:1-66]()\n\n## Core Site Settings\n\nThe top-level configuration defines the fundamental properties of the documentation site:\n\n| Field | Value | Purpose |\n|-------|-------|---------|\n| `$schema` | `\"https://mintlify.com/docs.json\"` | Schema validation URL for IDE support |\n| `theme` | `\"mint\"` | Mintlify theme variant [docs/docs.json:3-3]() |\n| `name` | `\"Agent Skills\"` | Site title displayed in browser and navigation [docs/docs.json:4-4]() |\n| `favicon` | `\"/favicon.svg\"` | Path to favicon file [docs/docs.json:10-10]() |\n\nThese settings establish the site's identity and basic visual framework.\n\n**Sources:** [docs/docs.json:2-10]()\n\n## Visual and Promotional Elements\n\n### Color Configuration\nThe color palette is defined in the `colors` object and controls the visual appearance across the site [docs/docs.json:5-9]():\n\n```json\n\"colors\": {\n  \"primary\": \"#7f7f7f\",\n  \"light\": \"#bfbfbf\",\n  \"dark\": \"#404040\"\n}\n```\n\n| Color Key | Hex Value | Usage |\n|-----------|-----------|-------|\n| `primary` | `#7f7f7f` | Primary UI elements, links, active states |\n| `light` | `#bfbfbf` | Light mode accents, hover states |\n| `dark` | `#404040` | Dark mode accents, text emphasis |\n\n### Global Banner\nThe site includes a dismissible announcement banner used to promote community engagement, such as the official Discord server and related GitHub discussions [docs/docs.json:11-14]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/docs.json:5-14]()\n\n## Navigation and Redirection\n\n### Navigation Bar\nThe navbar configuration defines the primary navigation elements displayed at the top of every page. The primary navbar element is configured as a GitHub link to the repository [docs/docs.json:15-20]().\n\n### Page Navigation Structure\nThe site's page hierarchy is defined in the `navigation.pages` array. It supports top-level pages and grouped sections for specific audiences like \"skill creators\" and \"client implementors\" [docs/docs.json:21-43]().\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    DocsJSON[\"docs.json\"]\n    NavPages[\"navigation.pages array\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Top Level\"\n      Home[\"home\"]\n      Spec[\"specification\"]\n      Clients[\"clients\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Groups\"\n      Creators[\"For skill creators\"]\n      Implementors[\"For client implementors\"]\n    end\n    \n    DocsJSON --\u003e NavPages\n    NavPages --\u003e Home\n    NavPages --\u003e Spec\n    NavPages --\u003e Clients\n    NavPages --\u003e Creators\n    NavPages --\u003e Implementors\n    \n    Creators --\u003e Q[\"skill-creation/quickstart\"]\n    Creators --\u003e B[\"skill-creation/best-practices\"]\n    Creators --\u003e O[\"skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions\"]\n    Creators --\u003e E[\"skill-creation/evaluating-skills\"]\n    Creators --\u003e S[\"skill-creation/using-scripts\"]\n    \n    Implementors --\u003e A[\"client-implementation/adding-skills-support\"]\n```\n\n**Diagram: Navigation Hierarchy Mapping**\n\n### Redirects\nThe configuration includes a `redirects` array to handle legacy URLs or aliases. For example, requests to `/integrate-skills` are automatically routed to the technical implementation guide [docs/docs.json:56-65]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/docs.json:15-43](), [docs/docs.json:56-65]()\n\n## Contextual Integration Options\n\nThe `contextual` object defines which external AI tools and platforms are presented to users for interacting with documentation content, such as opening code snippets in specific IDEs or LLM interfaces [docs/docs.json:44-55]().\n\n| Option | Function |\n|--------|----------|\n| `copy` | Copy code snippets or content to clipboard |\n| `view` | View content in different formats |\n| `chatgpt` | Open content in ChatGPT |\n| `claude` | Open content in Claude |\n| `perplexity` | Search content in Perplexity |\n| `mcp` | Model Context Protocol integration |\n| `cursor` | Open in Cursor IDE |\n| `vscode` | Open in VS Code |\n\n**Sources:** [docs/docs.json:44-55]()\n\n## Configuration Flow and Integration\n\nThe following diagram illustrates how the `docs.json` configuration integrates with the Mintlify platform and content files to produce the final site.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Configuration Layer\"\n        DocsJSON[\"docs.json\"]\n        ThemeConfig[\"theme: 'mint'\"]\n        ColorsConfig[\"colors object\"]\n        NavigationConfig[\"navigation object\"]\n        RedirectsConfig[\"redirects object\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Content Layer\"\n        QuickstartMDX[\"skill-creation/quickstart.mdx\"]\n        EvalSkillsMDX[\"skill-creation/evaluating-skills.mdx\"]\n        ClientShowcaseJSX[\"snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Mintlify Platform\"\n        MintEngine[\"Mintlify Rendering Engine\"]\n        RouteGenerator[\"Route Generator\"]\n        AssetPipeline[\"Asset Pipeline\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Output\"\n        LocalServer[\"http://localhost:3000\"]\n        ProductionSite[\"Published Site\"]\n    end\n    \n    DocsJSON --\u003e MintEngine\n    QuickstartMDX --\u003e MintEngine\n    EvalSkillsMDX --\u003e MintEngine\n    ClientShowcaseJSX --\u003e AssetPipeline\n    \n    ThemeConfig --\u003e MintEngine\n    ColorsConfig --\u003e MintEngine\n    NavigationConfig --\u003e RouteGenerator\n    RedirectsConfig --\u003e RouteGenerator\n    \n    MintEngine --\u003e LocalServer\n    RouteGenerator --\u003e ProductionSite\n    AssetPipeline --\u003e ProductionSite\n```\n\n**Diagram: Configuration Processing and Site Generation**\n\nThe configuration file serves as the central control point. Mintlify reads `docs.json`, resolves all referenced `.mdx` content and `.jsx` snippets, applies theme and color settings, and generates the routing table [docs/docs.json:1-66]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/docs.json:1-66](), [docs/skill-creation/quickstart.mdx:1-4](), [docs/snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx:6-82]()\n\n## Configuration Modification Workflow\n\nTo modify the documentation site configuration:\n\n1. **Edit Configuration**: Modify [docs/docs.json:1-66]() with desired changes.\n2. **Local Preview**: Run the development server to see changes in real-time.\n3. **Add New Pages**: Create the `.mdx` file (e.g., [docs/skill-creation/evaluating-skills.mdx:1-5]()) and add its path to the `navigation.pages` array in `docs.json` [docs/docs.json:21-43]().\n4. **Deploy**: Changes are automatically deployed upon pushing to the main branch.\n\n**Sources:** [docs/docs.json:1-66](), [docs/skill-creation/evaluating-skills.mdx:1-5]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"31:T1989,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# LogoCarousel Component\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx](docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx)\n- [docs/style.css](docs/style.css)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThe `LogoCarousel` component is a specialized React component designed for the Agent Skills documentation site to showcase ecosystem adoption. It presents a continuously scrolling display of partner platforms and tools that support the Agent Skills standard. The component implements a single-track infinite scrolling mechanism (updated from previous dual-track versions), utilizes a Fisher-Yates shuffle for fair exposure, and provides theme-aware rendering for light and dark modes.\n\n**Sources:** [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:1-4]()\n\n## Component Architecture\n\nThe `LogoCarousel` is implemented as a functional React component that accepts a `clients` array as a prop. It manages the randomization of these clients and calculates dynamic animation timing based on the cumulative width of the logos.\n\n### High-Level Data Flow\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Data Input\"\n        clients[\"clients Prop (Array of Objects)\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"State Management\"\n        useState[\"useState(shuffled)\"]\n        useEffect[\"useEffect (Mount)\"]\n        FisherYates[\"shuffle() Function\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Layout \u0026 Timing Logic\"\n        doubled[\"doubled (shuffled + shuffled)\"]\n        GAP_PX[\"GAP_PX (48px)\"]\n        PX_PER_SECOND[\"PX_PER_SECOND (40)\"]\n        cycleWidth[\"cycleWidth (reduce function)\"]\n        cycleDuration[\"cycleDuration (cycleWidth / PX_PER_SECOND)\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Rendering\"\n        Track[\"div.logo-carousel-track\"]\n        LogoComp[\"Logo Sub-component\"]\n    end\n\n    clients --\u003e useState\n    useEffect --\u003e FisherYates\n    FisherYates --\u003e useState\n    useState --\u003e doubled\n    useState --\u003e cycleWidth\n    cycleWidth --\u003e cycleDuration\n    doubled --\u003e Track\n    cycleDuration --\u003e Track\n    Track --\u003e LogoComp\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:5-30](), [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:40-45]()\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### Shuffle Algorithm\nTo ensure \"fair exposure\" for all partners, the component shuffles the `clients` array on every component mount using the Fisher-Yates (Knuth) shuffle algorithm. This ensures that no single partner consistently appears at the beginning of the track.\n\n| Step | Operation | Code Reference |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| 1 | Create a shallow copy of the `items` array | [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:11-11]() |\n| 2 | Iterate backwards from the last element | [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:12-12]() |\n| 3 | Pick a random index `j` between 0 and `i` | [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:13-13]() |\n| 4 | Swap elements at `i` and `j` | [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:14-14]() |\n\n**Sources:** [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:10-18]()\n\n### Scrolling Mechanism and Timing\nThe component uses a calculated animation duration to ensure a consistent scrolling speed regardless of the number of logos or their individual widths.\n\n1.  **Infinite Loop**: The shuffled array is doubled (`[...shuffled, ...shuffled]`) [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:21-21](). This allows the CSS animation to reset seamlessly.\n2.  **Width Calculation**: It calculates the `cycleWidth` by summing the scaled width of each logo (base 150px * `client.scale`) plus a constant `GAP_PX` (48px) [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:23-28]().\n3.  **Dynamic Duration**: The animation duration is derived by dividing the total width by a constant speed of 40 pixels per second [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:24-29]().\n4.  **CSS Animation**: The `logo-carousel-track` applies a linear infinite animation named `logo-scroll` [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:40-40]().\n\n### Theme-Aware Rendering\nThe internal `Logo` sub-component handles theme-specific assets. It renders two `\u003cimg\u003e` tags for every client, using Tailwind CSS classes to toggle visibility based on the user's system theme.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Logo Component Logic\"\n        direction TB\n        ClientData[\"Client Object\"]\n        LightImg[\"Light Variant (img)\"]\n        DarkImg[\"Dark Variant (img)\"]\n        \n        ClientData --\u003e LightImg\n        ClientData --\u003e DarkImg\n        \n        LightImg --- ClassL[\"className='block dark:hidden'\"]\n        DarkImg --- ClassD[\"className='hidden dark:block'\"]\n    end\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:31-36]()\n\n## Styling and Animations\n\nThe visual behavior is defined in the global stylesheet, providing the layout for the carousel and the keyframe definitions for the scroll.\n\n| CSS Selector | Purpose | Code Reference |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `.logo-carousel` | Container with `overflow: hidden` to clip the track. | [docs/style.css:87-90]() |\n| `.logo-carousel-track` | Flex container that holds the logos; uses `will-change: transform`. | [docs/style.css:92-98]() |\n| `@keyframes logo-scroll` | Translates the track from `0` to `-50%` (minus half the gap). | [docs/style.css:100-103]() |\n| `prefers-reduced-motion` | Disables animation for users with motion sensitivity. | [docs/style.css:105-107]() |\n\n**Sources:** [docs/style.css:87-107]()\n\n## Data Structures\n\n### Client Object Schema\nThe `clients` array passed to the component expects objects with the following structure:\n\n| Field | Type | Description |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `name` | `string` | The display name/alt text for the partner. |\n| `url` | `string` | The destination link when the logo is clicked. |\n| `lightSrc` | `string` | Path to the logo asset for light mode. |\n| `darkSrc` | `string` | Path to the logo asset for dark mode. |\n| `scale` | `number` | (Optional) A multiplier for the base 150px width. |\n\n**Sources:** [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:25-26](), [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:32-34](), [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:42-42]()\n\n## Component Layout Structure\n\nThe component returns a single track contained within a carousel wrapper.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"LogoCarousel Return\"\n        DivOuter[\"div.logo-carousel\"]\n        DivTrack[\"div.logo-carousel-track\"]\n        MapDoubled[\"doubled.map()\"]\n        DivLogoWrap[\"div (Width calculated)\"]\n        LogoComp[\"Logo Component\"]\n        \n        DivOuter --\u003e DivTrack\n        DivTrack --\u003e MapDoubled\n        MapDoubled --\u003e DivLogoWrap\n        DivLogoWrap --\u003e LogoComp\n    end\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:38-48]()\n\nSources: [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:1-49](),
