source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [docs/clients.mdx](docs/clients.mdx)\n- [docs/docs.json](docs/docs.json)\n- [docs/images/logos/fast-agent/fast-agent-dark.svg](docs/images/logos/fast-agent/fast-agent-dark.svg)\n- [docs/images/logos/fast-agent/fast-agent-light.svg](docs/images/logos/fast-agent/fast-agent-light.svg)\n- [docs/skill-creation/evaluating-skills.mdx](docs/skill-creation/evaluating-skills.mdx)\n- [docs/skill-creation/quickstart.mdx](docs/skill-creation/quickstart.mdx)\n- [docs/skill-creation/using-scripts.mdx](docs/skill-creation/using-scripts.mdx)\n- [docs/snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx](docs/snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx)\n- [docs/snippets/clients.jsx](docs/snippets/clients.jsx)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThe `ClientShowcase` component is a React-based interactive gallery used to display the ecosystem of AI agents and platforms that have implemented support for the Agent Skills standard. It is primarily utilized on the `clients.mdx` page to provide a visual and searchable directory of compatible clients.\n\n## Overview\n\nThe component is designed to handle a growing list of partners while ensuring \"fairness\" in visibility through a default randomized display. It supports two viewing modes: a shuffled (random) order and an alphabetical sort. The component is theme-aware, automatically switching between light and dark mode logo assets based on the user's system or documentation settings.\n\n### Component Architecture\n\nThe component follows a unidirectional data flow where the `clients` data array is passed as a prop and managed via a local state reducer.\n\n| Feature | Implementation Detail |\n| :--- | :--- |\n| **State Management** | `useReducer` for managing display mode and sorted/shuffled data [docs/snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx:18-24]() |\n| **Layout** | 2-column responsive grid (`grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2`) [docs/snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx:55]() |\n| **Randomization** | Fisher-Yates shuffle algorithm [docs/snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx:7-14]() |\n| **Theme Support** | CSS-based toggling (`dark:hidden` and `hidden dark:block`) for logos [docs/snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx:28-29]() |\n\n**Sources:**\n- [docs/snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx:1-83]()\n- [docs/clients.mdx:1-10]()\n\n---\n\n## Data Schema: Client Object\n\nThe component consumes an array of objects defined in `docs/snippets/clients.jsx`. This centralizes the ecosystem data, allowing for easy updates by contributors.\n\n### Schema Definition\n\n| Property | Type | Description |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `name` | `string` | The display name of the client/platform [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:10](). |\n| `description` | `string` | A brief summary of the tool's capabilities [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:11](). |\n| `url` | `string` | Primary website link for the tool [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:12](). |\n| `lightSrc` | `string` | Path to the logo variant for light backgrounds [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:13](). |\n| `darkSrc` | `string` | Path to the logo variant for dark backgrounds [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:14](). |\n| `scale` | `number` | (Optional) Multiplier to adjust logo size (default: 1) [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:32](). |\n| `instructionsUrl` | `string` | (Optional) Link to documentation on using Skills with this tool [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:15](). |\n| `sourceCodeUrl` | `string` | (Optional) Link to the public repository (if open source) [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:24](). |\n\n**Sources:**\n- [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:8-143]()\n- [docs/snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx:58-76]()\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### State Transitions and Reducer\nThe component uses `React.useReducer` to synchronize the UI mode (Shuffle vs. Alpha) with the underlying data array.\n\nTitle: ClientShowcase State Flow\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Natural Language Space\"\n        A[\"User clicks 'Alphabetical'\"]\n        B[\"User clicks 'Shuffle'\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space\"\n        A --\u003e C[\"dispatch('alpha')\"]\n        B --\u003e D[\"dispatch('shuffle')\"]\n        \n        C --\u003e E[\"sort(clients)\"]\n        D --\u003e F[\"shuffle(arr)\"]\n        \n        E --\u003e G[\"useReducer State Update\"]\n        F --\u003e G\n        \n        G --\u003e H[\"Component Re-render\"]\n    end\n```\n**Sources:**\n- [docs/snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx:7-24]()\n- [docs/snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx:51-52]()\n\n### Logo Rendering and Scaling\nThe `Logo` sub-component handles theme switching. It renders two `\u003cimg\u003e` tags; one is hidden in dark mode, and the other is hidden in light mode using Tailwind CSS utility classes [docs/snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx:28-29]().\n\nThe container for the logo uses a fixed height of `80px` and a dynamic width calculated as `150 * (client.scale || 1)` to normalize logos with different aspect ratios [docs/snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx:58]().\n\n**Sources:**\n- [docs/snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx:26-31]()\n- [docs/snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx:58-60]()\n\n---\n\n## Component Integration\n\nThe component is integrated into the documentation site by importing both the raw data and the React component into an MDX file.\n\nTitle: Component Integration Map\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"File System\"\n        DATA[\"docs/snippets/clients.jsx\"]\n        COMP[\"docs/snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx\"]\n        PAGE[\"docs/clients.mdx\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Logic\"\n        DATA -- \"export const clients\" --\u003e PAGE\n        COMP -- \"export const ClientShowcase\" --\u003e PAGE\n        PAGE -- \"render \u003cClientShowcase clients={clients} /\u003e\" --\u003e UI[\"Browser DOM\"]\n    end\n```\n\n### Contribution Workflow\nTo add a new client to the showcase, contributors follow a three-step process as outlined in the project's documentation comments:\n1. Upload logo assets to `/images/logos/[logo-name]/` [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:5]().\n2. Add an entry to the `clients` array in `docs/snippets/clients.jsx` [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:8]().\n3. Ensure both `lightSrc` and `darkSrc` paths are correctly mapped [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:13-14]().\n\n**Sources:**\n- [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:1-8]()\n- [docs/clients.mdx:6-9]()\n- [docs/snippets/ClientShowcase.jsx:1-6]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"33:T2110,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Partner Logo Assets\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [docs/favicon.svg](docs/favicon.svg)\n- [docs/images/logos/google-ai-edge-gallery/google-ai-edge-gallery-dark.svg](docs/images/logos/google-ai-edge-gallery/google-ai-edge-gallery-dark.svg)\n- [docs/images/logos/google-ai-edge-gallery/google-ai-edge-gallery-light.svg](docs/images/logos/google-ai-edge-gallery/google-ai-edge-gallery-light.svg)\n- [docs/images/logos/nanobot/nanobot-logo-dark.png](docs/images/logos/nanobot/nanobot-logo-dark.png)\n- [docs/images/logos/nanobot/nanobot-logo-light.png](docs/images/logos/nanobot/nanobot-logo-light.png)\n- [docs/images/logos/vtcode/vt_code_dark.svg](docs/images/logos/vtcode/vt_code_dark.svg)\n- [docs/images/logos/vtcode/vt_code_light.svg](docs/images/logos/vtcode/vt_code_light.svg)\n- [docs/images/logos/workshop/workshop-logo-dark.svg](docs/images/logos/workshop/workshop-logo-dark.svg)\n- [docs/images/logos/workshop/workshop-logo-light.svg](docs/images/logos/workshop/workshop-logo-light.svg)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThis document describes the organization, naming conventions, and structure of the 27+ partner logo assets used in the Agent Skills documentation site. These logos represent platforms and tools that support the Agent Skills standard and are displayed in the `LogoCarousel` component on the documentation homepage and the `ClientShowcase` component.\n\nFor information about the component architecture that consumes these assets, see [LogoCarousel Component](6.2) and [ClientShowcase Component](6.3).\n\n## Directory Structure\n\nPartner logos are organized in a hierarchical directory structure under `docs/images/logos/` where each product or platform has its own subdirectory containing theme-specific variants.\n\n```text\ndocs/images/logos/\n├── vtcode/\n│   ├── vt_code_dark.svg\n│   └── vt_code_light.svg\n├── nanobot/\n│   ├── nanobot-logo-dark.png\n│   └── nanobot-logo-light.png\n├── workshop/\n│   ├── workshop-logo-dark.svg\n│   └── workshop-logo-light.svg\n├── google-ai-edge-gallery/\n│   ├── google-ai-edge-gallery-dark.svg\n│   └── google-ai-edge-gallery-light.svg\n└── [23+ more product directories]\n```\n\nEach product directory typically contains at least two variants: one optimized for light backgrounds (usually featuring dark text/icons) and one for dark backgrounds (usually featuring white or light-colored text/icons).\n\n**Sources:** [docs/images/logos/vtcode/vt_code_light.svg:1-5](), [docs/images/logos/workshop/workshop-logo-light.svg:1-12](), [docs/images/logos/google-ai-edge-gallery/google-ai-edge-gallery-dark.svg:1-7]()\n\n## Naming Conventions\n\n### Standard Patterns\n\nLogo files follow several naming patterns based on their theme optimization:\n\n| Pattern | Description | Examples |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `*-light.svg` | Designed for light backgrounds (dark content) | `vt_code_light.svg`, `workshop-logo-light.svg` |\n| `*-dark.svg` | Designed for dark backgrounds (light content) | `vt_code_dark.svg`, `workshop-logo-dark.svg` |\n| `*-logo-light.png` | Rasterized light variant | `nanobot-logo-light.png` |\n| `*-logo-dark.png` | Rasterized dark variant | `nanobot-logo-dark.png` |\n\n### File Format Support\n\nThe repository utilizes both vector and raster formats, though SVG is preferred for scalability.\n\n- **SVG**: Most common format, ensuring crisp rendering and theme-aware fill adjustments (e.g., `vt_code_dark.svg` [docs/images/logos/vtcode/vt_code_dark.svg:1]()).\n- **PNG**: Used for specific partners where vector assets were unavailable or complex transparency was required (e.g., `nanobot-logo-dark.png` [docs/images/logos/nanobot/nanobot-logo-dark.png:1]()).\n\n**Sources:** [docs/images/logos/vtcode/vt_code_dark.svg:1](), [docs/images/logos/nanobot/nanobot-logo-dark.png:1](), [docs/images/logos/workshop/workshop-logo-dark.svg:1]()\n\n## Implementation Examples\n\n### Theme-Specific Color Values\n\nThe light and dark variants adjust the `fill` attributes of SVG paths or text elements to ensure contrast against the documentation background.\n\n#### Light Background Variant (Dark Text)\nIn `vt_code_light.svg`, the text element uses a black fill (`#000`) to remain visible on the light theme.\n**Source:** [docs/images/logos/vtcode/vt_code_light.svg:2]()\n\n#### Dark Background Variant (Light Text)\nIn `vt_code_dark.svg`, the same text element is switched to a white fill (`#fff`) for the dark theme.\n**Source:** [docs/images/logos/vtcode/vt_code_dark.svg:2]()\n\n### Path-Based Fill Management\nFor more complex logos like Workshop, the SVG paths themselves contain explicit fill values that differ between files.\n- `workshop-logo-light.svg` uses `fill=\"#0A0D10\"` for text paths [docs/images/logos/workshop/workshop-logo-light.svg:2]().\n- `workshop-logo-dark.svg` uses `fill=\"white\"` for the same paths [docs/images/logos/workshop/workshop-logo-dark.svg:2]().\n\n## Asset Integration Flow\n\nThe following diagram illustrates how raw SVG and PNG assets are associated with code entities and rendered in the documentation UI.\n\n### Logo Rendering Pipeline\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Filesystem Space\"\n        SVG_L[\"vt_code_light.svg\"]\n        SVG_D[\"vt_code_dark.svg\"]\n        PNG_L[\"nanobot-logo-light.png\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space\"\n        DATA[\"logos array in LogoCarousel.jsx\"]\n        ENTRY_VT[\"{ name: 'VT Code', lightSrc: '...', darkSrc: '...' }\"]\n        COMP[\"LogoCarousel Component\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Natural Language / UI Space\"\n        UI_L[\"Light Mode UI (black text)\"]\n        UI_D[\"Dark Mode UI (white text)\"]\n    end\n\n    SVG_L --\u003e|Path reference| ENTRY_VT\n    SVG_D --\u003e|Path reference| ENTRY_VT\n    PNG_L --\u003e|Path reference| DATA\n    DATA --\u003e|Contains| ENTRY_VT\n    ENTRY_VT --\u003e|Mapped by| COMP\n    \n    COMP --\u003e|className='block dark:hidden'| UI_L\n    COMP --\u003e|className='hidden dark:block'| UI_D\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docs/images/logos/vtcode/vt_code_light.svg:1-5](), [docs/images/logos/vtcode/vt_code_dark.svg:1-5](), [docs/images/logos/nanobot/nanobot-logo-light.png:1]()\n\n### Directory Mapping to Ecosystem Categories\n\nThe logos are categorized within the documentation components (like `ClientShowcase`) into logical ecosystem groups based on their directory origin.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Code Categories\"\n        IDE[\"category: 'IDE'\"]\n        PLAT[\"category: 'AI Platform'\"]\n        GALLERY[\"category: 'Gallery'\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Logo Assets\"\n        VT_LOGO[\"vtcode/vt_code_light.svg\"]\n        WORK_LOGO[\"workshop/workshop-logo-light.svg\"]\n        GOOGLE_LOGO[\"google-ai-edge-gallery/google-ai-edge-gallery-light.svg\"]\n    end\n\n    IDE --- VT_LOGO\n    PLAT --- WORK_LOGO\n    GALLERY --- GOOGLE_LOGO\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docs/images/logos/vtcode/vt_code_light.svg:1](), [docs/images/logos/workshop/workshop-logo-light.svg:1](), [docs/images/logos/google-ai-edge-gallery/google-ai-edge-gallery-light.svg:1]()\n\n## Asset Specifications\n\n### SVG Optimization\nMost partner logos include standard XML headers and viewbox definitions to ensure proper scaling within the documentation layout.\n\n- **VT Code**: Uses a standard `400x120` viewbox with `SF Pro Display` font styling [docs/images/logos/vtcode/vt_code_dark.svg:1-2]().\n- **Workshop**: Uses a `690x142` viewbox with complex path definitions for brand-specific typography [docs/images/logos/workshop/workshop-logo-dark.svg:1-12]().\n- **Google AI Edge Gallery**: Uses a square `256x256` viewbox and multi-colored paths (`#FCC934`, `#EE675C`, `#5BB974`, `#669DF6`) that remain consistent across both light and dark themes [docs/images/logos/google-ai-edge-gallery/google-ai-edge-gallery-dark.svg:1-5]().\n\n### Visual Consistency\nTo maintain a uniform look in the `LogoCarousel`:\n1. **Aspect Ratio**: Most logos are horizontal lockups, though some (like Google AI Edge Gallery) are square icons.\n2. **Padding**: Spacing is primarily handled by the `logo-carousel-track` CSS class, but individual assets like `favicon.svg` define tight `32x32` viewboxes [docs/favicon.svg:1]().\n3. **Scaling**: Components typically apply a standard height or width while maintaining aspect ratio via `object-contain`.\n\n**Sources:** [docs/images/logos/vtcode/vt_code_dark.svg:1](), [docs/images/logos/workshop/workshop-logo-dark.svg:1](), [docs/images/logos/google-ai-edge-gallery/google-ai-edge-gallery-dark.svg:1](), [docs/favicon.svg:1]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"34:T1aaf,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Documentation Styling\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx](docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx)\n- [docs/style.css](docs/style.css)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis page documents the custom CSS styling implemented for the Agent Skills documentation site. These styles extend the base Mintlify theme to provide specialized functionality such as hierarchical section numbering, animated logo carousels, and theme-aware visual enhancements.\n\n## Overview\n\nThe documentation's visual identity and custom behavior are primarily defined in `docs/style.css`. This stylesheet manages:\n\n*   **Auto-numbered Headings**: A dynamic numbering system for `h2` through `h6` elements.\n*   **Logo Carousel Animations**: Smooth, infinite-scroll transitions for partner logos.\n*   **Typography Hierarchy**: Fine-tuned font weights and monospace configurations.\n*   **Theme Integration**: Support for light and dark modes using standard CSS variables and utility classes.\n\n**Sources**: [docs/style.css:1-108]()\n\n## Base Content Styling\n\nThe core content area uses the `#content-area` selector to apply foundational typography rules.\n\n### Typography and Hierarchy\nThe site utilizes a specific monospace stack, prioritizing JetBrains Mono for code blocks and technical identifiers.\n\n| Element | Style Rule | Purpose |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `--font-mono` | `var(--font-jetbrains-mono), ui-monospace, ...` | Sets the global monospace font stack [docs/style.css:2-2](). |\n| `h5` | `font-weight: 500` | Medium weight for sub-section headings [docs/style.css:4-6](). |\n| `h6` | `font-weight: 400` | Normal weight for the deepest heading level [docs/style.css:8-10](). |\n\n**Sources**: [docs/style.css:1-11]()\n\n## Automatic Section Numbering\n\nThe documentation employs a hierarchical numbering system (e.g., 1.1, 1.1.1) implemented entirely in CSS. This system is \"opt-in\" and only activates when a specific trigger element is present.\n\n### Activation Mechanism\nThe numbering logic is wrapped in a `:has()` pseudo-class selector. It remains inactive unless the page includes an element with the ID `enable-section-numbers`.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Natural Language Space\"\n        NL_Page[\"Documentation Page\"]\n        NL_Toggle[\"Section Numbering Toggle\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space\"\n        CSS_Trigger[\"#enable-section-numbers\"]\n        CSS_Logic[\"body:has(#enable-section-numbers)\"]\n        CSS_Counter[\"counter-reset: h2-counter ...\"]\n        CSS_Pseudo[\"h2[id]::before { content: counter(h2-counter) }\"]\n    end\n\n    NL_Page --\u003e CSS_Trigger\n    NL_Toggle --\u003e CSS_Trigger\n    CSS_Trigger --\u003e CSS_Logic\n    CSS_Logic --\u003e CSS_Counter\n    CSS_Counter --\u003e CSS_Pseudo\n```\n**Sources**: [docs/style.css:17-21]()\n\n### Counter Implementation\nThe system uses five CSS counters: `h2-counter`, `h3-counter`, `h4-counter`, `h5-counter`, and `h6-counter`.\n\n1.  **Initialization**: Counters are reset at the `#content-area` and `#table-of-contents` levels [docs/style.css:22-25]().\n2.  **Hierarchical Reset**: When a higher-level heading is encountered, all subordinate counters are reset (e.g., an `h3` resets counters for `h4` through `h6`) [docs/style.css:32-35]().\n3.  **Incrementing**: Each heading level increments its specific counter [docs/style.css:50-50]().\n4.  **Content Injection**: The `::before` pseudo-element injects the combined counter string (e.g., `counter(h2-counter) \".\" counter(h3-counter)`) before the heading text [docs/style.css:57-57]().\n\nThis logic is mirrored for the Table of Contents (`#table-of-contents`), ensuring that the navigation menu matches the on-page heading numbers [docs/style.css:49-81]().\n\n**Sources**: [docs/style.css:15-82]()\n\n## Logo Carousel Styling\n\nThe `LogoCarousel` component relies on specific CSS classes to handle layout and animation. The component logic in `docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx` calculates dynamic durations while `docs/style.css` defines the structural behavior.\n\n### Layout and Track\nThe carousel consists of a container that clips overflow and an internal \"track\" that holds the logos in a flex row.\n\n*   **.logo-carousel**: Set to `overflow: hidden` and `width: 100%` [docs/style.css:87-90]().\n*   **.logo-carousel-track**: Uses `display: flex` with a `3rem` (48px) gap. The `width` is set to `max-content` to allow the track to extend beyond the viewport for animation [docs/style.css:92-98]().\n\n### Animation: `logo-scroll`\nThe infinite scroll effect is achieved by translating the track horizontally.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Natural Language Space\"\n        UI_Carousel[\"Partner Logo Strip\"]\n        UI_Motion[\"Smooth Scrolling\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space\"\n        CSS_Track[\".logo-carousel-track\"]\n        JS_Duration[\"cycleDuration = cycleWidth / PX_PER_SECOND\"]\n        CSS_Anim[\"@keyframes logo-scroll\"]\n        CSS_Trans[\"transform: translateX(calc(-50% - 1.5rem))\"]\n    end\n\n    UI_Carousel --\u003e CSS_Track\n    UI_Motion --\u003e CSS_Anim\n    CSS_Anim --\u003e CSS_Trans\n    CSS_Track -- \"uses\" --\u003e CSS_Anim\n    JS_Duration -- \"sets style.animation\" --\u003e CSS_Track\n```\n\nThe animation moves the track from `0` to `calc(-50% - 1.5rem)` [docs/style.css:100-103](). This works in tandem with the `LogoCarousel` component, which duplicates the logo set (`doubled` array) to create a seamless loop [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:21-46]().\n\n### Accessibility and Performance\n*   **Performance**: The track uses `will-change: transform` to hint to the browser that the element should be promoted to its own compositor layer [docs/style.css:97-97]().\n*   **Reduced Motion**: The animation is disabled if the user has requested reduced motion in their system settings [docs/style.css:105-107]().\n\n**Sources**: [docs/style.css:86-108](), [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:21-49]()\n\n## Theme-Specific Visual Customizations\n\nThe documentation site implements theme-aware rendering for graphical assets.\n\n### Image Variants\nThe `LogoCarousel` component handles light and dark mode logo switching using Tailwind utility classes:\n*   **Light Mode**: Images with `dark:hidden` are shown [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:33-33]().\n*   **Dark Mode**: Images with `hidden dark:block` are shown [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:34-34]().\n\n### Visual Constraints\nTo maintain a clean look, logos in the carousel have specific styling applied via React:\n*   `noZoom` attribute prevents Mintlify's default image zoom behavior [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:33-34]().\n*   `!my-0` (important margin-y: 0) overrides default Markdown image spacing [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:33-34]().\n*   `object-contain` ensures logos maintain aspect ratio within their allocated width [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:33-34]().\n\n**Sources**: [docs/snippets/LogoCarousel.jsx:31-49]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"35:T1827,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Ecosystem and Adoption\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md)\n- [docs/home.mdx](docs/home.mdx)\n- [docs/images/logos/fast-agent/fast-agent-dark.svg](docs/images/logos/fast-agent/fast-agent-dark.svg)\n- [docs/images/logos/fast-agent/fast-agent-light.svg](docs/images/logos/fast-agent/fast-agent-light.svg)\n- [docs/snippets/clients.jsx](docs/snippets/clients.jsx)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThis document provides an overview of the Agent Skills ecosystem, showcasing adoption across 27+ platforms including IDEs, CLIs, and AI agents. It details the value proposition of the open standard and the technical implementation of the ecosystem showcase. For a full catalog of integrated tools, see [Compatible Platforms and Tools](#7.1). For stakeholder benefits, see [Value Propositions](#7.2). For governance and contribution models, see [Open Standard and Governance](#7.3).\n\n## Ecosystem Overview\n\nAgent Skills has achieved broad adoption across the AI development landscape. The ecosystem is categorized into primary segments: IDEs (e.g., VS Code, Cursor), CLI tools (e.g., Claude Code, Gemini CLI), AI Platforms (e.g., OpenHands, Letta), and Development Tools (e.g., GitHub Copilot). \n\nThe ecosystem is technically represented in the codebase through a shared data structure in `docs/snippets/clients.jsx` [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:8-143](), which powers both the animated `LogoCarousel` [docs/home.mdx:49]() on the homepage and the interactive `ClientShowcase` component.\n\n**Sources:** [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:8-143](), [docs/home.mdx:7-49]()\n\n## Ecosystem Architecture: From Spec to Implementation\n\nThe following diagram illustrates how the `SKILL.md` specification bridges the gap between the \"Natural Language Space\" (instructions) and the \"Code Entity Space\" (tools and scripts) across various platforms.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Natural Language Space\"\n        SKILL_MD[\"SKILL.md Specification\"]\n        PROMPT[\"System Prompt Injection\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space (Agent Skills Core)\"\n        REF_LIB[\"skills-ref Library\"]\n        VALIDATOR[\"Validator Class\"]\n        PARSER[\"Parser Module\"]\n        PROPERTIES[\"SkillProperties Model\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Compatible Platforms (Adoption)\"\n        CLAUDE_CODE[\"Claude Code CLI\"]\n        VS_CODE[\"VS Code Extension\"]\n        OPEN_HANDS[\"OpenHands Platform\"]\n        GEMINI_CLI[\"Gemini CLI\"]\n    end\n\n    SKILL_MD --\u003e PARSER\n    PARSER --\u003e PROPERTIES\n    PROPERTIES --\u003e VALIDATOR\n    VALIDATOR --\u003e REF_LIB\n    \n    REF_LIB --\u003e PROMPT\n    PROMPT --\u003e CLAUDE_CODE\n    PROMPT --\u003e VS_CODE\n    PROMPT --\u003e OPEN_HANDS\n    PROMPT --\u003e GEMINI_CLI\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:18-132](), [docs/home.mdx:10-25]()\n\n## Client Showcase Implementation\n\nThe ecosystem is visualized using the `ClientShowcase` React component. This component manages a list of compatible partners and provides a UI for discovery.\n\n### Technical Data Structure\nEach client in the ecosystem is defined by a standard schema in the `clients` array [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:8]():\n- `name`: Display name of the tool [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:10]().\n- `description`: High-level value proposition [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:11]().\n- `lightSrc` / `darkSrc`: Theme-aware logo paths [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:13-14]().\n- `instructionsUrl`: Link to platform-specific setup [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:15]().\n- `sourceCodeUrl`: Link to open-source implementations (optional) [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:24]().\n\n### Component Logic\nThe `ClientShowcase` component (and the `LogoCarousel` [docs/home.mdx:49]()) manages display states, often including a \"fairness\" shuffle to ensure all partners get equal visibility.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant UI as ClientShowcase.jsx\n    participant Reducer as useReducer\n    participant Data as clients.jsx\n    participant Algo as Fisher-Yates Shuffle\n\n    UI-\u003e\u003eData: Import clients array\n    UI-\u003e\u003eReducer: Dispatch \"shuffle\" action\n    Reducer-\u003e\u003eAlgo: Execute shuffle(clients)\n    Algo--\u003e\u003eReducer: Return randomized array\n    Reducer--\u003e\u003eUI: Update state.clients\n    UI-\u003e\u003eUI: Map clients to 2-column grid\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:8-143](), [docs/home.mdx:7-49]()\n\n## Platform Distribution\n\nThe ecosystem currently tracks over 27 integrations. Key highlights include:\n\n| Category | Key Code Entities / Platforms | Primary Integration Path |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| **IDEs** | VS Code, Cursor, Junie, Trae | Filesystem-based discovery |\n| **CLIs** | Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Goose | Terminal environment / Bash |\n| **Platforms** | OpenHands, Letta, Mux | Tool-based / API integration |\n| **Enterprise** | GitHub Copilot, Databricks | System prompt injection |\n\n**Sources:** [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:8-143](), [docs/home.mdx:10-25]()\n\n## Open Standard and Contribution\n\nAgent Skills is maintained by Anthropic as an open standard [docs/home.mdx:53](). The ecosystem grows through community contributions, specifically via:\n\n1.  **Ecosystem Listings**: Partners can submit PRs to `docs/snippets/clients.jsx` to be featured in the showcase [CONTRIBUTING.md:26-36]().\n2.  **AI Disclosure Policy**: All contributions assisted by AI (e.g., Claude Code, ChatGPT) must be disclosed in the PR [CONTRIBUTING.md:78-86]().\n3.  **Governance**: Proposals for spec changes are handled via GitHub Discussions to ensure community alignment before implementation [CONTRIBUTING.md:15-21]().\n\n**Sources:** [CONTRIBUTING.md:1-115](), [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:1-7](), [docs/home.mdx:51-56]()\n\n## Value Proposition Summary\n\nThe Agent Skills ecosystem provides a \"build once, run anywhere\" model for agentic capabilities [docs/home.mdx:31]().\n- **For Authors**: Portability across 27+ platforms.\n- **For Developers**: Access to a library of pre-validated expert knowledge [docs/home.mdx:29]().\n- **For Platforms**: A standardized way to allow users to extend agent capabilities without custom tool development [docs/home.mdx:12]().\n\nFor more details, see [Value Propositions](#7.2).\n\n**Sources:** [docs/home.mdx:10-32](), [CONTRIBUTING.md:44-49]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"36:T240a,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Compatible Platforms and This data structure bridges the gap between the natural language product names and the technical assets (logos) required for rendering.\n\n### Code Entity to Asset Mapping\n\nThe following diagram maps specific code entities within the `clients` array to their corresponding filesystem assets.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Code_Entities_clients_jsx\"\n        \"E1\"[\"clients[0].name: 'Junie'\"]\n        \"E2\"[\"clients[1].name: 'Gemini CLI'\"]\n        \"E3\"[\"clients[12].name: 'VS Code'\"]\n        \"E4\"[\"clients[13].name: 'Claude Code'\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Filesystem_Logo_Assets\"\n        \"A1\"[\"/images/logos/junie/junie-logo-on-dark.svg\"]\n        \"A2\"[\"/images/logos/gemini-cli/gemini-cli-logo_dark.svg\"]\n        \"A3\"[\"/images/logos/vscode/vscode-alt.svg\"]\n        \"A4\"[\"/images/logos/claude-code/Claude-Code-logo-Ivory.svg\"]\n    end\n    \n    \"E1\" -- \"darkSrc\" --\u003e \"A1\"\n    \"E2\" -- \"darkSrc\" --\u003e \"A2\"\n    \"E3\" -- \"darkSrc\" --\u003e \"A3\"\n    \"E4\" -- \"darkSrc\" --\u003e \"A4\"\n```\n\n**Figure 2: Mapping Code Entities to Logo Assets**\n\nSources: [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:8-132](), [docs/images/logos/junie/junie-logo-on-dark.svg:1-11](), [docs/images/logos/vscode/vscode-alt.svg:1-23]()\n\n## Implementation: Theme-Aware Rendering\n\nCompatible tools often require different visual treatments for light and dark modes. The repository stores multiple variants of each logo to support this.\n\n### Asset Structure and SVG Implementation\nLogo assets are stored in subdirectories named after the client. For example, `fast-agent` includes both `fast-agent-light.svg` [docs/images/logos/fast-agent/fast-agent-light.svg:1-11]() and `fast-agent-dark.svg` [docs/images/logos/fast-agent/fast-agent-dark.svg:1-11]().\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    \"ClientObject\"[\"Client Object Entity\"]\n    \"LightMode\"[\"Light Mode Variant\"]\n    \"DarkMode\"[\"Dark Mode Variant\"]\n    \n    \"ClientObject\" --\u003e \"LightMode\"\n    \"ClientObject\" --\u003e \"DarkMode\"\n    \n    subgraph \"Fast_Agent_Assets\"\n [docs/images/logos/fast-agent/fast-agent-dark.svg:8](), [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:30-31]()\n\n## Client Entry Schema\n\nEach entry in the `clients` array [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:8]() must conform to a specific schema to be rendered correctly.\n\n| Field | Type | Description |\n|-------|------|-------------|\n| `name` | `string` | The display name of the platform (e.g., \"Junie\" [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:10]()). |\n| `description` | `string` | A technical summary of the tool's purpose [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:11](). |\n| `url` | `string` | The primary product or project website [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:12](). |\n| `lightSrc` | `string` | Path to the light-theme SVG asset [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:13](). |\n| `darkSrc` | `string` | Path to the dark-theme SVG asset |\n\n### Scaling and Normalization\nSome logos, like **Autohand** [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:32]() or **Mux** [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:60](), use a `scale: 0.8` property to ensure visual consistency within the showcase grid. This metadata allows the UI to normalize different SVG viewbox dimensions.\n\nSources: [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:8-143]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"37:T22de,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Value Propositions\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md)\n- [LICENSE](LICENSE)\n- [README.md](README.md)\n- [docs/home.mdx](docs/home.mdx)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document details the value propositions of the Agent Skills format for three primary stakeholder groups: skill authors, agent developers, and teams/enterprises. It explains how specific features of the `SKILL.md` format and ecosystem architecture enable these benefits.\n\nFor information about the specific platforms and tools that support Agent Skills, see [7.1. Compatible Platforms and Tools](). For details about the governance and evolution of the standard, see [7.3. Open Standard and Governance]().\n\n---\n\n## Stakeholder Value Model\n\nThe Agent Skills format creates value through a three-sided ecosystem where each stakeholder group receives distinct benefits while contributing to the overall network effect.\n\n### Ecosystem Value Flow\nTitle: Stakeholder Interaction and Format Benefits\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph SkillAuthors[\"Skill Authors\"]\n        BUILD[\"Build once, deploy everywhere\"]\n        REUSE[\"Reusable skill packages\"]\n        VERSION[\"Version control compatible\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph AgentDevs[\"Agent Developers\"]\n        EXTENSIBLE[\"User-extensible agents\"]\n        NO_TRAIN[\"No pre-training required\"]\n        CONTEXT[\"Efficient context usage\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Enterprises[\"Teams \u0026 Enterprises\"]\n        PORTABLE[\"Portable knowledge packages\"]\n        AUDIT[\"Auditable workflows\"]\n        STANDARD[\"Standard format interoperability\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Format[\"SKILL.md Format\"]\n        YAML[\"YAML frontmatter metadata\"]\n        MARKDOWN[\"Markdown instructions\"]\n        RESOURCES[\"Optional resource directories\"]\n        DISCOVERY[\"Progressive disclosure model\"]\n    end\n    \n    BUILD --\u003e Format\n    EXTENSIBLE --\u003e Format\n    PORTABLE --\u003e Format\n    \n    Format --\u003e REUSE\n    Format --\u003e NO_TRAIN\n    Format --\u003e AUDIT\n    \n    DISCOVERY --\u003e CONTEXT\n    YAML --\u003e VERSION\n    STANDARD --\u003e REUSE\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docs/home.mdx:10-32](), [README.md:3-6]()\n\n---\n\n## Skill Authors: Build Once, Deploy Everywhere\n\nSkill authors gain the ability to create capabilities that work across multiple agent platforms without modification [README.md:5-6]().\n\n### Multi-Platform Compatibility\n\nThe standardized `SKILL.md` format ensures that a single skill package can be consumed by any agent system that supports the Agent Skills specification [docs/home.mdx:12-14]().\n\n| Value Proposition | Technical Enabler | Agent System Requirement |\n|-------------------|-------------------|--------------------------|\n| Write once, run anywhere | Standardized `SKILL.md` format | Parse YAML frontmatter |\n| Broad distribution | Large number of AI tools | Implement discovery phase |\n| Consistent behavior | Specification-defined semantics | Load instructions on activation |\n| Version control friendly | Plain text format | Handle filesystem paths |\n\n### Deployment Architecture\nTitle: Skill Distribution to Compatible Clients\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    AUTHOR[\"Skill Author\u003cbr/\u003eCreates SKILL.md\"]\n    \n    subgraph SkillPackage[\"Skill Package Folder\"]\n        SKILL_MD[\"SKILL.md\u003cbr/\u003emetadata + instructions\"]\n        SCRIPTS[\"scripts/\u003cbr/\u003eOptional executable code\"]\n        REFS[\"references/\u003cbr/\u003eOptional documentation\"]\n        ASSETS[\"assets/\u003cbr/\u003eOptional templates\"]\n    end\n    \n    AUTHOR --\u003e SKILL_MD\n    SKILL_MD --\u003e SCRIPTS\n    SKILL_MD --\u003e REFS\n    SKILL_MD --\u003e ASSETS\n    \n    subgraph Agents[\"Compatible Agent Systems\"]\n        IDE[\"IDE Extensions\"]\n        CLI[\"CLI Tools\"]\n        PLATFORMS[\"AI Platforms\"]\n        DEVTOOLS[\"Development Tools\"]\n    end\n    \n    SkillPackage --\u003e IDE\n    SkillPackage --\u003e CLI\n    SkillPackage --\u003e PLATFORMS\n    SkillPackage --\u003e DEVTOOLS\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docs/home.mdx:14-23](), [docs/home.mdx:45-49](), [README.md:5-6]()\n\n---\n\n## Agent Developers: User-Provided Capabilities\n\nAgent developers benefit from an extensibility mechanism that allows end users to provide new capabilities without requiring model updates or pre-training [docs/home.mdx:25-32]().\n\n### Zero Pre-Training Extension Model\n\nThe Agent Skills format enables capability extension without fine-tuning or complex retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) infrastructure [docs/home.mdx:27-32]().\n\n### Code Entity Integration Flow\nTitle: Code Entity Mapping for Agent Integration\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph Filesystem[\"Filesystem Space\"]\n        DIR[\"skill-directory/\"]\n        S_MD[\"SKILL.md\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph CodeSpace[\"Code Entity Space (skills-ref)\"]\n        PARSER[\"parser.py\"]\n        PROPS[\"SkillProperties class\"]\n        VALIDATOR[\"validator.py\"]\n        PROMPT_GEN[\"prompt.py\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph AgentLogic[\"Agent Runtime\"]\n        DISCOVERY[\"Discovery Phase\"]\n        ACTIVATION[\"Activation Phase\"]\n    end\n\n    S_MD --\u003e|\"Parsed by\"| PARSER\n    PARSER --\u003e|\"Populates\"| PROPS\n    PROPS --\u003e|\"Verified by\"| VALIDATOR\n    PROPS --\u003e|\"Input to\"| PROMPT_GEN\n    PROMPT_GEN --\u003e|\"Generates XML for\"| DISCOVERY\n    S_MD --\u003e|\"Read full text for\"| ACTIVATION\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docs/home.mdx:33-43](), [CONTRIBUTING.md:38-40]()\n\n### Progressive Disclosure for Context Efficiency\n\nThe three-phase progressive disclosure model minimizes context window consumption while maintaining full capability access [docs/home.mdx:33-43]().\n\n| Phase | Content Loaded | Context Cost | Trigger |\n|-------|----------------|--------------|---------|\n| **Discovery** | `name`, `description` | Minimal (Metadata only) | Agent startup / scanning |\n| **Activation** | Full `SKILL.md` instructions | Moderate (Instructions) | Task matches description |\n| **Execution** | `scripts/`, `references/`, `assets/` | Variable (On-demand) | Agent follows instructions |\n\n**Sources:** [docs/home.mdx:33-43]()\n\n---\n\n## Teams and Enterprises: Portable Knowledge Packages\n\nTeams and enterprises gain the ability to capture and share organizational knowledge in a standardized, portable format [docs/home.mdx:27-32]().\n\n### Knowledge Portability and Governance\n\nSkills packages encapsulate domain expertise that can be shared across teams and version-controlled like code [docs/home.mdx:27-32]().\n\n| Use Case | Implementation | Business Value |\n|----------|----------------|-------|\n| Legal review processes | `SKILL.md` with checklist instructions | Consistent compliance |\n| Data analysis pipelines | `SKILL.md` + `scripts/` Python tools | Reproducible analysis |\n| Architecture patterns | `SKILL.md` + `references/` docs | Standardized designs |\n| Security audits | `SKILL.md` with specialized context | Audit trail |\n\n### Knowledge Capture Workflow\nTitle: From Domain Expertise to Portable Skill\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph ExpertSpace[\"Natural Language Space\"]\n        EXP[\"Domain Expert Expertise\"]\n        PROC[\"Standard Operating Procedure\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph CodeEntitySpace[\"Code Entity Space\"]\n        SKILL_FILE[\"SKILL.md File\"]\n        FRONTMATTER[\"YAML Frontmatter\"]\n        BODY[\"Markdown Body\"]\n        SCRIPTS_DIR[\"scripts/ directory\"]\n    end\n\n    EXP --\u003e|\"Formalized into\"| PROC\n    PROC --\u003e|\"Written to\"| BODY\n    PROC --\u003e|\"Metadata defined in\"| FRONTMATTER\n    PROC --\u003e|\"Automation scripts in\"| SCRIPTS_DIR\n    \n    subgraph Distribution[\"Enterprise Distribution\"]\n        GIT[\"Git Repository\"]\n        CI[\"CI/CD Validation\"]\n    end\n\n    SKILL_FILE --\u003e GIT\n    SCRIPTS_DIR --\u003e GIT\n    GIT --\u003e CI\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docs/home.mdx:14-23](), [docs/home.mdx:27-32](), [CONTRIBUTING.md:11-13]()\n\n---\n\n## Technical Enablers\n\n### Format Features to Value Mapping\n\n| Format Feature | Technical Detail | Value Enabled |\n|----------------|------------------|---------------|\n| **YAML Frontmatter** | `name`, `description` [docs/home.mdx:14-14]() | Efficient discovery and indexing |\n| **Markdown Body** | Instructions and workflows [docs/home.mdx:14-14]() | Human-readable and AI-executable logic |\n| **Directory Structure** | `scripts/`, `references/`, `assets/` [docs/home.mdx:17-22]() | Full resource bundling for complex tasks |\n| **Open Standard** | Apache 2.0 / CC-BY-4.0 [README.md:21-22]() | No vendor lock-in; community-driven |\n\n### Open Standard Benefits\n\nThe open standard nature of Agent Skills, maintained by Anthropic and open to community contributions, provides transparency and trust for enterprise adoption [docs/home.mdx:51-55](), [README.md:18-19]().\n\n*   **Interoperability**: Build once, use across any compatible client [README.md:5-6]().\n*   **Transparency**: Clear disclosure policies for AI-assisted contributions ensure quality and accountability [CONTRIBUTING.md:78-88]().\n*   **Community Driven**: Feedback and proposals are handled via GitHub Discussions and Issues [CONTRIBUTING.md:11-20]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/home.mdx:51-55](), [README.md:18-22](), [CONTRIBUTING.md:1-25]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"38:T1c8f,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Open Standard and Governance\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md)\n- [LICENSE](LICENSE)\n- [README.md](README.md)\n- [docs/home.mdx](docs/home.mdx)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis page explains the open standard nature of Agent Skills, the governance model maintained by Anthropic, and the structured process for community contributions. It details how the ecosystem evolves through public discourse, technical contributions, and a rigorous disclosure policy for AI-assisted work.\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nAgent Skills is an open format designed to provide a universal language for extending AI agent capabilities [docs/home.mdx:12-14](). By maintaining the specification as an open standard, the project ensures that skills remain portable across diverse agent platforms, preventing vendor lock-in and fostering a shared library of machine expertise [docs/home.mdx:25-32]().\n\n## Governance Model and Anthropic's Role\n\nAgent Skills is maintained by **Anthropic** and is open to contributions from the community [README.md:16-18](). While Anthropic acts as the primary steward and maintainer of the core specification and documentation, the project's evolution is driven by real-world implementation challenges faced by the broader ecosystem [CONTRIBUTING.md:19-20]().\n\n### Maintenance Responsibilities\n- **Specification Authority**: Defining the `SKILL.md` format and progressive disclosure model [docs/home.mdx:33-43]().\n- **Reference Implementation**: Maintaining the `skills-ref` Python library [README.md:14-14]().\n- **Ecosystem Curation**: Reviewing and approving ecosystem listings and partner logos [CONTRIBUTING.md:26-36]().\n- **Community Moderation**: Managing GitHub Issues and Discussions to guide the project's roadmap [CONTRIBUTING.md:15-24]().\n\n## Community Contribution Model\n\nThe project utilizes GitHub as the central hub for governance and collaboration. Contributions are categorized into specific channels to ensure high-signal feedback.\n\n### Feedback Channels\n\n| Channel | Purpose | Source |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| **GitHub Issues** | Concrete bug reports in the spec, documentation, or reference library. | [CONTRIBUTING.md:11-14]() |\n| **GitHub Discussions** | Feature requests, spec design questions, proposals, and open-ended feedback. | [CONTRIBUTING.md:15-17]() |\n| **Pull Requests** | Direct contributions to documentation, ecosystem listings, or bug fixes. | [CONTRIBUTING.md:30-34]() |\n\n### Contribution Workflow\n\nThe following diagram illustrates how a community proposal moves from an idea to an integrated part of the standard, bridging the gap between natural language requests and the codebase.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Natural Language Space (Community)\"\n        IDEA[\"Feature Idea / Problem\"]\n        DISC[\"GitHub Discussions\"]\n        DISC_URL[\"github.com/agentskills/agentskills/discussions\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code \u0026 Spec Space (Implementation)\"\n        PR[\"Pull Request\"]\n        DOCS[\"/docs/ directory\"]\n        REF[\"/skills-ref/ directory\"]\n        CLIENTS[\"docs/snippets/clients.jsx\"]\n    end\n\n    IDEA --\u003e DISC\n    DISC --- DISC_URL\n    DISC -- \"If validated\" --\u003e PR\n    PR --\u003e DOCS\n    PR --\u003e REF\n    PR --\u003e CLIENTS\n    \n    subgraph \"Governance Gate (Anthropic)\"\n        REVIEW[\"Maintainer Review\"]\n        MERGE[\"Merge to main branch\"]\n    end\n\n    DOCS --\u003e REVIEW\n    REF --\u003e REVIEW\n    CLIENTS --\u003e REVIEW\n    REVIEW --\u003e MERGE\n```\n**Diagram: Contribution Data Flow**\nSources: [CONTRIBUTING.md:9-17](), [CONTRIBUTING.md:69-74](), [README.md:18-19]()\n\n## Ecosystem Listings and Partner Integration\n\nThe Agent Skills ecosystem is showcased on the documentation site through partner listings. To be included, products must have a publicly available implementation that can discover and execute skills today [CONTRIBUTING.md:26-29]().\n\n### Submission Requirements\nContributors wishing to add their platform to the ecosystem must provide:\n1.  **Logo Assets**: SVG (preferred) or PNG variants for light and dark modes, following the naming convention in `docs/images/logos/` [CONTRIBUTING.md:30-32]().\n2.  **Client Entry**: A structured object added to the array in `docs/snippets/clients.jsx` [CONTRIBUTING.md:33-34]().\n3.  **Verification**: A link to product documentation showing the Skills implementation [CONTRIBUTING.md:34-34]().\n\n### Client Data Structure\nThe `clients` array drives UI components like the `LogoCarousel` [docs/home.mdx:49-49](). The following diagram maps the relationship between the configuration file and the rendered documentation.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space\"\n        C_JSX[\"docs/snippets/clients.jsx\"]\n        C_ARRAY[\"const clients = [...]\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"UI Component Space\"\n        CAROUSEL[\"LogoCarousel.jsx\"]\n        SHOWCASE[\"ClientShowcase.jsx\"]\n    end\n\n    C_JSX --\u003e C_ARRAY\n    C_ARRAY -- \"Imported by\" --\u003e CAROUSEL\n    C_ARRAY -- \"Imported by\" --\u003e SHOWCASE\n    \n    subgraph \"Attributes\"\n        ATTR[\"name, url, lightSrc, darkSrc\"]\n    end\n    \n    C_ARRAY --- ATTR\n```\n**Diagram: Ecosystem Data Mapping**\nSources: [docs/home.mdx:7-8](), [CONTRIBUTING.md:30-34]()\n\n## AI Disclosure Policy\n\nGiven the nature of the project, many contributors use AI agents to assist in development. The project enforces a mandatory disclosure policy for such contributions [CONTRIBUTING.md:78-80]().\n\n### Disclosure Rules\n- **Mandatory Disclosure**: Any AI assistance (e.g., Claude Code, ChatGPT) must be disclosed in the PR or issue, including the extent of use [CONTRIBUTING.md:81-85]().\n- **Exceptions**: Trivial spacing or typo fixes do not require disclosure [CONTRIBUTING.md:87-88]().\n- **Human Understanding**: Contributors must personally understand the changes and provide a clear rationale for how they fit within Agent Skills goals [CONTRIBUTING.md:99-106]().\n- **Enforcement**: Maintainers reserve the right to close submissions that do not follow the disclosure policy [CONTRIBUTING.md:108-110]().\n\nSources: [CONTRIBUTING.md:78-111]()\n\n## Licensing and Roadmap\n\n### Licensing Terms\nAgent Skills uses a dual-licensing approach to maximize both adoption and contribution:\n- **Code \u0026 Specification**: Licensed under **Apache License 2.0** [README.md:20-22](). This includes the `skills-ref` library and the format definitions.\n- **Documentation**: Licensed under **CC-BY-4.0** [README.md:22-22]().\n\n### Ecosystem Evolution Roadmap\nThe project is currently in an early, focused stage. The roadmap prioritizes:\n1.  **Core Stability**: Iterating on the core specification before accepting major architectural changes [CONTRIBUTING.md:47-49]().\n2.  **Reference Tooling**: Determining the long-term direction for the `skills-ref` library [CONTRIBUTING.md:38-40]().\n3.  **Wider Adoption**: Expanding the list of compatible platforms through community-submitted listings [CONTRIBUTING.md:26-29]().\n\n**Currently Not Accepting**:\n- **Skill Submissions**: The repository does not currently maintain a directory of community skills [CONTRIBUTING.md:44-46]().\n- **Reference Library PRs**: Code contributions to `skills-ref/` are not being accepted during the current iteration phase [CONTRIBUTING.md:38-40]().\n\nSources: [README.md:20-23](), [CONTRIBUTING.md:38-50](), [LICENSE:1-132]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"39:T1844,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Repository Configuration\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [.claude/hooks/session-start.sh](.claude/hooks/session-start.sh)\n- [.claude/settings.json](.claude/settings.json)\n- [.gitignore](.gitignore)\n- [docs/CLAUDE.md](docs/CLAUDE.md)\n- [package.json](package.json)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThis document details the configuration files that govern the Agent Skills repository's development environment, version control behavior, and AI-assisted coding integration. It covers how the repository manages file exclusions to maintain a clean version history and how it leverages Claude Code hooks to automate environment checks and provide project-specific guidance.\n\nFor detailed technical breakdowns, see the following child pages:\n- **Version Control Configuration**: [Version Control Configuration](#8.1) — Detailed explanation of `.gitignore` patterns at the root and subproject levels.\n- **Claude Code Integration**: [Claude Code Integration](#8.2) — Documentation of `.claude/` settings, session-start automation hooks, and the `CLAUDE.md` guidance file.\n\n---\n\n## Overview\n\nThe repository configuration is designed to support a polyglot environment involving Python development (for `skills-ref`), Node.js/React development (for the Mintlify documentation site), and AI-assisted workflows. The configuration is split between standard version control rules and tool-specific settings that streamline the contributor experience.\n\n**Sources:** [.gitignore:1-10](), [.claude/settings.json:1-15](), [docs/CLAUDE.md:1-24]()\n\n---\n\n## Configuration Architecture\n\nThe following diagram illustrates how different configuration files interact with the developer's environment and the repository's functional areas.\n\n### System Configuration Map\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Local Environment\"\n        SHELL[\"Shell/Terminal\"]\n        IDE[\"IDE (VSCode/PyCharm)\"]\n        CLAUDE[\"Claude Code CLI\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Repository Config\"\n        GITIGNORE[\".gitignore\"]\n        CLAUDE_SET[\".claude/settings.json\"]\n        HOOKS[\".claude/hooks/session-start.sh\"]\n        CLAUDE_MD[\"docs/CLAUDE.md\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Functional Areas\"\n        DOCS[\"docs/ (Mintlify)\"]\n        SKILLS[\"skills-ref/ (Python)\"]\n    end\n\n    IDE --\u003e GITIGNORE\n    GITIGNORE --\u003e|\"Excludes .vscode, .idea\"| DOCS\n    GITIGNORE --\u003e|\"Excludes node_modules\"| DOCS\n    \n    CLAUDE --\u003e CLAUDE_SET\n    CLAUDE --\u003e CLAUDE_MD\n    CLAUDE_SET --\u003e|\"Triggers\"| HOOKS\n    HOOKS --\u003e|\"Checks for 'mint' CLI\"| DOCS\n    \n    GITIGNORE --\u003e|\"Global Exclusions\"| SKILLS\n```\n**Diagram: Repository Configuration Interplay**\n\nThis diagram bridges the \"Natural Language Space\" of developer tools to the \"Code Entity Space\" by showing how specific files like `.claude/settings.json` and `.gitignore` mediate access to project areas.\n\n**Sources:** [.gitignore:1-10](), [.claude/settings.json:1-14](), [.claude/hooks/session-start.sh:1-9](), [docs/CLAUDE.md:1-5]()\n\n---\n\n## Version Control Strategy\n\nThe repository utilizes a standard `.gitignore` file to prevent environment-specific and temporary files from being committed. Key exclusions include:\n\n*   **Operating System Metadata**: Files like `.DS_Store` [ .gitignore:2 ]().\n*   **IDE Settings**: Directories for IntelliJ/PyCharm (`.idea/`) and VS Code (`.vscode/`) [ .gitignore:5-6 ]().\n*   **Web Dependencies**: The `node_modules/` directory used by the Mintlify documentation engine [ .gitignore:9 ]().\n\nFor a full analysis of these exclusions and the layered ignore strategy used in the `skills-ref` subproject, see [Version Control Configuration](#8.1).\n\n**Sources:** [.gitignore:1-10]()\n\n---\n\n## AI Development Integration\n\nThe repository includes a `.claude/` directory and a `CLAUDE.md` file to optimize the experience for developers using the Claude Code CLI. This configuration ensures that the AI agent is aware of the project's specific requirements upon starting a session.\n\n### Claude Code Workflow\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Claude Code Session\"\n        START[\"Session Start\"]\n        READ_CONFIG[\"Read .claude/settings.json\"]\n        READ_GUIDE[\"Read docs/CLAUDE.md\"]\n        EXEC_HOOK[\"Exec session-start.sh\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Validation \u0026 Guidance\"\n        CHECK_MINT[\"command -v mint\"]\n        CMD_DEV[\"npm run dev\"]\n    end\n\n    START --\u003e READ_CONFIG\n    START --\u003e READ_GUIDE\n    READ_CONFIG --\u003e EXEC_HOOK\n    EXEC_HOOK --\u003e CHECK_MINT\n    READ_GUIDE --\u003e CMD_DEV\n```\n**Diagram: Claude Code Initialization Logic**\n\nThe integration uses a `SessionStart` hook [ .claude/settings.json:3 ]() to execute a bash script [ .claude/hooks/session-start.sh:1 ]() that validates the presence of the Mintlify CLI (`mint`), which is essential for documentation development [ .claude/hooks/session-start.sh:6-8 ](). Additionally, `docs/CLAUDE.md` provides Claude with the specific command `npm run dev` to start the local development server [ docs/CLAUDE.md:11-14 ]().\n\nFor details on configuring these hooks and the underlying JSON schema, see [Claude Code Integration](#8.2).\n\n**Sources:** [.claude/settings.json:1-14](), [.claude/hooks/session-start.sh:1-9](), [docs/CLAUDE.md:1-14]()\n\n---\n\n## Development Environment Settings\n\nThe repository configuration assumes a modern development stack:\n\n| Component | Tooling | Configured In |\n|-----------|---------|---------------|\n| **Documentation** | Mintlify | `docs/CLAUDE.md`, `package.json` |\n| **Version Control** | Git | `.gitignore` |\n| **AI Assistance** | Claude Code | `.claude/settings.json`, `docs/CLAUDE.md` |\n| **Package Management** | npm / pip | `package.json`, root and subproject configs |\n\nThe `session-start.sh` hook specifically targets the documentation workflow, providing an asynchronous timeout of 15,000ms to ensure the environment check does not block the developer's start-up unnecessarily [ .claude/hooks/session-start.sh:3 ](). Documentation development is further facilitated by a root-level `package.json` script that triggers the Mintlify development server [ package.json:4-6 ]().\n\n**Sources:** [.claude/hooks/session-start.sh:3-8](), [.gitignore:9](), [package.json:4-6](), [docs/CLAUDE.md:9-14]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"3a:T1842,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Version Control Configuration\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [.gitignore](.gitignore)\n- [skills-ref/.gitignore](skills-ref/.gitignore)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis page documents the version control exclusion strategy for the Agent Skills repository. The project utilizes a hierarchical `.gitignore` structure to manage exclusions at both the global repository level and within the `skills-ref` Python package subdirectory. This ensures that environment-specific files, build artifacts, and sensitive local configurations are not committed to the repository.\n\n## Overview of Exclusions\n\nThe repository manages exclusions through two primary files:\n1.  **Root `.gitignore`**: Handles general OS-level files, IDE configurations, and documentation-related dependencies. [[.gitignore:1-10]]()\n2.  **`skills-ref/.gitignore`**: Handles Python-specific artifacts, virtual environments, and testing/linting caches for the reference implementation. [[skills-ref/.gitignore:1-23]]()\n\n### Exclusion Flow Diagram\n\nThe following diagram illustrates how different types of files are filtered out of the version control system based on their origin and purpose.\n\n**Git Exclusion Data Flow**\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Local_Environment\"\n        OS_Files[\"OS Files (.DS_Store)\"]\n        IDE_Files[\".vscode/, .idea/\"]\n        Node_Deps[\"node_modules/\"]\n        Py_Cache[\"__pycache__/, *.py[cod]\"]\n        Build_Art[\"dist/, build/\"]\n        Venvs[\".venv/, venv/\"]\n        Test_Cache[\".pytest_cache/, .coverage\"]\n        Lint_Cache[\".ruff_cache/, .mypy_cache/\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Git_Filters\"\n        Root_Ignore[\"Root .gitignore\"]\n        Sub_Ignore[\"skills-ref/.gitignore\"]\n    end\n\n    OS_Files --\u003e Root_Ignore\n    IDE_Files --\u003e Root_Ignore\n    Node_Deps --\u003e Root_Ignore\n\n    Py_Cache --\u003e Sub_Ignore\n    Build_Art --\u003e Sub_Ignore\n    Venvs --\u003e Sub_Ignore\n    Test_Cache --\u003e Sub_Ignore\n    Lint_Cache --\u003e Sub_Ignore\n\n    Root_Ignore -- \"Blocks\" --\u003e Git_Index[\"Git Index (Tracked Files)\"]\n    Sub_Ignore -- \"Blocks\" --\u003e Git_Index\n```\n**Sources:** [/.gitignore:1-10](), [/skills-ref/.gitignore:1-23]()\n\n---\n\n## Root Configuration\n\nThe root `.gitignore` focuses on general development environment hygiene and dependencies related to the documentation site (which uses Node.js for Mintlify components).\n\n| Category | Patterns | Reason |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| **macOS** | `.DS_Store` | Prevents system-specific metadata files from cluttering the repo [[.gitignore:1-2]](). |\n| **IDEs** | `.idea/`, `.vscode/` | Excludes project-specific settings for JetBrains and VS Code to allow developers to use their own configurations [[.gitignore:4-6]](). |\n| **Dependencies** | `node_modules/` | Excludes external libraries required for the documentation site components [[.gitignore:8-9]](). |\n\n**Sources:** [/.gitignore:1-10]()\n\n---\n\n## Python Reference Library Configuration\n\nThe `skills-ref/` directory contains a Python-based reference implementation. Its local `.gitignore` is specifically tailored to the Python ecosystem, ensuring that transient execution data and build outputs do not enter the source tree.\n\n### Python Runtime and Build Artifacts\nTo maintain a clean distribution state, the following are excluded:\n*   **Bytecode**: `__pycache__/` and `*.py[cod]` are ignored to prevent compiled Python files from being tracked [[skills-ref/.gitignore:1-3]]().\n*   **Distribution**: `dist/`, `build/`, `.eggs/`, and `*.egg-info/` are ignored. These are generated during the packaging process [[skills-ref/.gitignore:4-7]]().\n\n### Environment and Tooling Caches\nThe configuration ensures that local development environments remain isolated:\n*   **Virtual Environments**: Both `.venv/` and `venv/` are excluded, enforcing that users create their own isolated environments [[skills-ref/.gitignore:9-11]]().\n*   **Testing**: `pytest` artifacts such as `.pytest_cache/` and coverage reports (`.coverage`, `htmlcov/`) are ignored [[skills-ref/.gitignore:13-16]]().\n*   **Static Analysis**: Caches for `mypy` (`.mypy_cache/`) and `ruff` (`.ruff_cache/`) are excluded to keep the repository focused on source code rather than tool performance data [[skills-ref/.gitignore:18-22]]().\n\n### Technical Entity Mapping\n\nThe following diagram maps the exclusion categories to the specific tools and processes used in the `skills-ref` development workflow.\n\n**Tooling to Exclusion Mapping**\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Python_Toolchain\"\n        Interpreter[\"Python Interpreter\"]\n        Pytest[\"pytest\"]\n        Ruff[\"ruff\"]\n        Mypy[\"mypy\"]\n        BuildTool[\"build/setuptools\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Excluded_Entities\"\n        PyCache[\"__pycache__\"]\n        TestCache[\".pytest_cache\"]\n        RuffCache[\".ruff_cache\"]\n        MypyCache[\".mypy_cache\"]\n        Dist[\"dist/\"]\n    end\n\n    Interpreter --\u003e|\"Generates\"| PyCache\n    Pytest --\u003e|\"Generates\"| TestCache\n    Ruff --\u003e|\"Generates\"| RuffCache\n    Mypy --\u003e|\"Generates\"| MypyCache\n    BuildTool --\u003e|\"Generates\"| Dist\n\n    PyCache -.-\u003e|\"Ignored by\"| Git[\".gitignore\"]\n    TestCache -.-\u003e|\"Ignored by\"| Git\n    RuffCache -.-\u003e|\"Ignored by\"| Git\n    MypyCache -.-\u003e|\"Ignored by\"| Git\n    Dist -.-\u003e|\"Ignored by\"| Git\n```\n**Sources:** [/skills-ref/.gitignore:1-23]()\n\n---\n\n## Summary Table of Exclusions\n\n| File/Directory | Location | Context |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `.DS_Store` | Root | macOS Metadata [[.gitignore:2]]() |\n| `.idea/`, `.vscode/` | Root | IDE Configuration [[.gitignore:5-6]]() |\n| `node_modules/` | Root | Mintlify/React Dependencies [[.gitignore:9]]() |\n| `__pycache__/` | `skills-ref/` | Python Bytecode [[skills-ref/.gitignore:2]]() |\n| `.venv/`, `venv/` | `skills-ref/` | Python Virtual Environments [[skills-ref/.gitignore:10-11]]() |\n| `dist/`, `build/` | `skills-ref/` | Python Package Distribution [[skills-ref/.gitignore:5-6]]() |\n| `.pytest_cache/` | `skills-ref/` | Test Runner Cache [[skills-ref/.gitignore:14]]() |\n| `.ruff_cache/` | `skills-ref/` | Linting Cache [[skills-ref/.gitignore:22]]() |\n| `.mypy_cache/` | `skills-ref/` | Type Checking Cache [[skills-ref/.gitignore:19]]() |\n\n**Sources:** [/.gitignore:1-10](), [/skills-ref/.gitignore:1-23]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"3b:T1602,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Claude Code Integration\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [.claude/hooks/session-start.sh](.claude/hooks/session-start.sh)\n- [.claude/settings.json](.claude/settings.json)\n- [docs/CLAUDE.md](docs/CLAUDE.md)\n- [package.json](package.json)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis page documents the configuration of Claude Code within the `agentskills` repository. The integration is primarily managed through the `.claude/` directory and `docs/CLAUDE.md`, which provide environment-specific settings, automation hooks, and guidance for the Claude Code agent to work effectively with the repository's documentation and skill formats.\n\n## Configuration Overview\n\nClaude Code is configured to execute specific logic upon the initiation of a new session and is provided with specific project context via a markdown guide. This ensures that the environment is validated and that the agent has immediate context regarding the project's dependencies, specifically the Mintlify documentation engine.\n\n### Data Flow: Session Initialization\n\nWhen a Claude Code session starts, the tool parses the local `.claude/settings.json` file. This file acts as the entry point for session-level automation, directing the agent to execute shell scripts that perform environment checks. Simultaneously, Claude Code reads `docs/CLAUDE.md` to understand the project's purpose and available commands.\n\n**Claude Code Hook Execution Flow**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Claude Code Environment\"\n        A[\"Session Start Event\"] --\u003e B[\"Read .claude/settings.json\"]\n        B --\u003e C[\"Trigger SessionStart Hook\"]\n        A --\u003e H[\"Read docs/CLAUDE.md\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Local Filesystem\"\n        C --\u003e D[\".claude/hooks/session-start.sh\"]\n        D --\u003e E[\"Check Mintlify CLI Presence\"]\n        H --\u003e I[\"Quick Start Commands\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Output / Feedback\"\n        E --\u003e F[\"Stdout: Async Configuration\"]\n        E --\u003e G[\"Stderr/Warning: Missing Dependency\"]\n        I --\u003e J[\"npm run dev\"]\n    end\n```\n\n**Sources:** [.claude/settings.json:1-15](), [.claude/hooks/session-start.sh:1-9](), [docs/CLAUDE.md:1-24]()\n\n---\n\n## Settings Definition\n\nThe primary configuration file is located at `.claude/settings.json`. It defines the behavior of the agent when interacting with the repository.\n\n| Key | Type | Description |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `hooks` | Object | Root container for all event-driven automation [.claude/settings.json:2]() |\n| `SessionStart` | Array | A list of actions to perform immediately upon starting a session [.claude/settings.json:3]() |\n| `type` | String | Defines the execution method (e.g., `command`) [.claude/settings.json:7]() |\n| `command` | String | The path to the executable script [.claude/settings.json:8]() |\n\n**Sources:** [.claude/settings.json:1-15]()\n\n---\n\n## Session Start Hook\n\nThe script `.claude/hooks/session-start.sh` is a Bash script responsible for preparing the developer's environment. It performs two primary functions: configuring the agent's response behavior and verifying documentation tooling.\n\n### Async Configuration\nThe script outputs a JSON object to standard output: `{\"async\":true,\"asyncTimeout\":15000}` [.claude/hooks/session-start.sh:3](). This informs Claude Code that the hook should be treated as an asynchronous operation with a 15-second timeout, preventing session initialization from hanging indefinitely if a check takes too long.\n\n### Tooling Validation\nSince the `agentskills` repository heavily utilizes Mintlify for its documentation site, the hook checks for the existence of the `mint` command [.claude/hooks/session-start.sh:6]().\n\n*   **Logic**: It uses `command -v mint` to check the system path [.claude/hooks/session-start.sh:6]().\n*   **Action**: If the command is not found, it prints a warning message to the console providing the installation command: `npm i -g mint` [.claude/hooks/session-start.sh:7-8]().\n\n**Hook Implementation Detail**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Execution: session-start.sh\"\n        Start[\"Start Hook\"] --\u003e Config[\"Echo Async JSON to Stdout\"]\n        Config --\u003e CheckMint[\"command -v mint\"]\n        CheckMint -- \"Not Found\" --\u003e Warn[\"Print Install Instructions\"]\n        CheckMint -- \"Found\" --\u003e End[\"Exit 0\"]\n        Warn --\u003e End\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code Entities\"\n        Config -.-\u003e JSON[\"JSON Config\"]\n        CheckMint -.-\u003e CLI[\"Mintlify CLI\"]\n    end\n```\n\n**Sources:** [.claude/hooks/session-start.sh:1-9]()\n\n---\n\n## Agent Guidance (CLAUDE.md)\n\nThe `docs/CLAUDE.md` file serves as a specialized instruction manual for Claude Code when it is active in the repository.\n\n### Project Context\nIt identifies the repository as an open format for teaching AI agents specialized workflows through `SKILL.md` files [docs/CLAUDE.md:3](). It specifically notes that the documentation site in `docs/` is built with Mintlify [docs/CLAUDE.md:7]().\n\n### Development Commands\nThe file provides the standard command for local development:\n*   **Local Dev Server**: `npm run dev` [docs/CLAUDE.md:13](), which executes `cd docs \u0026\u0026 npx mint dev` as defined in `package.json` [package.json:5]().\n*   **Preview URL**: `http://localhost:3000` [docs/CLAUDE.md:16]().\n\n### Documentation Maintenance\nClaude is instructed on how to modify the documentation:\n*   **Navigation**: Updates must be made in `docs/docs.json` under the `navigation.pages` array [docs/CLAUDE.md:20]().\n*   **New Pages**: Create a `.mdx` file in `/docs` and add the filename (without extension) to the navigation config [docs/CLAUDE.md:21]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/CLAUDE.md:1-24](), [package.json:1-7]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"3c:T1bd3,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md)\n- [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx](docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx)\n- [docs/home.mdx](docs/home.mdx)\n- [docs/images/logos/fast-agent/fast-agent-light.svg](docs/images/logos/fast-agent/fast-agent-light.svg)\n- [docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx](docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx)\n- [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx](docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx)\n- [docs/snippets/clients.jsx](docs/snippets/clients.jsx)\n- [docs/specification.mdx](docs/specification.mdx)\n- [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/models.py](skills-ref/src/skills_ref/models.py)\n- [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py](skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis page defines codebase-specific terms, jargon, and abbreviations used throughout the Agent Skills repository. It serves as a technical reference for onboarding engineers to understand the implementation details and data flow of the Agent Skills ecosystem.\n\n## Core Concepts\n\n### Skill\nA **Skill** is a structured directory containing procedural knowledge, executable scripts, and static resources designed to extend an AI agent's capabilities [docs/specification.mdx:6-17](). At its technical minimum, a skill must contain a `SKILL.md` file [docs/specification.mdx:8-12]().\n\n### SKILL.md\nThe primary entry point and manifest for a skill. It uses a hybrid format: **YAML frontmatter** for machine-readable metadata and **Markdown** for human-and-agent-readable instructions [docs/specification.mdx:19-21]().\n*   **Implementation**: The `skills-ref` library parses this file to extract structured properties into a `SkillProperties` model [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/models.py:8-26]().\n\n### Progressive Disclosure\nA three-phase context management strategy designed to minimize token usage while maximizing agent capability [docs/specification.mdx:214-222]().\n1.  **Discovery**: Loading only `name` and `description` metadata (~100 tokens) [docs/specification.mdx:218]().\n2.  **Activation**: Loading the full `SKILL.md` body (recommended \u003c 5000 tokens) [docs/specification.mdx:219]().\n3.  **Execution**: On-demand loading of auxiliary files in `scripts/`, `references/`, or `assets/` [docs/specification.mdx:220]().\n\n### Logic Flow: Natural Language to Code Entity\nThe following diagram illustrates how a user's natural language request triggers the transition into the \"Code Entity Space\" where specific skill files are accessed via the `SKILL.md` manifest.\n\n**Diagram: Skill Activation Pipeline**\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Natural Language Space\"\n        UserPrompt[\"User: 'Roll a d20'\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Agent Logic (Client Implementation)\"\n        Scanner[\"Directory Scanner\"]\n        Matcher[\"Semantic Matcher (LLM)\"]\n        Activator[\"Skill Activator\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space (Filesystem)\"\n        SkillDir[\"Skill Directory: .agents/skills/roll-dice/\"]\n        SkillMD[\"SKILL.md (Metadata + Body)\"]\n        ScriptsDir[\"scripts/ (Executable)\"]\n    end\n\n    UserPrompt --\u003e Matcher\n    Scanner --\u003e|Reads name/description| Matcher\n    Matcher --\u003e|Matches 'roll-dice'| Activator\n    Activator --\u003e|Loads full body| SkillMD\n    SkillMD --\u003e|References| ScriptsDir\n```\nSources: [docs/home.mdx:33-43](), [docs/specification.mdx:6-17](), [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:18-31]()\n\n---\n\n## Technical Terms \u0026 Components\n\n### skills-ref\nThe reference Python library used for validating skill structures and parsing metadata [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:1-22]().\n*   **Key Functions**:\n    *   `validate()`: The core validation function that checks `SKILL.md` for schema compliance and directory name matches [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:150-177]().\n    *   `validate_metadata()`: Validates parsed YAML fields like `name`, `description`, and `compatibility` [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:118-147]().\n\n### SkillProperties\nA Python dataclass that represents the structured metadata extracted from a skill's frontmatter [skills_ref/src/skills_ref/models.py:8-26](). It includes fields for `name`, `description`, `license`, `compatibility`, `allowed_tools`, and `metadata` [skills_ref/src/skills_ref/models.py:21-26]().\n\n### Trigger Rate\nA metric used during description optimization to measure how reliably an agent activates a skill for relevant prompts. It is calculated as the fraction of runs where the skill was invoked [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx:85-89]().\n\n### Context Parsimony\nA best practice principle of omitting information that an LLM already knows (like general programming syntax or definitions of common file formats) to focus strictly on domain-specific expertise [docs/skill-creation/best-practices.mdx:50-53]().\n\n---\n\n## System Architecture\n\nThe following diagram maps the documentation site's React components to the data structures and assets they consume within the repository.\n\n**Diagram: Documentation Site Data Mapping**\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Data Entities (Code Space)\"\n        ClientsArray[\"clients.jsx: export const clients = [...]\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"UI Components (Natural Language/Visual Space)\"\n        LogoCarousel[\"LogoCarousel.jsx (Animated Slider)\"]\n        ClientShowcase[\"ClientShowcase.jsx (Filterable List)\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Asset Entities\"\n        Logos[\"/docs/images/logos/ (SVG/PNG)\"]\n    end\n\n    ClientsArray --\u003e|Mapped to| LogoCarousel\n    ClientsArray --\u003e|Mapped to| ClientShowcase\n    Logos --\u003e|Rendered by| LogoCarousel\n    Logos --\u003e|Rendered by| ClientShowcase\n```\nSources: [docs/snippets/clients.jsx:8-142](), [docs/home.mdx:7-8](), [CONTRIBUTING.md:32-33]()\n\n---\n\n## Glossary Table\n\n| Term | Definition | Code Pointer |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| **Frontmatter** | The YAML block at the top of a `SKILL.md` file containing metadata. | [docs/specification.mdx:23-32]() |\n| **Allowed-Tools** | Experimental field listing pre-approved tools a skill may use. | [docs/specification.mdx:163-174]() |\n| **Compatibility** | Optional field indicating environment requirements like system packages or network access. | [docs/specification.mdx:123-141]() |\n| **NFKC** | Unicode normalization form used when validating skill and directory names. | [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:37-61]() |\n| **Near-miss** | A test query that shares keywords with a skill but should not trigger it. | [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx:53-55]() |\n| **Progressive Disclosure** | The three-tier loading strategy: Catalog -\u003e Instructions -\u003e Resources. | [docs/client-implementation/adding-skills-support.mdx:18-31]() |\n\nSources: [docs/specification.mdx:23-32](), [docs/specification.mdx:163-174](), [skills-ref/src/skills_ref/validator.py:37-61](), [docs/skill-creation/optimizing-descriptions.mdx:53-55](),
